Business justification: License agreements must comply with regulatory obligations beyond broadcast license and filing — foreign ownership restrictions, must-carry obligations, local content requirements. Linking to regulato']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: License agreements define commercial terms that map to revenue stream categories (licensing fees, royalties, minimum guarantees). Finance requires agreement-level revenue stream classification for con']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upfront payment made upon execution of the agreement, typically recoupable against future royalties or license fees.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the license agreement. Controls whether rights are available for scheduling and distribution.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the license agreement based on the nature of the rights transaction. Determines workflow, royalty calculation rules, and windowing strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|co-production|syndication|first-run syndication|off-network syndication|international syndication|music sync|output deal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry unless terminated by either party. True = auto-renews, False = expires at term end.']
  blackout_restrictions varchar [note: 'Geographic or temporal restrictions on content availability (e.g., no broadcast in certain markets during specific events, sports blackout rules). Free-text description.']
  clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional rights clearance verification is required before content can be broadcast or distributed under this agreement. True = clearance workflow required, False = no additional clearance needed.']
  content_rating_restriction varchar [note: 'Content rating limitations imposed by this agreement (e.g., PG-13 and below only, no R-rated content). Comma-separated list of allowed ratings per MPA or TV Parental Guidelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this license agreement record was first created in Rightsline.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this agreement (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the license agreement becomes legally binding and rights become available for exploitation. Start of the agreement term.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the license grants exclusive rights within the territory and media windows. True = exclusive, False = non-exclusive.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the license agreement terminates and rights revert to the licensor. End of the agreement term. Nullable for perpetual agreements.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this agreement (e.g., State of California, England and Wales, New York).']
  holdback_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days during which content must be withheld from certain distribution windows to protect exclusivity in other windows (e.g., theatrical holdback before SVOD release).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this license agreement record was last updated in Rightsline.']
  media_rights_granted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of media exploitation rights included in this agreement (e.g., linear broadcast, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, theatrical, home video, digital download, mobile streaming).']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment to the licensor regardless of actual usage or revenue performance. Common in output deals and syndication agreements.']
  music_sync_license_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement includes music synchronization rights for use of musical compositions in audiovisual content. True = music sync rights included, False = no music sync rights.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contractual terms, special conditions, or internal comments about the agreement.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Structure of the payment obligation. Determines how total_license_fee is disbursed over time.']
  per_episode_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'License fee charged per episode for episodic content. Applicable to syndication and first-run syndication agreements.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each automatic renewal period if auto_renewal_flag is true. Null if agreement does not auto-renew.']
  residuals_obligation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes obligations to pay talent residuals for reuse of content. True = residuals required, False = no residuals.']
  royalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate ongoing royalty payments to the licensor. Determines how revenue is shared or how usage is monetized.']
  royalty_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue or receipts paid to the licensor as ongoing royalties. Applicable when royalty_calculation_method is percentage-based.']
  run_count_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times each episode or program may be broadcast or streamed under this agreement. Common in syndication deals. Null indicates unlimited runs.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was executed by all parties. May differ from effective_date.']
  sublicensing_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to sublicense the rights to third parties. True = sublicensing allowed, False = sublicensing prohibited.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the agreement before expiry or renewal.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territories covered by this license agreement. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers (e.g., USA,CAN,MEX or WORLDWIDE or EMEA).']
  total_license_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contractual payment obligation for the license rights over the full term of the agreement. Sum of all payment milestones.']
  windowing_strategy varchar [note: 'Sequential release strategy defining the order and timing of content availability across distribution channels (theatrical, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, linear, syndication). Free-text description of the windowing plan.']

  Note: 'Master record for all content licensing, acquisition, and syndication agreements managed in Rightsline. Captures the full contractual relationship between Media Broadcasting and rights grantors or licensees (studios, distributors, music publishers, talent guilds, syndication partners). Stores agreement type (acquisition, co-production, syndication, first-run syndication, off-network syndication, international syndication, music sync, output deal), licensor and licensee identities, effective and expiry dates, territory scope, exclusivity flags, total license fee, payment schedule with milestone detail (advances, minimum guarantees, per-episode fees, annual installments), run count limitations for syndication, governing law jurisdiction, and agreement status (draft, executed, expired, terminated). This is the SSOT for all rights-bearing contracts and the anchor entity for all downstream window, royalty, and clearance records.'
}

Table rights.grant {
  grant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rights grant record. Primary key.']
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.acquisition_deal. Business justification: Rights grants are the formal authorization resulting from acquisition deals. Rights management teams must trace each grant back to the originating acquisition deal for audit, rights chain verification']
  asset_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_collection. Business justification: Rights grants are frequently issued at the collection level (e.g., a streaming platform is granted rights to an entire TV series or film library bundle). grant currently only links to individual media']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Rights grants must enforce content rating restrictions by territory. Clearance workflow validates that granted rights comply with rating-based distribution restrictions (e.g., TV-MA content on childre']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rights grants are administered by specific business units (e.g., international licensing, domestic syndication departments) mapped to cost centers. Cost center assignment on grants supports department']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent license agreement from which this grant is derived.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset (film, series, episode, music track) to which this grant applies.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Individual grants of rights (especially sublicense grants, compulsory license grants) may require separate regulatory filings distinct from the parent agreement filing. Copyright registration and comp']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Grants of specific right types (broadcast, streaming, cable) must comply with regulatory obligations (must-carry, local content quotas, compulsory license terms). Linking enables compliance validation']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Rights grants define exploitation type and platform (SVOD, theatrical, broadcast) that map directly to revenue stream categories. Finance requires grant-level revenue stream classification for ASC 606']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Rights grants apply to specific territories. Each rights grant covers one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory string storage and enables consistent territory m']
  blackout_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic or temporal blackout restrictions apply to this grant (e.g., sports blackouts, regional restrictions).']
  carriage_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a carriage fee (distribution payment) is associated with this grant.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current clearance status of the grant. Indicates whether all rights verification and legal clearances have been completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rights grant record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial amounts in this grant (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  drm_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether DRM protection is mandated for content distributed under this grant.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the rights grant expires and exploitation must cease. Nullable for perpetual grants.']
  exclusivity_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the grant is exclusive (true) or non-exclusive (false). Exclusive grants prevent other parties from exploiting the same rights in the same territory and window.']
  grant_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the rights grant, typically a human-readable code used in Rightsline and operational workflows.']
  grant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rights grant. Reflects whether the grant is currently in force, expired, or otherwise inactive.']
  holdback_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a holdback period applies to this grant. Holdbacks restrict exploitation during specific windows to protect other distribution channels.']
  holdback_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the holdback period, after which exploitation may resume. Nullable if no holdback applies.']
  holdback_start_date date [note: 'The start date of any holdback period during which exploitation is restricted. Nullable if no holdback applies.']
  language_version varchar [note: 'Language version of the content covered by this grant (e.g., English, Spanish, French). May reference dubbed, subtitled, or original language versions.']
  media_type varchar [note: 'The media format or delivery mechanism covered by this grant (e.g., television, film, digital streaming, audio).']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment amount for this grant, regardless of actual revenue. Nullable if no MG applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rights grant record was last modified.']
  music_sync_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a separate music synchronization license is required for the music tracks embedded in the content.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional terms, conditions, or operational instructions related to this grant.']
  right_type varchar [note: 'Type of distribution or exploitation right granted. Defines the platform or medium for which the content may be used. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|svod|avod|tvod|theatrical|home_video|music_sync|podcast|linear|non_linear|vod — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage royalty rate applied to revenue generated under this grant. Used for royalty calculations.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the rights grant becomes effective and exploitation may begin.']
  sublicense_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to sublicense the rights to third parties.']
  talent_residuals_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether talent residual payments (reuse payments to actors, directors, writers) are triggered by this grant.']
  window_name varchar [note: 'Named distribution window (e.g., Theatrical, Premium VOD, Pay-TV, Free-TV) that this grant falls within. Used for windowing strategy enforcement.']

  Note: 'Granular rights entitlement record derived from a license agreement. Each row represents a specific bundle of rights granted for a content asset — capturing right type (broadcast, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, theatrical, home video, music sync, podcast), media type, territory or territory group, language version, exclusivity indicator, holdback applicability, and the grant period (start and end dates). Multiple grants can exist per agreement and per content asset. Feeds the availability engine in scheduling and distribution domains. Powered by Rightsline availabilities module.'
}

Table rights.rights_content_window {
  rights_content_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the content window record. Primary key for the rights content window entity.']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.grant. Business justification: A rights_content_window defines the sequential release window strategy derived from a specific grant (the granular rights entitlement). rights_content_window already references license_agreement_id an']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent rights agreement or license contract that governs this window. Links to the master rights agreement.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific content asset (film, series, episode, program) to which this window applies. Links to the content master record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Content windows must comply with regulatory obligations beyond closed captions — local content quotas, children\'s programming windows (E/I requirements), must-carry rules. Linking to regulatory_obliga']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Content windows define platform/window type (SVOD, AVOD, linear, EST) that map to specific revenue streams. Finance uses window-level revenue stream classification for deferred revenue scheduling and ']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Content windows apply to specific territories. Each window covers one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory string storage and enables consistent territory metad']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this window record is currently active and should be considered in availability calculations. False if the window has been superseded, cancelled, or archived.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'The computed net availability status of the content for this window, considering window dates, holdbacks, and blackouts. Refreshed whenever upstream rights or holdback records change. Directly consumed by scheduling and distribution domains to validate playout eligibility.']
  blackout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a geographic or temporal blackout restriction is currently active for this window. True if content is blacked out and cannot be distributed; false otherwise.']
  blackout_reason varchar [note: 'The business or regulatory reason for the blackout restriction (e.g., sports event local blackout, contractual exclusivity, regulatory compliance). Populated only when blackout_flag is true.']
  clearance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting clearance issues, pending approvals, conditional restrictions, or special instructions related to rights clearance for this window.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the rights clearance workflow for this window. Indicates whether all necessary rights, music licenses, talent approvals, and regulatory clearances have been obtained to authorize distribution.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who created this window record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this window record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  drm_requirement varchar [note: 'The DRM technology or protection scheme required for content distribution during this window. Enforces content protection and anti-piracy requirements specified in the rights agreement.']
  exclusivity_tier varchar [note: 'The level of exclusivity granted for this window. Exclusive windows prevent simultaneous distribution on competing platforms; non-exclusive allows parallel exploitation. First and second window designations indicate priority in the windowing hierarchy.']
  geo_blocking_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether geographic blocking technology must be enforced to restrict access to the specified territory. True if geo-blocking is mandatory; false if not required.']
  holdback_end_date date [note: 'The date when a holdback period ends and distribution is re-authorized. Marks the conclusion of the exclusivity restriction.']
  holdback_start_date date [note: 'The date when a holdback period begins, temporarily restricting distribution even within an open window. Used to enforce exclusivity periods and prevent simultaneous exploitation across competing platforms.']
  last_availability_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the availability_status was last recalculated. Updated whenever upstream rights, holdback, or blackout records change. Used to track data freshness for scheduling and distribution systems.']
  license_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary fee paid by the platform or distributor for the rights to exploit the content during this window. May be a flat fee, minimum guarantee, or advance against royalties.']
  license_fee_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the license fee amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed payment amount that the distributor or platform must pay regardless of actual revenue or usage. Common in SVOD and licensing deals.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this window record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this window record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data lineage.']
  platform_scope varchar [note: 'The specific platform, channel, service, or outlet authorized for distribution during this window (e.g., Netflix, HBO Max, ABC Network, theatrical chain). May be a single platform or a platform group.']
  revenue_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of gross or net revenue that the rights holder receives from exploitation during this window. Common in TVOD, AVOD, and theatrical windows.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to revenue or usage to calculate royalty payments for this window. Used when the window operates under a revenue-sharing or usage-based royalty model.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or module from which this window record originated (e.g., Rightsline Windows Module, manual entry, API import). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  sublicense_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the platform or distributor is authorized to sublicense the content to third parties during this window. True if sublicensing is permitted; false if prohibited.']
  usage_cap_type varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the usage cap (e.g., streams for SVOD, broadcasts for linear, screenings for theatrical). Defines how usage is counted against the cap.']
  usage_cap_units varchar [note: 'The maximum number of usage units (streams, downloads, broadcasts, screenings) permitted during this window. Used to enforce contractual usage limits.']
  window_close_date date [note: 'The date when this exploitation window ends and the content is no longer authorized for distribution on the specified platform. End of the availability period. Nullable for perpetual windows.']
  window_extension_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this window has been extended beyond its original close date. Tracks amendments and extensions to the original window terms.']
  window_open_date date [note: 'The date when this exploitation window becomes active and the content is authorized for distribution on the specified platform. Start of the availability period.']
  window_priority varchar [note: 'The numeric priority or sequence order of this window in the overall windowing strategy. Lower numbers indicate earlier windows in the release hierarchy (e.g., 1 for theatrical, 2 for PVOD, 3 for SVOD). Used to enforce windowing hierarchy rules.']
  window_type varchar [note: 'The exploitation window category defining the distribution model and platform type. Determines the sequential release strategy position in the windowing hierarchy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theatrical|pvod|tvod|svod|avod|linear|fast|home_video|syndication|vod — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Defines the sequential release window strategy (windowing) for a specific content asset across exploitation platforms, and computes the net availability status. Each record captures the window type (theatrical, PVOD, TVOD, SVOD, AVOD, linear, FAST, home video, syndication), platform or channel scope, window open and close dates, holdback start and end dates, territory, exclusivity tier, and computed availability status (available, held-back, blacked-out, expired). Enforces the windowing hierarchy that prevents simultaneous exploitation across competing platforms. The net availability (considering holdbacks and blackouts) is refreshed whenever upstream rights or holdback records change. Directly consumed by scheduling and distribution domains to validate playout eligibility. Powered by Rightsline Windows module.'
}

Table rights.holdback {
  holdback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the holdback restriction record. Primary key.']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: Holdbacks frequently apply to specific renditions (e.g., 4K version held back from SVOD while SD is available on a lower-tier platform). holdback already has media_asset_id but not asset_version_id; t']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.contract. Business justification: Holdbacks are frequently triggered by talent contract exclusivity clauses (e.g., prohibiting competing platform licensing during contract term). Linking holdback to the originating talent contract ena']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Distribution agreements frequently impose holdback restrictions — a distributor may require exclusive holdback periods before content can be distributed elsewhere. Direct link enables automated holdba']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_grant. Business justification: Holdbacks restrict specific rights grants. Each holdback applies to one rights grant, establishing which exploitation rights are being restricted. This FK enables tracing holdback restrictions back to']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source license agreement that establishes this holdback restriction.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset subject to this holdback restriction.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Holdbacks in certain territories are mandated by regulatory obligations (theatrical window requirements under local film regulations, blackout rules under sports broadcasting regulations). Linking ena']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Holdbacks apply to specific territories. Each holdback restricts one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory string storage and enables consistent territory metada']
  automated_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this holdback restriction is automatically enforced by scheduling and distribution systems or requires manual review.']
  carriage_negotiation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the MVPD or vMVPD carriage negotiation that established this holdback restriction.']
  clearance_workflow_status varchar [note: 'Status of the clearance workflow process that validates whether content can be scheduled or distributed despite this holdback restriction.']
  conflict_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Documentation of any conflicts with other rights, windows, or holdbacks and how they were resolved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this holdback restriction record was first created in the system.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the holdback restriction is in effect, calculated from start to end date.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the holdback restriction expires and the content becomes available for exploitation on the previously restricted platform, territory, channel, or format.']
  enforcement_status varchar [note: 'Current enforcement state of the holdback restriction, indicating whether it is actively blocking content exploitation or has been modified.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of exclusivity granted by this holdback, defining whether the restriction is absolute or allows limited exploitation.']
  holdback_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the holdback restriction, used for external reference and clearance workflows.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this holdback restriction record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this holdback restriction record was last updated or modified.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification of this holdback restriction has been sent to relevant scheduling, distribution, and operations teams.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this holdback restriction, indicating its importance in windowing strategy and enforcement.']
  restricted_channel varchar [note: 'Specific broadcast or distribution channel that is restricted from airing or distributing the content during the holdback period.']
  restricted_format varchar [note: 'Distribution format or delivery method that is restricted during the holdback period, supporting windowing strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: theatrical|linear_tv|svod|avod|tvod|vod|physical_media|syndication|streaming|broadcast — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  restricted_platform varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the platform, distributor, or service that is restricted from exploiting the content during the holdback period. Applies to platform_holdback restriction types.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holdback restriction type that defines the nature of the exclusivity period.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the holdback restriction becomes effective and the content is blocked from the specified platform, territory, channel, or format.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who authorized the waiver or override of the holdback restriction.']
  waiver_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the waiver or override of the holdback restriction was officially approved.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or explanation for waiving or overriding the holdback restriction, if applicable.']
  windowing_strategy_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or context explaining how this holdback fits into the overall content windowing and sequential release strategy.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this holdback restriction record.']

  Note: 'Records exclusivity holdback restrictions that prevent a content asset from being exploited on a specific platform, territory, or channel during a defined period. Each holdback captures the restriction type (platform holdback, territory holdback, channel holdback, format holdback), the restricted platform or distributor, holdback start and end dates, the triggering window type and source window reference, the source license agreement, and enforcement status (active, expired, waived, overridden). Holdbacks are enforced by the clearance workflow before any scheduling or distribution action is approved. Critical for MVPD carriage negotiations, OTT windowing compliance, and theatrical-to-home-video sequencing. Links to rights_content_window and license_agreement.'
}

Table rights.rights_territory {
  rights_territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the geographic territory. Primary key.']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Rights territories define licensed geographic scope; market coverage defines measurement boundaries. Linking validates that licensed territories have Nielsen/measurement coverage for compliance report']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Rights territories have territory-specific regulatory obligations (GDPR for EU, COPPA for US, local content quotas). The territory product has denormalized flags (gdpr_applicable_flag, coppa_applicabl']
  blackout_restricted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is subject to geographic blackout restrictions for certain content (e.g., sports events, theatrical windows). True if blackouts apply, False otherwise.']
  broadcast_standard varchar [note: 'Technical broadcast television standard used in the territory (e.g., ATSC for North America, DVB for Europe, ISDB for Japan). Determines encoding and transmission requirements for linear distribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: atsc|dvb|isdb|dtmb|ntsc|pal|secam — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_rating_system varchar [note: 'Official content classification and rating system used in the territory (e.g., TV-MA/TV-PG for USA, BBFC for UK, FSK for Germany). Governs age-appropriate content labeling and parental controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the territory (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, JPY). Used for royalty calculations, license fee invoicing, and revenue reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the territory record became active and available for rights grants and distribution agreements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the territory record expires or is no longer valid for new rights grants (nullable for indefinite territories). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  holdback_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is eligible for holdback periods in windowing strategies (e.g., delaying SVOD release to protect theatrical revenue). True if holdbacks apply, False otherwise.']
  internet_penetration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the population with internet access in the territory (e.g., 95.00 for USA, 60.00 for India). Used for OTT and streaming market opportunity assessment.']
  iso_alpha_2_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., US, GB, CA). Used for system integrations and data exchange with distribution platforms.']
  iso_alpha_3_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Provides additional clarity for territories with similar two-letter codes.']
  iso_numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit ISO 3166-1 numeric country code (e.g., 840 for USA, 826 for GBR). Language-independent identifier for international systems.']
  language_primary varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 code for the primary language spoken in the territory (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French). Used for metadata localization and subtitle requirements.']
  language_secondary varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 code for a secondary or co-official language in the territory (e.g., fr for Canada, ca for Spain). Supports multi-language content delivery and compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional territory-specific information, special handling instructions, or regulatory nuances (e.g., \'Requires local dubbing for theatrical release\', \'Subject to military censorship\').']
  ott_market_maturity varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory\'s OTT streaming market maturity: mature (high penetration, competitive), developing (growing adoption), emerging (early stage), nascent (minimal infrastructure). Informs distribution strategy and rights pricing.']
  population varchar [note: 'Total population of the territory. Used for audience reach estimation, market sizing, and rights valuation.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Primary broadcast and media regulatory authority governing the territory (e.g., FCC for USA, Ofcom for UK, CRTC for Canada). Enforces content standards, licensing, and compliance requirements.']
  rights_territory_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the territory in the rights management system: active (available for licensing), inactive (not currently used), restricted (subject to special clearance), pending (under review for activation).']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code representing the territory (e.g., US, USA, GB, GBR). Standardized identifier used in rights grants and distribution agreements.']
  territory_group varchar [note: 'Regional grouping of the territory used for rights aggregation and windowing strategies (e.g., EMEA, LATAM, North America, Worldwide, APAC). Enables multi-territory licensing and holdback management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: worldwide|north_america|latin_america|emea|apac|europe|asia|africa|middle_east|oceania — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the territory (e.g., United States, United Kingdom, Canada). Human-readable identifier used in contracts and reporting.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory scope: country (single nation), region (multi-country group), worldwide (global rights), or custom (bespoke territory definition).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the territory (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo). Used for scheduling, playout, and live event coordination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  vat_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Value-Added Tax (VAT) or Goods and Services Tax (GST) applies to transactions in the territory. True if VAT/GST applies, False otherwise. Used for royalty and subscription billing calculations.']
  vat_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard VAT or GST rate percentage applicable in the territory (e.g., 20.00 for UK, 19.00 for Germany, 0.00 for territories without VAT). Used for financial reconciliation and revenue recognition.']
  withholding_tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard withholding tax rate percentage applied to royalty payments to rights holders in the territory (e.g., 30.00 for USA non-resident aliens, 0.00 for domestic). Used for talent residuals and royalty calculations.']

  Note: 'Reference master for geographic territories and territory groups used in rights grants, holdbacks, and royalty calculations. Each record defines a territory by ISO country code, territory name, territory group (e.g., EMEA, LATAM, North America, Worldwide), broadcast regulatory body (FCC, Ofcom, etc.), currency, and whether the territory is subject to blackout restrictions. Provides the standardized territory taxonomy consumed by all rights, scheduling, and distribution products.'
}

Table rights.royalty_rule {
  royalty_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the royalty calculation rule. Primary key.']
  cba_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.cba_rate_card. Business justification: Royalty rules must conform to CBA-mandated minimum rates and residual formulas. Linking royalty_rule to cba_rate_card enables automated guild compliance validation — if a royalty rule\'s rate falls bel']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Royalty rules specify the GL account to which royalty expenses are posted. Linking royalty_rule to chart_of_accounts ensures royalty calculations are mapped to the correct GL account for financial rep']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Performance-based royalty rules often specify demographic targets (e.g., "2% of net if A18-49 rating exceeds 2.0"). Linking rules to segments enables automated threshold evaluation and demographic-spe']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_grant. Business justification: Royalty rules may apply to specific rights grants. Each rule may apply to one rights grant, defining how royalties are calculated for that grant. This FK enables tracing royalty rules back to the spec']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent license agreement or rights grant to which this royalty rule applies. Links this rule to the contract context in Rightsline.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Royalty rules in media broadcasting are often defined by regulatory obligations (statutory rates set by CRB, guild minimums under collective bargaining). Linking enables validation that royalty rules ']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Royalty rules define calculation methods per exploitation type and platform, which maps to revenue stream categories. Finance uses this link to ensure royalty expense calculations align with the corre']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the royalty rule within the rights management workflow (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this royalty rule. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this royalty rule was approved. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rights holder has audit rights to verify royalty calculations under this rule. True if audit rights are granted, False otherwise.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'The financial or usage metric upon which the royalty calculation is based (e.g., gross revenue, net revenue, subscriber count, stream count, GRP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gross_revenue|net_revenue|subscriber_count|stream_count|grp|unit_sales|advertising_revenue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  content_type varchar [note: 'The type of content to which this royalty rule applies (e.g., film, series, episode, music, documentary, sports, news, short-form). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: film|series|episode|music|documentary|sports|news|short_form — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts in this royalty rule (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  deduction_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deductions (e.g., distribution costs, marketing expenses) are allowed before calculating the royalty. True if deductions are permitted, False otherwise.']
  deduction_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of revenue or basis that may be deducted before applying the royalty rate. Nullable if no deduction applies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this royalty rule ceases to be active. Nullable for open-ended rules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this royalty rule becomes active and applicable to royalty calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this royalty rule record was last updated or modified.']
  maximum_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum royalty payment cap for this rule. Royalty calculations will not exceed this amount. Nullable if no cap applies.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed royalty payment amount for this rule, regardless of calculated royalty. Nullable if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this royalty rule, including special conditions, exceptions, or clarifications.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which royalty payments calculated by this rule are made (e.g., monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, annually, per event, on demand).']
  payment_timing varchar [note: 'The timing of royalty payments relative to the calculation period (e.g., advance, arrears, upon delivery, upon release, milestone-based).']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The distribution platform or exploitation type to which this royalty rule applies (e.g., SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, linear, theatrical, syndication). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: svod|avod|tvod|linear|theatrical|syndication|vod|ott|fast — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the rate value (e.g., percentage, per stream, per subscriber, per unit, per GRP, flat amount).']
  rate_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The numeric rate or percentage applied in the royalty calculation. For percentage-based royalties, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.5 for 15.5%). For per-unit rates, this is the amount per unit.']
  recoupment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this royalty rule includes recoupment provisions (e.g., advance payments must be recouped before additional royalties are paid). True if recoupment applies, False otherwise.']
  recoupment_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold that must be recouped before additional royalty payments are made. Nullable if no recoupment applies.']
  residual_formula varchar [note: 'The formula or calculation logic for residual payments to talent (actors, directors, writers) based on reuse, reruns, or secondary exploitation. Nullable if not applicable.']
  royalty_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the royalty rule (e.g., draft, active, suspended, expired, terminated).']
  royalty_type varchar [note: 'The type of royalty calculation method applied by this rule. Determines how the royalty amount is computed.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code or identifier for this royalty rule, used for reference in contracts and reporting.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the royalty rule, describing its purpose or scope (e.g., \'SVOD Revenue Share - Tier 1\', \'Per-Stream Rate - Music Sync\').']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'The priority or precedence of this royalty rule when multiple rules may apply to the same content or transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  territory_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic territory or territories to which this royalty rule applies. May be a single country code, region, or comma-separated list of territories.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the threshold value (e.g., revenue, subscriber count, stream count, unit count, GRP).']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The threshold value that triggers a rate tier change or special calculation logic (e.g., revenue threshold for tiered royalty rates).']

  Note: 'Defines the royalty calculation rules and rate structures associated with a license agreement or rights grant. Captures royalty type (flat fee, revenue share percentage, per-stream rate, per-unit rate, residual formula), calculation basis (gross revenue, net revenue, subscriber count, stream count, GRP), applicable platform or exploitation type, rate tiers and thresholds, minimum guarantee amounts, and effective date range. Serves as the configuration engine for the royalty calculation process in Rightsline. Distinct from actual royalty payment transactions.'
}

Table rights.royalty_statement {
  royalty_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the royalty statement. Primary key.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Royalty statements with net amounts due create accounts payable entries for payment to rights holders. Required for vendor payment processing, cash management, financial close, and reconciliation of r']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Distribution agreements specify royalty obligations owed to rights holders. Royalty statements are generated per distribution agreement for financial reconciliation and partner reporting. Direct link ']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.grant. Business justification: A royalty_statement is generated for a specific grant\'s exploitation period. royalty_statement already has license_agreement_id (the master agreement) and holder_id (the payee), but is missing the dir']
  holder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rights holder (licensor, talent guild, music publisher, or syndication partner) to whom this statement is issued. Links to the party receiving royalty payments.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Royalty statements trigger GL postings for royalty expense accrual, payment processing, and advance recoupment. Required for month-end close, royalty expense recognition, and financial audit trail lin']

Partner_id identifies the commercial partner entity, distinct from the rig']

Media broadcasting royalty operations require linking each stat']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation.   revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Royalty statements must be classified against a revenue stream for ASC 606/IFRS 15 P&L and segment reporting.   royalty_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.royalty_rule. Rights administrators']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net adjustment amount applied to this statement for corrections, credits, or debits from prior periods.']
  advance_recoupment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from gross royalties to recoup advances previously paid to the rights holder under the agreement.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that approved this royalty statement for payment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this royalty statement was approved for payment.']
  content_title_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured breakdown of royalties by content title or asset. Stored as JSON or delimited string for detailed reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this royalty statement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which royalty amounts are denominated.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date on which the rights holder formally disputed this royalty statement.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason provided by the rights holder if the statement status is disputed. Captures objections to calculations, missing revenues, or contractual interpretation issues.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert foreign currency revenues to the statement currency, if applicable. Rate is typically the average rate for the statement period.']
  exploitation_type_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured breakdown of royalties by exploitation type (theatrical, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, linear, syndication, home video, etc.). Stored as JSON or delimited string for detailed reporting.']
  gross_royalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total royalty amount earned by the rights holder before any deductions, recoupments, or adjustments.']
  minimum_guarantee_shortfall decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between the contractual minimum guarantee and actual royalties earned for the period. A positive value indicates the rights holder is entitled to a minimum guarantee payment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this royalty statement record was last modified or updated.']
  net_royalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final royalty amount payable to the rights holder after all deductions, recoupments, taxes, and adjustments. This is the amount that will be paid.']
  paid_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when payment for this royalty statement was successfully processed and remitted to the rights holder.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method that will be used to remit the net royalty amount to the rights holder.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number or transaction identifier for the payment associated with this statement, once payment has been processed.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which a disputed statement was resolved and moved to approved or paid status.']
  statement_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment of the royalties on this statement is contractually due to the rights holder.']
  statement_frequency varchar [note: 'The reporting frequency for this royalty statement as defined in the underlying rights agreement.']
  statement_issue_date date [note: 'The date on which this royalty statement was officially issued to the rights holder.']
  statement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this royalty statement, including explanations of unusual adjustments, special circumstances, or clarifications for the rights holder.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this royalty statement, used for reference in correspondence and payment processing.']
  statement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the reporting period covered by this royalty statement.']
  statement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the reporting period covered by this royalty statement.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the royalty statement. Draft statements are under review, approved statements are ready for payment, disputed statements have objections from rights holders, paid statements have been settled, and cancelled statements have been voided.']
  territory_breakdown varchar [note: 'Structured breakdown of royalties by geographic territory or market. Stored as JSON or delimited string for detailed reporting.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount withheld from royalty payment as required by applicable tax treaties and regulations.']

  Note: 'Periodic royalty statement generated for a rights holder (licensor, talent guild, music publisher, or syndication partner) covering a specific reporting period. Captures statement period (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual), total royalties due, breakdown by exploitation type and territory, advance recoupment amounts, minimum guarantee shortfall, currency, exchange rate applied, and statement status (draft, approved, disputed, paid). The SSOT for royalty obligations owed to external rights holders. Feeds the billing domain for payment processing.'
}

Table rights.residual {
  residual_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the residual payment obligation record. Primary key.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Residual payments to talent and guilds (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA) generate AP transactions. Linking residual to accounts_payable enables payment tracking, guild audit compliance, and residuals payment stat']
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.acquisition_deal. Business justification: Guild residuals (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA) are triggered by content exploitation under acquisition deals. Residuals reporting to guilds requires identifying the acquisition deal that authorized the exploit']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: Guild residual obligations are triggered by exploitation of specific versions (e.g., a foreign-dubbed version aired in a new territory triggers separate residuals from the original). Linking residual ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying rights contract or license agreement that governs this residual obligation.']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_grant. Business justification: Residuals arise from specific rights grants. Each residual traces to one rights grant that authorized the exploitation triggering the residual. This FK enables tracing residual obligations back to the']
  guild_affiliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.guild_affiliation. Business justification: Residuals are calculated and reported per guild (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA). Linking residual to guild_affiliation enables proper guild reporting, pension/health contribution calculation, and CBA-rate looku']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Residual payments trigger GL postings for expense recognition, liability accrual, and payment clearing. Required for payroll accounting, guild reporting, financial statement preparation, and audit tra']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Residual obligations arise from license agreements. Each residual payment traces to one license agreement that defines the residual terms. This FK enables tracing residual obligations back to the sour']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset (program, episode, film) whose exploitation triggered this residual payment.']
  nielsen_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.nielsen_rating. Business justification: Guild residual calculations for talent are triggered by audience rating thresholds. Linking residuals to actual ratings enables automated calculation, provides audit trail for union reporting, and sup']
  production_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_episode. Business justification: Residuals are calculated per exploitation of a specific production episode under SAG-AFTRA, WGA, and DGA guild agreements. Linking residual to production_episode enables episode-level residual reporti']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Residuals require guild report submissions (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA). The residual product already has guild_report_submission_date and guild_report_submitted_flag, confirming this filing relationship. Li']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Residuals are triggered by and must comply with specific regulatory obligations (collective bargaining agreements with regulatory force, guild minimums). Linking enables compliance teams to track whic']
  rights_content_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_content_window. Business justification: A residual payment obligation arises from the reuse or re-exploitation of content in a specific window (e.g., SVOD re-run, syndication). residual already has exploitation_type, exploitation_start_date']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Residuals are calculated by territory. Each residual relates to one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory code storage and enables consistent territory metadata ']
  royalty_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.royalty_rule. Business justification: Residuals are calculated using royalty rules. Each residual uses one royalty rule that defines the calculation formula. This FK enables tracing residual calculations back to the authoritative royalty ']
  syndication_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.syndication_agreement. Business justification: Syndication is a primary trigger for talent residuals under SAG-AFTRA and WGA rules. Residuals calculations and guild reporting require direct reference to the syndication agreement that triggered the']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, writer, director, crew member) entitled to receive this residual payment.']
  calculated_residual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The computed residual payment amount owed to the talent based on the formula type, calculation basis, and applicable rates.']
  calculation_basis_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base revenue or gross receipts amount used as the input to the residual formula calculation.']
  calculation_basis_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the calculation basis amount (e.g., USD, GBP, CAD).']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The date when the residual amount was calculated by the royalty system.']
  contract_cycle varchar [note: 'The collective bargaining agreement cycle or contract period under which this residual is calculated (e.g., 2020-2023, 2023-2026).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user identifier or system account that created this residual payment record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this residual payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the calculated residual amount (e.g., USD, GBP, CAD).']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date when the residual payment was flagged as disputed. Nullable if never disputed.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for payment dispute if status is DISPUTED (e.g., calculation error, missing exhibition data, contract interpretation disagreement).']
  exhibition_count varchar [note: 'The number of times the content was exhibited or broadcast during the residual calculation period, used for per-exhibition or tiered formulas.']
  exploitation_end_date date [note: 'The date when the content exploitation period that triggered this residual ended. Nullable for ongoing exploitation.']
  exploitation_start_date date [note: 'The date when the content exploitation period that triggered this residual began (e.g., first streaming availability date, first rerun air date).']
  exploitation_type varchar [note: 'The type of content reuse or re-exploitation that triggered this residual payment obligation (rerun, streaming, foreign broadcast, home video, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, syndication, theatrical re-release, clip licensing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RERUN|STREAMING|FOREIGN_BROADCAST|HOME_VIDEO|SVOD|AVOD|TVOD|SYNDICATION|THEATRICAL_RERELEASE|CLIP_LICENSING — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  formula_type varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to determine the residual amount (fixed amount, percentage of gross receipts, percentage of distributor gross, tiered scale based on exhibition count, per-exhibition fee, minimum guarantee).']
  guild_report_submission_date date [note: 'The date when the residual payment was reported to the guild or union. Nullable if not yet reported.']
  guild_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this residual payment has been included in the mandatory guild reporting submission (True/False).']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net residual payment amount disbursed to the talent after withholding taxes and any other deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this residual payment, including special circumstances, calculation adjustments, or dispute details.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date when the residual payment was actually disbursed to the talent or guild. Nullable if not yet paid.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the residual payment must be made to the talent or guild, per the collective bargaining agreement payment schedule.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the residual payment is disbursed (direct deposit, check, wire transfer, guild trust fund, payroll integration).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External payment system reference number or transaction identifier for the disbursed residual payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the residual payment obligation (pending calculation approval, approved for payment, paid, disputed by talent or guild, withheld pending resolution, cancelled).']
  percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to the calculation basis when the formula type is percentage-based (e.g., 0.0350 for 3.5%).']
  reporting_period varchar [note: 'The financial or royalty reporting period to which this residual obligation belongs (e.g., Q1-2024, 2024-H1), used for guild reporting and financial reconciliation.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date when a disputed residual payment was resolved. Nullable if dispute is unresolved or no dispute occurred.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'The user identifier or system account that last modified this residual payment record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this residual payment record was last modified in the system.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of tax withheld from the residual payment per applicable tax regulations (federal, state, foreign withholding).']

  Note: 'Tracks talent residual payment obligations arising from the reuse or re-exploitation of content under guild agreements (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA, IATSE). Each record captures the guild or union, contract cycle, content asset, exploitation type triggering the residual (rerun, streaming, foreign broadcast, home video), residual formula type, calculated residual amount, payment due date, and payment status. Residuals are distinct from general royalties — they are talent-specific reuse payments governed by collective bargaining agreements. Feeds talent and billing domains.'
}

Business justification: Music sync license fees create payables to music rights holders (composers, publishers, PROs). Required for music clearance cost tracking, payment processing, and reconciliation of music licensing obl']
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.acquisition_deal. Business justification: Music sync licenses must be cleared for content acquired under acquisition deals. Music clearance teams track which acquisition deals require sync clearance — a mandatory pre-delivery step. Direct lin']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: Music sync licenses are version-specific: a theatrical cut may contain different music than a TV edit or international dub. Linking music_sync_license to asset_version enables precise cue sheet manage']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.contract. Business justification: Music sync licenses for talent (composers, performers) are governed by underlying talent contracts specifying sync rights scope, exclusivity, and fee structures. Linking music_sync_license to the gove']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Music sync licenses are administered by the music licensing department (cost center). Cost center assignment on sync licenses supports departmental budget tracking and cost allocation for music cleara']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_grant. Business justification: Music sync licenses may derive from broader rights grants. Each sync license may reference one rights grant that authorized the music usage. This FK enables tracing sync licenses back to the authorita']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master rights contract or agreement under which this music synchronization license is granted.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset (program, episode, film, promo) in which the licensed music is synchronized and embedded.']
  production_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_episode. Business justification: A music sync license is cleared for use in a specific production episode. Music clearance teams and cue sheet filers must know which episode each sync license applies to for PRO reporting and residual']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Music sync licenses require cue sheet submissions and PRO (ASCAP/BMI/SESAC) filings. In media broadcasting, every sync license triggers a regulatory/PRO filing. Linking to regulatory_filing enables mu']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Music sync licensing must comply with compulsory license obligations (Section 115, cable compulsory) and PRO reporting obligations. Linking to regulatory_obligation allows compliance teams to verify e']
  rights_content_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_content_window. Business justification: A music synchronization license authorizes the use of a musical composition within a specific content exploitation window (e.g., a theatrical window, SVOD window). music_sync_license already has grant']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Music sync licenses apply to specific territories. Each license covers one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory string storage and enables consistent territory ']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.talent_profile. Business justification: Music sync licenses require talent approval when performers have contractual rights over music usage in their performances. Real business process: talent approval workflow for music clearances in cont']
  clearance_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the music synchronization license was officially cleared and approved for use. Nullable if not yet approved.']
  clearance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting clearance workflow details, restrictions, special conditions, or communications with rights holders during the licensing process.']
  clearance_requested_date date [note: 'Date when the music synchronization license clearance was initially requested from the rights holder or clearance team.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the music synchronization license clearance workflow indicating whether the license has been approved for use in content.']
  composer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the composer or songwriter who created the musical composition.']
  composition_title varchar [note: 'The title of the musical composition or work being licensed for synchronization use.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this music synchronization license record in the rights management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this music synchronization license record was first created in the rights management system.']
  cue_sheet_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cue sheet (detailed music usage report) must be filed with performance rights organizations for this license, typically required for broadcast and public performance.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the music excerpt or cue being licensed, measured in seconds. Used for calculating royalties and determining fair use considerations.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this synchronization license grants exclusive rights to use the music in the specified territory and platforms, preventing the rights holder from licensing the same music to competitors during the license period.']
  isrc_code varchar [note: 'International Standard Recording Code uniquely identifying the sound recording being licensed. Format: 2-letter country code + 3-character registrant code + 2-digit year + 5-digit designation.']
  iswc_code varchar [note: 'International Standard Musical Work Code uniquely identifying the musical composition being licensed. Format: T-NNNNNNNNN-C where N is digit and C is check digit.']
  license_end_date date [note: 'The date when the music synchronization license expires and the right to use the licensed music terminates. Nullable for perpetual licenses.']
  license_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'One-time or upfront fee paid to the rights holder for the synchronization license. Does not include ongoing royalties.']
  license_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the license fee amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or reference number for this music synchronization license, typically assigned by the rights management system or licensing authority.']
  license_start_date date [note: 'The date when the music synchronization license becomes effective and the licensed music may be used in the specified content.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the music synchronization license indicating the scope and nature of rights granted (master use for sound recording, synchronization for composition, blanket for multiple works, per-title for single work, mechanical for reproduction, performance for public performance).']
  licensed_platforms varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution platforms and channels where the licensed music may be used (e.g., linear broadcast, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, theatrical, home video, social media).']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed payment to the rights holder regardless of actual usage or revenue. Nullable if no minimum guarantee applies.']
  pro_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the Performance Rights Organization (e.g., ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, PRS, SOCAN) with which the composer or publisher is affiliated for performance royalty collection.']
  restrictions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any restrictions, limitations, or special conditions attached to this music synchronization license (e.g., no use in advertising, no use with controversial content, limited to specific episodes).']
  rights_holder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity (publisher, record label, production company) that owns or controls the rights to the musical work or sound recording.']
  rights_holder_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rights holder entity (publisher for composition rights, record label for master recording rights, PRO for performance rights organization, independent artist for self-owned rights, production company for commissioned works, collective for rights societies).']
  royalty_basis varchar [note: 'The metric or revenue base upon which ongoing royalties are calculated (gross revenue, net revenue, per-stream count, per-download, per-unit sold, or flat fee with no ongoing royalties).']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to revenue or usage metrics for calculating ongoing royalty payments to the rights holder. Nullable if license is flat-fee only.']
  sublicense_permitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the licensee is permitted to sublicense the music synchronization rights to third parties (e.g., for syndication or international distribution partners).']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last updated this music synchronization license record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this music synchronization license record was last modified or updated.']
  usage_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the music is used within the content asset (background music, theme song, featured performance, promotional use, trailer, opening/closing credits).']

  Note: 'Master record for music synchronization licenses that authorize the use of a specific musical composition or sound recording within a content asset. Captures the music work (composition title, ISRC, ISWC), rights holder (publisher, label, PRO), sync license type (master use, synchronization, blanket, per-title), licensed territory, licensed platforms, license fee, royalty rate, license period, and clearance status. Music sync licenses are a distinct rights category from content licenses and are required for every piece of music embedded in a broadcast or streaming asset.'
}
