  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: Clearance requests are submitted for specific renditions (e.g., clearing the 4K HDR version for a premium SVOD platform vs. SD for a basic tier). clearance_request already has media_asset_id but not a']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Clearance approval requires confirming the airing channel holds the appropriate broadcast license for the content type and territory. Media broadcasting clearance workflows explicitly check broadcast ']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Clearance workflow validates content rating before approving distribution. Analysts check that requested platform/daypart matches rating restrictions (e.g., TV-14 content not cleared for daytime child']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.contract. Business justification: Clearance requests with talent_approval_required=true must reference the governing talent contract to verify exclusivity clauses, holdback periods, and approval authority. Clearance analysts pull the ']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Distribution agreements trigger clearance requests when a distributor seeks to air or stream content. Clearance teams must reference the specific distribution agreement to verify rights scope, territo']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_grant. Business justification: Clearance requests validate against specific rights grants to determine if exploitation is permitted. Each clearance request checks one rights grant for authorization. This FK enables validation of ex']
  holdback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.holdback. Business justification: Clearance requests may be blocked by specific holdbacks. Each clearance request may reference one blocking holdback that prevents exploitation. This FK enables tracing clearance denials back to the sp']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary licensing agreement that governs the requested exploitation. Links to the contract record in Rightsline that defines available windows, territories, and restrictions.']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset requiring clearance verification before broadcast, streaming, or distribution. Links to the digital asset managed in Dalet Galaxy MAM.']
  nielsen_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.nielsen_rating. Business justification: Clearance analysts evaluate requests against historical ratings performance for similar content to assess residual liability exposure, guarantee impact, and rights fee justification before approving e']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Clearance requests are triggered by sales opportunities requiring content availability verification before deal closure. Ad sales and content licensing workflows require rights clearance to confirm no']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Clearance requests are evaluated against regulatory obligations (political ad rules, children\'s programming mandates, local content quotas). Compliance teams need to identify which regulatory obligati']
  rights_availability_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific rights window (theatrical, SVOD, AVOD, linear, etc.) being verified for availability. Links to windowing strategy definitions in Rightsline.']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Clearance requests are for specific territories. Each request relates to one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory code storage and enables consistent territory ']
  sponsorship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sponsorship. Business justification: Sponsorships with product integration, branded content, or talent involvement require dedicated clearance requests covering content rating, talent approval, and music sync. Linking clearance_request d']
  syndication_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.syndication_agreement. Business justification: Syndication clearance is a distinct named workflow in media broadcasting — syndicating content requires specific rights clearance checks per syndication agreement. Direct link enables clearance teams ']
  analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations recorded by the clearance analyst during review. May include references to specific contract clauses, communications with rights holders, or special handling instructions.']
  blocking_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary reason for clearance denial. Enables reporting on common rights obstacles and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: holdback_period|territory_restriction|window_unavailable|rights_expired|music_sync_missing|talent_approval_required|residuals_unpaid|clearance_incomplete — promote to reference product]']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why a clearance request was blocked or denied. May reference specific contract clauses, holdback periods, territorial restrictions, or missing rights. Null if request is cleared.']
  clearance_decision varchar [note: 'Final determination made by the clearance analyst regarding whether the content may be exploited as requested. Approved with conditions may require specific usage restrictions or additional payments.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the clearance request workflow. Tracks progression from submission through review to final disposition. Cleared status allows content to proceed to playout; blocked status prevents distribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_review|cleared|conditionally_cleared|blocked|escalated|expired|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conditional_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or restrictions that must be met for conditional clearance approval. May include required credits, usage limitations, geographic blackouts, or additional royalty payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the clearance request record was first created in the database. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the clearance request required escalation beyond standard analyst review. May reference contract ambiguity, conflicting rights claims, or high-value strategic decisions.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the clearance request was escalated to senior rights management or legal counsel due to complexity, ambiguity, or high business impact. Null if no escalation occurred.']
  estimated_audience_reach varchar [note: 'Projected number of unique viewers or households expected to access the content in the requested exploitation. Used for royalty calculations and rights holder reporting.']
  estimated_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected Gross Rating Point value for the scheduled broadcast or streaming event. GRP represents the sum of ratings achieved by the content and may trigger tiered royalty obligations.']
  estimated_residuals_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected total residual payments owed to talent if the clearance is approved and content is distributed. Used for financial planning and budget approval workflows.']
  exploitation_type varchar [note: 'The intended method of content distribution or monetization requiring clearance. Defines the rights window and licensing requirements that must be verified (e.g., SVOD - Subscription Video On Demand, AVOD - Advertising-Supported Video On Demand, TVOD - Transactional Video On Demand). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_broadcast|svod|avod|tvod|theatrical|syndication|vod|simulcast|fast|digital_download — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of data synchronization between Rightsline clearance system and downstream scheduling and playout systems (Ericsson MediaFirst). Ensures cleared content is automatically released to scheduling workflows.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the clearance request record. Enables change tracking and audit trail for compliance and operational monitoring.']
  music_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether separate music synchronization licenses must be verified before content can be cleared for distribution. True if content contains licensed music requiring additional clearance.']
  platform_channel varchar [note: 'Specific platform, channel, or outlet where the content is intended to be distributed (e.g., HBO Max, NBC Network, YouTube, Hulu). Used to verify platform-specific licensing restrictions.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the clearance request based on scheduling urgency, revenue impact, or strategic importance. Urgent requests require expedited review to avoid schedule disruption.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the clearance request, typically formatted as CLR-YYYYMMDD sequence for tracking and reference in scheduling and distribution workflows.']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the clearance request was initially submitted into the workflow system. Marks the start of the clearance review lifecycle and SLA clock.']
  requested_air_date date [note: 'The target date when the content is scheduled to be broadcast, streamed, or published. Critical for verifying that rights are available within the requested window and that no holdback periods are violated.']
  requested_air_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp for the intended broadcast or streaming start time, including time zone. Used for daypart-specific rights verification and scheduling coordination.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department initiating the clearance request. Identifies which operational area needs rights verification before proceeding with content exploitation.']
  residuals_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requested exploitation will trigger residual payments to talent per guild agreements. True if reuse payments are required for this distribution window.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the clearance request was finalized with a cleared, blocked, or conditionally cleared decision. Marks the end of the clearance workflow and enables SLA compliance measurement.']
  review_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when a clearance analyst began active review of the request. Used to measure queue time and analyst response performance.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clearance request was resolved within the defined SLA target hours. Used for operational performance reporting and process improvement.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Number of business hours within which the clearance request must be resolved per service level agreement. Varies by priority level (urgent requests have shorter SLA targets).']
  talent_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether talent (actors, directors, producers) approval or notification is contractually required before the requested exploitation. True if talent has approval rights per guild agreements or individual contracts.']

  Note: 'Workflow record capturing a rights clearance request initiated before a content asset is scheduled for broadcast, streaming, or distribution. Tracks the requesting department (scheduling, distribution, production), content asset, intended exploitation type and platform, requested air or publish date, territory, clearance status (pending, cleared, conditionally cleared, blocked, escalated), blocking reason if applicable, assigned clearance analyst, and resolution date. Enforces the clearance gate that prevents unlicensed content from reaching playout or distribution systems. Integrates with Ericsson MediaFirst scheduling and Rightsline availabilities.'
}

Table rights.rights_availability_window {
  rights_availability_window_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the computed rights availability window record. Primary key.']
  accessibility_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.accessibility_obligation. Business justification: Availability windows must comply with accessibility obligations (captioning, audio description, video description) per platform and content type. Links distribution windows to the specific accessibili']
  asset_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.asset_version. Business justification: Rights availability windows specify max_resolution constraints (already on rights_availability_window), which map directly to specific asset_versions. Linking to asset_version enables automated delive']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source rights grant record from which this availability window is derived. Links to the upstream rights grant agreement.']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Availability windows derive from license agreements. Each computed availability window traces back to one license agreement that defines the underlying rights. This FK enables tracing availability bac']
  media_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content asset for which this availability window is computed. Links to the content master record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Availability windows must comply with regulatory obligations (local content quotas, children\'s programming mandates, must-carry rules) beyond the accessibility_obligation already linked. Linking to re']
  restricting_holdback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.holdback. Business justification: A holdback directly restricts an availability window — the computed availability window must account for active holdbacks. Adding restricting_holdback_id to rights_availability_window links the availa']
  rights_content_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_content_window. Business justification: rights_availability_window is the computed net exploitable window derived from the windowing strategy defined in rights_content_window. The availability window represents the result of applying holdba']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.territory. Business justification: Availability windows apply to specific territories. Each window covers one territory. Normalizing to territory reference eliminates redundant territory code storage and enables consistent territory me']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription_plan. Business justification: Rights availability windows define platform_type and exclusivity_flag that map directly to subscription plan service tiers. Content availability planning requires knowing which subscription plan a rig']
  advertising_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether advertising insertion (pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll) is permitted during content playback for this availability window. True = ads allowed, False = ad-free distribution only.']
  availability_end_date date [note: 'The date when the content asset is no longer available for exploitation on the specified platform and territory. Represents the end of the net exploitable window. Nullable for perpetual rights.']
  availability_start_date date [note: 'The date when the content asset becomes available for exploitation on the specified platform and territory. Represents the beginning of the net exploitable window.']
  blackout_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a geographic broadcast restriction (blackout) applies to this availability window. True = blackout in effect, False = no blackout restriction.']
  carriage_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a carriage fee (distribution payment to the content provider) applies to this availability window. True = carriage fee required, False = no carriage fee.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'Rights verification status indicating whether all necessary clearances (music synchronization, talent, third-party IP) have been obtained for broadcast. Cleared = all rights verified, Pending = clearance in progress, Blocked = clearance denied, Conditional = clearance with restrictions.']
  computed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this availability window record was computed from the intersection of upstream rights grants, holdbacks, and content windows. Represents the calculation execution time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial amounts associated with this availability window (royalties, minimum guarantees). Defines the monetary unit for rights payments.']
  dai_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Ad Insertion technology is enabled for this availability window, allowing server-side ad stitching into the content stream. True = DAI enabled, False = DAI not permitted.']
  download_to_own_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether permanent download (download-to-own, DTO) is permitted during this availability window. True = DTO allowed, False = streaming-only distribution.']
  drm_requirement varchar [note: 'The level of Digital Rights Management protection required for this availability window. None = no DRM, Basic = minimal protection, Standard = industry-standard encryption, Premium = highest security level.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this availability window record became effective and actionable for scheduling and distribution systems. May differ from computed_timestamp if there is a delay before activation.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this availability window grants exclusive distribution rights on the specified platform and territory. True = exclusive rights, False = non-exclusive rights.']
  expiration_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an expiration notification has been sent to scheduling and distribution systems alerting them that this availability window is approaching its end date. True = notification sent, False = not yet notified.']
  holdback_period_days varchar [note: 'The number of days representing the holdback exclusivity period during which the content cannot be exploited on other platforms. Used to enforce windowing strategies.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the primary language for which this availability window is licensed. Defines the audio/subtitle language requirement.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this availability window was last verified against upstream rights and clearance records. Used for audit and compliance tracking.']
  max_resolution varchar [note: 'The maximum video resolution permitted for distribution during this availability window. SD = Standard Definition (480p), HD = High Definition (720p), FHD = Full HD (1080p), UHD = Ultra HD (2160p), 4K = 4K resolution, 8K = 8K resolution.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed payment amount owed to the rights holder for this availability window, regardless of actual revenue performance. Expressed in the contract currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, exceptions, or special conditions related to this availability window. Used for documenting non-standard terms or manual overrides.']
  offline_viewing_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temporary offline viewing (download for offline playback with expiration) is permitted during this availability window. True = offline viewing allowed, False = online streaming only.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'The distribution platform type for which this availability window applies. SVOD = Subscription Video On Demand, AVOD = Advertising-Supported Video On Demand, TVOD = Transactional Video On Demand, linear = traditional scheduled broadcasting, FAST = Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television, MVPD = Multichannel Video Programming Distributor.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage royalty rate applicable to revenue generated during this availability window. Used for royalty calculations and residuals payments to rights holders and talent.']
  simulcast_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether simultaneous multi-platform broadcast (simulcast) is permitted during this availability window. True = simulcast allowed, False = platform-exclusive distribution only.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the upstream system or module that provided the source rights grant and window data used to compute this availability window. Typically Rightsline or a rights management platform.']
  streaming_protocol varchar [note: 'The technical streaming protocol authorized for content delivery during this availability window. HLS = HTTP Live Streaming, MPEG-DASH = Dynamic Adaptive Streaming over HTTP, RTMP = Real-Time Messaging Protocol, smooth = Microsoft Smooth Streaming, progressive = progressive download.']
  subtitle_requirement varchar [note: 'Indicates the subtitle or closed captioning requirement for this availability window. None = no subtitles required, Optional = subtitles available but not mandatory, Mandatory = subtitles must be provided, Closed_caption = closed captioning required for accessibility compliance.']
  syndication_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the content can be syndicated (resold to multiple outlets) during this availability window. True = syndication permitted, False = syndication prohibited.']
  window_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the availability window. Active = currently exploitable, Pending = future window not yet started, Expired = window has ended, Suspended = temporarily unavailable, Revoked = rights withdrawn, Extended = window duration increased.']

  Note: 'Computed availability record representing the net exploitable window for a content asset on a specific platform and territory, derived from the intersection of rights grants, holdbacks, and content windows. Captures asset reference, platform type (SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, linear, FAST, MVPD), territory, availability start and end dates, exclusivity flag, blackout indicator, and the source rights grant and window records. This is the actionable availability feed consumed by scheduling and distribution domains to determine what can be programmed or published. Refreshed whenever upstream rights or window records change.'
}

Table rights.holder {
  holder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rights holder entity. Primary key for the rights holder master record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Rights holders (studios, publishers, guilds) transact with specific legal entities for royalty payments, tax withholding (1042-S for foreign holders), and intercompany accounting. Linking holder to le']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Rights holders (studios, production companies, distributors) are frequently also commercial partners. Linking holder to partner_partner enables integrated rights and commercial relationship management']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.talent_profile. Business justification: Rights holders in media broadcasting are frequently individual talent (actors, directors, composers) holding performance or composition rights. Linking holder to talent_profile enables residual routin']
  audit_rights_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rights holder has contractual audit rights to review revenue reporting and royalty calculations. True requires enhanced documentation retention.']
  bank_account_name varchar [note: 'Name on the bank account designated for royalty payments. Must match legal entity name for payment processing compliance.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for royalty payment remittance. Encrypted at rest and in transit per PCI DSS requirements.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number or SWIFT/BIC code for international wire transfers. Used for automated royalty payment processing.']
  clearance_priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to clearance requests for content from this rights holder. VIP and critical levels receive accelerated processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rights holder record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed for advance payments or minimum guarantees to this rights holder. Used for financial risk management.']
  default_royalty_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default royalty rate percentage applied to new agreements with this rights holder. Can be overridden at the contract level.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rights holder by organizational type. Determines applicable contract templates, royalty calculation methods, and clearance workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: film_studio|production_company|music_publisher|performing_rights_organization|talent_guild|record_label|news_agency|individual_creator — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exclusivity_preference varchar [note: 'Rights holder\'s stated preference for exclusivity terms in licensing agreements. Influences windowing strategy and holdback negotiations.']
  guild_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of talent guild or performing rights organization (PRO) that this rights holder is affiliated with. Determines residual payment rules and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sag_aftra|wga|dga|afm|ascap|bmi|sesac|gema|prs|socan — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this rights holder record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rights holder record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  minimum_guarantee_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum guarantee amount threshold below which this rights holder will not enter licensing agreements. Used for deal structuring and negotiation.']
  most_favored_nations_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rights holder has most favored nations clause requiring rate parity with other similar rights holders. True triggers rate comparison workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special handling instructions, negotiation history, relationship nuances, or other contextual information for this rights holder.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for royalty disbursements. Determines processing fees and settlement timelines.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days from invoice date for royalty payments to this rights holder. Typical values include 30, 60, or 90 days net.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the rights holder\'s preferred currency for royalty payments and contract valuations.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the rights holder contact. Used for contract notifications, clearance communications, and royalty statements.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the rights holder organization for licensing negotiations, clearance requests, and royalty inquiries.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the rights holder contact. Used for urgent clearance issues and contract negotiations.']
  registrant_identifier varchar [note: 'The ISAN or ISRC registrant identifier assigned to the rights holder by the respective international registration authority. Used for global rights tracking and content identification.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this rights holder was terminated or suspended. Null for active relationships.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this rights holder was established. Used for historical reporting and relationship tenure analysis.']
  rights_holder_code varchar [note: 'Internal business identifier code assigned to the rights holder for operational reference and system integration across Rightsline and billing systems.']
  rights_holder_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the rights holder entity as registered in their territory of incorporation. Used for contract execution and royalty payment identification.']
  rights_holder_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rights holder relationship. Active status is required for new license agreements and royalty payments.']
  royalty_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Standard royalty calculation methodology applied to this rights holder\'s agreements. Determines how residuals and royalties are computed.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number for the rights holder in their jurisdiction of incorporation. Required for tax reporting and withholding compliance.']
  tax_withholding_classification varchar [note: 'Tax withholding classification for royalty payments. Determines applicable withholding rates based on tax treaties and domestic regulations.']
  tax_withholding_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax withholding rate percentage for royalty payments to this rights holder. Derived from tax treaties and domestic law based on withholding classification.']
  territory_of_incorporation varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the legal jurisdiction where the rights holder entity is incorporated or registered. Determines applicable tax treaties and withholding requirements.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this rights holder record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master record for all external entities that hold intellectual property rights licensed to or from Media Broadcasting — including film studios, independent production companies, music publishers, performing rights organizations (PROs), talent guilds, record labels, news agencies, and individual creators. Captures rights holder name, entity type, ISAN or ISRC registrant identifier, primary contact, territory of incorporation, preferred currency, payment terms, and tax withholding classification. This is the SSOT for rights grantor and grantee identity within the rights domain, distinct from the partner domain which manages broader commercial relationships.'
}

Table subscriber.subscriber {
  subscriber_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscriber. Primary key for the subscriber master record across all service tiers (SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, bundled). System-generated surrogate key independent of household or device.']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Business justification: Links subscribers to Nielsen panel profiles and ACR measurement identities. Required for cross-platform audience measurement, ratings attribution, and MRC-accredited viewership reporting. Standard pra']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking subscriber to their household group. Multiple subscribers may share a household for family plans and concurrent stream management.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscriber account. Determines access entitlements and billing state. Active subscribers have full access; suspended accounts are temporarily blocked; cancelled accounts are terminated; trial accounts are in promotional period; churned accounts have completed cancellation.']
  adobe_profile_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this subscriber to their unified profile in Adobe Experience Platform. Used for personalization, segmentation, and journey orchestration.']
  arpu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monthly revenue generated by this subscriber across all revenue streams (subscription, transactional, advertising). Key metric for subscriber value assessment and segmentation.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of subscription billing. Monthly, quarterly, and annual are recurring; lifetime is one-time payment; pay-per-view is transactional.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether California subscriber has opted out of personal data sale. Required for CCPA compliance.']
  ccpa_opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when CCPA opt-out was recorded. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predictive score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating likelihood of subscriber cancellation within next billing cycle. Generated by machine learning model based on engagement, payment history, and behavioral signals.']
  coppa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscriber account is subject to COPPA restrictions (under 13 years old). When true, data collection and advertising are restricted.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing subscriber\'s primary geographic location. Used for content rights windowing, regional pricing, and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscriber record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Subscriber\'s date of birth. Used for age verification, parental controls, COPPA compliance, and demographic segmentation.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for subscriber communication, authentication, and personalization. Used as primary contact channel and login credential.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the subscriber, typically used for integration with Zuora billing and Adobe Experience Platform. May be customer-facing account number or external system key.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s given name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriber has provided explicit consent for data processing under GDPR. Required for EU subscribers.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when GDPR consent was obtained. Required for audit trail and consent management.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s self-identified gender. Used for demographic analytics and content personalization. Optional field respecting subscriber privacy preferences.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of subscriber\'s most recent authentication. Used for engagement tracking and churn prediction.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s family name as provided during registration. Used for personalization and customer service interactions.']
  last_viewing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of subscriber\'s most recent content viewing session. Used for engagement analysis and retention modeling.']
  ltv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Predicted total revenue this subscriber will generate over their entire relationship with the service. Used for acquisition cost justification and retention prioritization.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriber has consented to receive marketing communications. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed for this subscriber based on their service tier. Enforced by playout and CDN systems.']
  max_registered_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to this subscriber account. Used for device management and anti-piracy enforcement.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are active on this subscriber account. When true, content access is restricted based on rating thresholds and PIN protection.']
  parental_control_pin varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN code required to access age-restricted content when parental controls are enabled. Stored in hashed format for security.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for subscriber support and account recovery. May be used for two-factor authentication.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Subscriber\'s postal or ZIP code. Used for geographic segmentation, local content recommendations, and blackout enforcement.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing subscriber\'s preferred interface and content language. Used for UI localization and content recommendations.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the subscriber originally registered. Used for acquisition analytics and channel effectiveness measurement.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the subscriber account was first created. Used for cohort analysis, LTV calculation, and subscriber lifecycle tracking.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Current subscription service tier. SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand) includes basic and premium tiers; AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand) is free with ads; TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand) is pay-per-view; bundle includes multiple services.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription is scheduled to end or renew. Null for active recurring subscriptions. Used for churn prediction and retention campaigns.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the current subscription tier became effective. Used for billing cycle calculation and tenure analysis.']
  total_viewing_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative hours of content viewed by this subscriber since registration. Key engagement metric for LTV calculation and content recommendation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this subscriber record was last modified. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  zuora_account_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this subscriber to their billing account in Zuora subscription management system. Used for payment processing and revenue recognition integration.']

  Note: 'Master identity record for every subscriber across SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, and bundled service tiers. Serves as the SSOT for subscriber identity, capturing registration source, registration timestamp, account status, service tier, demographic attributes, ARPU, LTV, churn risk score, GDPR/CCPA/COPPA consent flags, and integration keys to Zuora (billing) and Adobe Experience Platform (personalization). Each subscriber has a unique identity independent of household or device.'
}

Table subscriber.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the household grouping. Primary key for the household entity.']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Business justification: Household-level audience measurement: Nielsen HUT ratings and addressable advertising are household-unit constructs. Linking household to audience_profile enables household-level GRP/HUT measurement, ']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Households in linear broadcast markets are served under specific broadcast licenses. Required for blackout enforcement, local content obligations, sports territory restrictions, and FCC regulatory rep']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: MVPD/telco-provisioned households are the billing unit under distribution agreements. Carriage fee reconciliation (base_fee_per_subscriber × household count) and partner bundle reporting require attri']
  distribution_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_partner. Business justification: household.mvpd_affiliation is a denormalized plain-text reference to a distribution partner (MVPD). Replacing it with a proper FK enables MVPD-level household analytics, carriage fee attribution, part']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: DMA-based blackout enforcement and local market audience reporting: households must be assigned to a market coverage area (DMA) for content blackout rules, retransmission consent compliance, and local']
  max_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.rating. Business justification: Household-level content filtering enforces a maximum rating ceiling for all profiles in the household. The max_content_rating plain column is a denormalized rating reference; a FK to content.rating.ra']
  account_created_date date [note: 'Date when the household account was first created in the system. Used for tenure analysis and customer lifetime value (LTV) calculations.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the household\'s service location. Used for geographic market assignment and service delivery.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional field for additional address detail.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household subscription automatically renews at the end of each billing period. When false, subscription expires unless manually renewed.']
  average_revenue_per_user decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monthly revenue generated by this household, calculated as total revenue divided by number of active months. Key metric for household value assessment.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month when the household\'s recurring subscription charge is processed. Typically set at initial subscription and remains consistent.']
  churn_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Predictive score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the likelihood of this household cancelling their subscription in the next billing period. Generated by machine learning models based on engagement and payment patterns.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the household is located. Used for geographic segmentation and local content availability.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the household location. Determines applicable content rights, pricing, and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this household record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to sharing their viewing data with third parties for analytics and advertising purposes. Required for privacy compliance.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the household, typically derived from the primary account holder\'s name or a custom household designation.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household account. Determines access rights and billing state.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household based on its composition and purpose. Determines applicable service tier rules and entitlement models.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code representing the household\'s preferred content and interface language. Used for content recommendations and UI localization.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent authentication event for any member of this household. Used for engagement tracking and inactive account identification.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful payment received from this household. Used for delinquency tracking and churn prediction.']
  lifetime_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated by this household since account creation, including subscription fees, transactional purchases (TVOD), and advertising revenue attribution. Used for customer segmentation and retention investment decisions.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional offers. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed for this household based on their service plan. Enforced by the streaming platform to prevent account sharing abuse.']
  max_registered_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to this household account. Used to enforce device limits per service tier.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control restrictions are active for this household. When enabled, content access is filtered based on rating thresholds and PIN protection.']
  parental_control_pin varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN code required to access age-restricted content when parental controls are enabled. Stored in hashed format for security.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument on file for this household\'s subscription billing. Determines payment processing flow and retry logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|gift_card|carrier_billing|app_store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the household address. Used for geographic market (DMA) assignment and service availability determination.']
  primary_subscriber_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary account holder or subscriber who owns this household account. This subscriber has administrative privileges for the household.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Current subscription service tier for the household. Determines content library access, stream quality (SD/HD/4K), and feature availability.']
  size varchar [note: 'Number of registered members or subscribers within this household. Used for family plan eligibility and simultaneous stream limit enforcement.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code for the household address. Used for regulatory compliance and regional content licensing.']
  subscription_end_date date [note: 'Date when the household\'s current subscription is scheduled to end or renew. Null for active auto-renewing subscriptions.']
  subscription_start_date date [note: 'Date when the household\'s current paid subscription began. May differ from account creation date if there was a trial period or reactivation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the household location. Used for scheduling, program guide display, and live event timing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this household record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the household\'s identity and location. Multiple methods may be used in combination for higher confidence.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of household identity and address verification process. Used to enforce geographic entitlements and prevent fraudulent account creation.']

  Note: 'Represents a logical household grouping of one or more subscribers sharing a service plan. Tracks household-level attributes including address, geographic market (DMA), household size, primary account holder reference, parental control settings, simultaneous stream limits, and household verification status. Supports MVPD household-based entitlement models, family plan billing, and account sharing enforcement.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription_plan {
  subscription_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription plan. Primary key for the subscription plan catalog.']
  content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Subscription plans define content rating boundaries for service tiers (kids plans, family plans, adult plans). Essential for parental control enforcement, plan differentiation, and age-appropriate con']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Partner-exclusive subscription plans (e.g., MVPD bundle tiers, telco-exclusive plans) are defined within distribution agreements. Plan availability, content scope, and blackout rules are governed by t']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Subscription plans are defined per legal entity in multi-territory media broadcasting operations, with different pricing, VAT treatment, and regulatory terms per jurisdiction. Finance and legal teams ']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Subscription plan content_library_scope and blackout_restrictions are governed by license agreements. Rights and product teams must know which license agreement authorizes each plan\'s content scope — ']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Subscription plans are platform-specific products (Disney+ Basic vs Hulu Live TV). Billing, entitlement provisioning, and subscriber reporting all require knowing which OTT platform a plan belongs to.']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.package. Business justification: Subscription plans define which content package a subscriber tier can access — a core entitlement provisioning and revenue reporting relationship. The content_library_scope plain column is a denormali']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Each subscription plan maps to specific revenue streams for financial reporting, revenue recognition policies, and segment analysis. Finance requires this mapping for ASC 606 compliance and management']
  target_demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Subscription tiers are designed targeting specific Nielsen demographics (A18-49, A25-54 key demos). Required for pricing strategy, tier optimization, and validating that plan design attracts target au']
  ad_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan includes advertising (AVOD/FAST model) or is ad-free (SVOD model).']
  addon_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plan can be purchased as an add-on to another base subscription plan (e.g., premium sports channel add-on).']
  audio_quality_max varchar [note: 'Maximum audio quality format available under this subscription plan (e.g., stereo, surround 5.1, Dolby Atmos, DTS:X).']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriptions under this plan automatically renew at the end of each billing period by default.']
  base_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard recurring price for the subscription plan before any discounts, promotions, or taxes. Expressed in the default currency.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which subscribers are billed for this plan (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual, biannual, one-time, usage-based).']
  blackout_restrictions boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan is subject to geographic blackout restrictions for certain live sports or regional content.']
  bundled_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a bundled subscription plan that combines multiple services or content packages (e.g., streaming + live TV + music).']
  cancellation_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing subscriber cancellation: anytime (immediate), end of billing period, no refund, or prorated refund.']
  churn_risk_category varchar [note: 'Assessed churn risk category for subscribers on this plan based on historical churn rate analysis (low, medium, high).']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed under this subscription plan. Null indicates unlimited concurrent streams.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract commitment period in months for this subscription plan. Null indicates month-to-month with no minimum commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD).']
  device_registration_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered to a subscriber account under this plan. Null indicates unlimited device registrations.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether offline download of content is permitted under this subscription plan.']
  download_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing at any given time. Null if download_enabled is false or unlimited.']
  early_termination_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged to subscribers who cancel before the end of the contract term. Null if no early termination fee applies.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this subscription plan is available for purchase (e.g., USA,CAN,GBR,AUS). Empty indicates global availability.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when this subscription plan was first made available to subscribers.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this subscription plan record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subscription plan record was last modified in the system.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental control features are available under this subscription plan to restrict content based on ratings.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the subscription plan used in billing systems and customer communications. Managed in Zuora product catalog.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the subscription plan features, benefits, and included content for subscriber-facing materials and internal reference.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the subscription plan displayed to subscribers (e.g., Premium Plus, Family Bundle, Sports Add-On).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subscription plan: active (available for new subscriptions), inactive (temporarily unavailable), deprecated (no longer offered but existing subscriptions continue), sunset (being phased out), coming soon (announced but not yet available).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Business model classification: SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand), FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television), hybrid, or add-on plan.']
  promotional_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a limited-time promotional subscription plan offered at a special price or with enhanced features.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the subscription plan tier indicating the level of service and content access (e.g., basic, standard, premium, ultimate, enterprise, family).']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date when this subscription plan will no longer be available for new subscriptions. Null if the plan is not scheduled for sunset.']
  target_arpu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target average revenue per user (ARPU) metric for financial planning and performance tracking of this subscription plan.']
  target_ltv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target lifetime value (LTV) metric representing the projected total revenue from a subscriber over the duration of their subscription to this plan.']
  trial_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for the free trial period if trial_eligible is true. Null if no trial is offered.']
  trial_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription plan is eligible for a free trial period for new subscribers.']
  video_quality_max varchar [note: 'Maximum video resolution quality available under this subscription plan (e.g., SD, HD, Full HD, 4K, 8K).']

  Note: 'Catalog of all available subscription plans and service tiers offered to subscribers — SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, FAST, bundled, and add-on plans. Captures plan name, tier classification, billing frequency, base price, trial eligibility, concurrent stream limit, download allowance, ad-supported flag, and plan lifecycle status. Managed in Zuora as the product catalog.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription {
  subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription record. Primary key.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Subscription billing account association: every subscription is billed through a billing account for invoice generation, dunning, and balance tracking. In Zuora-based media billing (referenced by zuor']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: MVPD/telco-bundled subscriptions are provisioned under distribution agreements that define service tier, blackout restrictions, and concurrent stream limits. Revenue share reporting and carriage fee r']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Subscription contracts are issued under a specific legal entity for statutory revenue reporting, VAT/GST compliance, and intercompany elimination. In media broadcasting, subscribers in different juris']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Rights-to-revenue traceability requires linking individual subscriptions to the license agreements authorizing their content. Finance and rights teams use this for royalty calculation, minimum guarant']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Active subscriptions belong to a specific OTT platform. Subscriber lifecycle management, platform-level MRR reporting, and churn analysis require direct platform association on the subscription record']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.package. Business justification: Active subscriptions must reference the content package they grant access to, supporting entitlement provisioning, access control enforcement, and MRR-by-package revenue reporting. A media-broadcastin']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.payment_instrument. Business justification: A subscription is billed using a subscriber\'s stored payment instrument. While subscription.payment_method_id already references billing.payment_method (cross-domain), adding payment_instrument_id cre']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method used for recurring billing of this subscription.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Subscriptions generate revenue tracked in specific revenue streams for ASC 606/IFRS 15 compliance, segment reporting, and P&L analysis. Finance teams need to classify subscription revenue by stream fo']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this subscription.']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that defines the service tier, features, and pricing structure.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel or source through which the subscriber acquired this subscription (e.g., web, mobile app, partner referral, promotional campaign).']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was activated and service delivery began. May differ from enrollment date if there was a pending period.']
  arpu decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average Revenue Per User calculated for this subscription over its lifetime, used for subscriber value analysis and forecasting.']
  auto_renew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription will automatically renew at the end of the current term. True = auto-renew enabled, False = manual renewal required.']
  base_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The standard subscription rate amount per billing cycle, in the billing currency, before any discounts or promotions.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of billing for this subscription: monthly, quarterly, annual, semi-annual, weekly, or one-time (for TVOD transactions).']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign identifier associated with the subscription acquisition, used for campaign performance tracking and attribution.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription was cancelled by the subscriber or system. Null for active subscriptions.']
  cancellation_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the cancellation: subscriber (voluntary), system (automated), admin (manual intervention), payment_failure (billing issue), fraud_detection (security). Null for active subscriptions.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for subscription cancellation, used for churn analysis and retention strategy. Null for active subscriptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|payment_failure|fraud|service_quality|price|content_selection|technical_issues — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the subscription contract term in months. Null for month-to-month or open-ended subscriptions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for subscription billing (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  current_term_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current billing term or subscription period. Represents the next renewal or expiration date.']
  current_term_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current billing term or subscription period.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the subscriber first enrolled in this subscription plan. Represents the start of the contractual relationship.']
  grace_period_end_date date [note: 'Date when the grace period ends after a payment failure or cancellation, after which access is fully revoked. Null if no grace period applies.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated or modified.']
  ltv decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lifetime Value representing the total revenue generated or projected from this subscription over its entire lifecycle.']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous video streams allowed under this subscription plan, enforced by the streaming platform.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are enabled for this subscription to restrict access to age-inappropriate content. True = enabled, False = disabled.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional rate expires and standard pricing resumes. Null if no promotion is active.']
  promotional_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The discounted subscription rate amount during the promotional period, in the billing currency. Null if no promotion is active.']
  promotional_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is currently on a promotional or discounted rate. True = promotional pricing active, False = standard pricing.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription is scheduled to renew for the next billing cycle.']
  subscription_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the subscription, displayed to subscribers and customer service representatives.']
  subscription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription: active (in service), suspended (temporarily paused), cancelled (terminated by subscriber or system), expired (end date reached), trialing (in trial period), pending (awaiting activation).']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Classification of subscription model: SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand), bundle, premium add-on, or trial.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the subscription was suspended (e.g., payment failure, subscriber request, fraud investigation). Null for non-suspended subscriptions.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the subscription service was fully terminated and access was revoked. May differ from cancellation date if there is a grace period.']
  trial_end_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period ends and billing begins, if applicable. Null for non-trial subscriptions.']
  trial_start_date date [note: 'Date when the trial period began, if applicable. Null for non-trial subscriptions.']
  zuora_subscription_reference varchar [note: 'External subscription identifier from Zuora billing system for revenue recognition and invoicing integration.']

  Note: 'Active or historical enrollment of a subscriber to a specific subscription plan. Represents the contractual service relationship — capturing plan enrollment date, renewal date, billing cycle, current status (active, suspended, cancelled, trialing), auto-renew flag, promotional rate, and Zuora subscription ID. One subscriber may have multiple subscriptions (e.g., base SVOD + premium add-on).'
}

Table subscriber.device_registration {
  device_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the device registration record. Primary key.']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Enables deduplicated reach/frequency repor']
  device_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.device_type. Business justification: Device certification and DRM enforcement require mapping subscriber devices to certified device_type records. QoS tier assignment, ABR profile selection, and DRM security level validation all depend o']

  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Device registrations are platform-scoped — DRM certificate references, concurrent stream limits, and device caps are enforced per OTT platform. Device management, fraud detection, and platform-level d']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who registered this device. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  ad_tracking_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has consented to advertising tracking on this device. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  advertising_device_identifier varchar [note: 'Device advertising identifier (IDFA for iOS, AAID for Android). Used for targeted advertising and attribution tracking. Subject to user consent per GDPR and CCPA.']
  audio_codec_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audio codecs supported by the device (e.g., AAC, Dolby Digital, Dolby Atmos, DTS). Determines audio track selection.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent streams allowed for this device under the subscribers plan. Enforced at playback initiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this device registration record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  deregistration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the device was deregistered. Used for churn analysis and fraud investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: user_initiated|account_closure|fraud_detected|device_limit_exceeded|inactivity|admin_action|device_lost_stolen — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deregistration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was deregistered or removed from the subscriber account. Null if device is still registered.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique cryptographic hash or identifier that uniquely identifies the physical device. Used for device authentication and fraud prevention.']
  device_limit_enforcement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this device counts toward the subscribers per-account device limit. Used to exempt customer service test devices or promotional devices.']
  device_nickname varchar [note: 'User-assigned friendly name for the device (e.g., Living Room TV, Johns iPhone). Improves device management user experience.']
  drm_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the DRM device certificate used for secure content decryption. Required for protected content playback.']
  drm_security_level varchar [note: 'Security level of the DRM implementation on the device. Determines maximum content quality and protection level (e.g., Widevine L1 for HD, L3 for SD).']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated fraud risk score for this device registration (0.00 to 100.00). Higher scores indicate higher likelihood of fraudulent activity.']
  geographic_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location derived from IP address or device GPS (country, region, city). Used for content windowing and blackout restrictions.']
  hdr_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports HDR video playback (HDR10, Dolby Vision, HLG). Enables premium content delivery.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the device was registered. Used for geolocation, fraud detection, and blackout enforcement.']
  last_active_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent authenticated activity from this device. Used to identify inactive devices and enforce inactivity policies.']
  max_download_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing on this device. Enforced per subscription plan terms.']
  max_resolution_supported varchar [note: 'Highest video resolution the device can decode and display. Used for adaptive bitrate streaming (ABR) profile selection.']
  offline_download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this device is authorized for offline content downloads. Subject to plan entitlements and DRM restrictions.']
  parental_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental controls are active on this device. Restricts access to age-inappropriate content per COPPA and content rating standards.']
  parental_control_pin_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the parental control PIN. Used to authenticate access to restricted content without storing the PIN in plaintext.']
  push_notification_token varchar [note: 'Device-specific token for sending push notifications (APNS for iOS, FCM for Android). Used for engagement campaigns and service alerts.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the device was registered. Used for registration workflow analytics and fraud detection.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the device registration. Determines whether the device is authorized for playback.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first registered by the subscriber. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this device registration record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'HTTP user agent string reported by the device. Contains detailed device, OS, and browser information for analytics and troubleshooting.']

  Note: 'Records every device registered by a subscriber for authenticated playback. Captures device type (smart TV, mobile, tablet, set-top box, web browser, gaming console), device fingerprint, manufacturer, model, OS platform and version, registration timestamp, last active timestamp, device nickname, device status (active, deregistered, blocked), and DRM device certificate reference. Enforces per-account device limits and supports device management workflows including remote deregistration.'
}

  activated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement transitioned from pending to active status and became usable by the subscriber.']
  ad_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement provides ad-free viewing experience. False indicates AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand) model applies.']
