  auto_renew boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement will automatically renew upon expiration based on the underlying subscription plan.']
  blackout_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement is exempt from geographic blackout restrictions typically applied to live sports or regional content.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance requirements applicable to this entitlement (e.g., \'GDPR\', \'CCPA\', \'COPPA\'). Used to enforce data privacy and regulatory obligations.']
  concurrent_stream_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous streams allowed under this entitlement. Null indicates no stream limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was first created in the system.']
  device_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent devices that can use this entitlement. Null indicates no device limit.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether offline download is permitted for content under this entitlement.']
  early_access_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entitlement grants early access to content before general release.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement becomes active and access rights begin.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entitlement: active (currently valid), suspended (temporarily disabled), expired (past end date), revoked (administratively cancelled), or pending (awaiting activation).']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entitlement based on how it was granted: subscription (standard plan), promotional (marketing grant), bundle (package deal), addon (premium add-on), mvpd_carriage (Multichannel Video Programming Distributor carriage agreement), or trial (free trial period).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement expires and access rights end. Null for perpetual entitlements.']
  external_entitlement_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this entitlement in the originating external system (e.g., Zuora subscription ID, MVPD authorization token).']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this entitlement is valid. Empty indicates no geographic restriction.']
  grant_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason code for why this entitlement was granted (e.g., \'Annual Subscription\', \'Promotional Campaign Q4-2024\', \'Customer Retention Offer\', \'MVPD Carriage Agreement\').']
  last_authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent playback authorization request that validated this entitlement at the CDN/DRM layer.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this entitlement record was last updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional administrative notes or special instructions related to this entitlement.']
  parental_control_level varchar [note: 'Maximum content rating level accessible under this entitlement based on parental control settings. Follows Motion Picture Association (MPA) and TV Parental Guidelines ratings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|g|pg|pg13|r|nc17|tv_y|tv_y7|tv_g|tv_pg|tv_14|tv_ma — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the entitlement was revoked or suspended, if applicable (e.g., \'Payment Failure\', \'Terms of Service Violation\', \'Subscriber Request\', \'Rights Expiration\').']
  revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement was permanently revoked, if applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated this entitlement record: zuora (subscription billing), adobe_experience (personalization platform), mvpd_integration (multichannel video programming distributor), promotional_engine (marketing campaigns), or manual (administrative grant).']
  suspended_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement was suspended, if applicable.']
  trial_entitlement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a trial entitlement granted as part of a free trial period.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific content access rights granted to a subscriber based on their active subscription plan(s). Maps subscriber to entitled content windows, channel packages, VOD libraries, and premium add-ons. Captures entitlement source (plan, promotional grant, MVPD carriage), effective date, expiry date, and entitlement status. Drives playback authorization decisions at the CDN/DRM layer.'
}

Table subscriber.parental_control {
  parental_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parental control configuration record. Primary key.']
  content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.rating. Business justification: Parental control enforcement requires joining to the canonical rating system to block content above a threshold. The content_rating_threshold plain column is a denormalized rating reference; replacing']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.household. Business justification: Parental controls can be configured at household level (applying to all profiles in the household) or at individual profile level. Currently parental_control has subscriber_id and profile_id FKs. Addi']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Parental control configurations are platform-specific — COPPA compliance obligations, content rating systems, and viewing restriction rules differ per OTT platform. Regulatory compliance reporting (CO']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber account that owns this parental control configuration. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  viewer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific viewing profile within the subscriber account to which this parental control applies. Enables per-profile restrictions within a household.']
  allowed_viewing_end_time timestamp [note: 'End time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is blocked after this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.']
  allowed_viewing_start_time timestamp [note: 'Start time of the daily viewing window in HH:MM format (24-hour clock). Content access is permitted starting at this time. Null if viewing_time_restriction_enabled is False.']
  autoplay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic playback of next episode or recommended content is enabled for this profile. True if autoplay is on, False if disabled (often disabled for child profiles to limit binge-watching).']
  blocked_content_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of content genre or category codes that are explicitly blocked for this profile (e.g., \'horror\', \'violence\', \'adult\'). Provides granular control beyond rating thresholds.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber has opted out of data sharing under CCPA. True if opted out, False otherwise. Affects how parental control data may be used for analytics or personalization.']
  configuration_name varchar [note: 'User-defined label for this parental control configuration (e.g., \'Kids Profile\', \'Teen Settings\'). Helps account holders manage multiple configurations.']
  content_language_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 639-1 language codes (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'fr\') to which content access is restricted. Null if no language restrictions apply. Used to limit content to specific languages for child profiles.']
  coppa_child_profile_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profile is designated as belonging to a child under 13 years of age, triggering COPPA compliance requirements. True if the profile is for a child under 13, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parental control configuration record was first created in the system.']
  device_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parental control configuration restricts viewing to specific registered devices. True if device restrictions are enforced, False if profile can be accessed from any device.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this parental control configuration became or will become active. Supports scheduled activation of restrictions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this parental control configuration expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null if no expiration is set.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder has provided explicit consent for processing parental control data under GDPR. True if consent is granted, False otherwise. Required for EU subscribers.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that last modified this parental control configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parental control configuration was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  maturity_level_override varchar [note: 'Custom maturity level designation that overrides standard rating thresholds for specific content. Used for granular exceptions (e.g., allowing a specific R-rated film for educational purposes). Null if no override is set.']
  max_daily_viewing_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of minutes of content viewing allowed per day for this profile. Null if no daily limit is set. Used to enforce screen time limits for child profiles.']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'Email address to which parental control alerts and notifications are sent (e.g., when a child attempts to access restricted content). Null if notifications are disabled.']
  parental_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this parental control configuration. Active indicates the configuration is enforced; Inactive indicates it is saved but not enforced; Suspended indicates temporary deactivation by system or admin.']
  pin_code_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the parental control PIN. Stored as a one-way hash for security. Never stored in plaintext. Used to authenticate access to restricted content or settings changes.']
  pin_protection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN is required to access content above the rating threshold or to modify parental control settings. True if PIN protection is active, False otherwise.']
  purchase_lock_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactional purchases (TVOD - Transactional Video On Demand) are blocked for this profile. True if purchase lock is active (preventing rentals/purchases), False if purchases are allowed.']
  search_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether search functionality is restricted to only show age-appropriate content. True if search results are filtered by parental controls, False if unrestricted search is allowed.']
  viewing_time_restriction_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether time-of-day viewing restrictions are enforced for this profile. True if time restrictions are active, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Stores parental control configurations set by the account holder for a household or individual profile. Captures content rating thresholds (MPA ratings: G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17), PIN protection settings, COPPA child profile flag, blocked content categories, purchase lock, and time-of-day viewing restrictions. Enforces COPPA compliance for profiles identified as under-13.'
}

Table subscriber.viewer_profile {
  viewer_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the viewer profile within a subscriber account or household. Primary key.']
  abr_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.abr_profile. Business justification: viewer_profile.video_quality_preference is a denormalized reference to an ABR profile tier. Linking to abr_profile allows the streaming system to resolve the correct encoding ladder for a viewer\'s pre']
  audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_profile. Business justification: Profile-level audience measurement: streaming platforms map each viewer profile to an audience measurement identity (Nielsen/Adobe) for cross-platform GRP/TRP reporting and addressable ad targeting. A']
  device_registration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for device tracking, security monitoring, and cross-device analytics. May reference device registration records.']
  favorite_genre_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.genre. Business justification: Personalization and recommendation engines require explicit genre preferences. Drives content discovery, homepage curation, and email marketing. Complements behavioral signals with declared preference']
  format_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to mediaasset.format_specification. Business justification: A viewer profile stores the preferred playback quality, which must resolve to a specific format_specification to select the correct asset_version for streaming or download. This per-profile quality re']
  max_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Viewer profiles enforce maximum content rating thresholds for parental controls and age-appropriate content filtering. Core mechanism for COPPA compliance, kids profile restrictions, and content acces']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Viewer profiles are platform-specific — personalization settings, quality preferences, and feature availability differ per OTT platform. Platform engineering and product teams need this link for platf']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Dynamic Ad Insertion (DAI) and personalization: viewer profiles must be mapped to audience activation segments to enable DAI targeting on streaming platforms. Media broadcasters assign viewer profiles']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent subscriber account that owns this viewer profile. One subscriber may have multiple viewer profiles (e.g., up to 5 profiles per account).']
  aep_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Adobe Experience Platform segment IDs that this viewer profile belongs to. Used for personalized content recommendations, targeted advertising, and dynamic user experience customization.']
  audio_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred audio track language. Used for multi-language content with multiple audio tracks.']
  autoplay_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether autoplay of next episode or content is enabled for this profile. True enables automatic playback; false requires manual selection.']
  autoplay_previews_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic preview playback is enabled when browsing content. True enables preview autoplay; false disables previews.']
  avatar_url varchar [note: 'URL reference to the profile avatar image stored in the Media Asset Management (MAM) system or Content Delivery Network (CDN). Used for visual identification in profile selection interfaces.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer has opted out of personal data sale under CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act). True indicates opt-out; false indicates consent. Required for California viewer data privacy compliance.']
  coppa_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is subject to COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance requirements. True for profiles of children under 13; false for adult profiles. Enforces enhanced privacy protections and parental consent requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, lifecycle tracking, and compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_saver_mode boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether data saver mode is enabled to reduce bandwidth consumption. True limits video quality and disables high-bandwidth features; false allows full quality streaming.']
  download_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this profile is entitled to download content for offline viewing. True enables download feature; false restricts to streaming only. Controlled by subscription tier and DRM (Digital Rights Management) policies.']
  gdpr_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) consent was obtained for this viewer profile. Required for EU audience data privacy compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  genre_preferences varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred content genre identifiers for personalization and recommendation algorithms. Derived from viewing history and explicit user selections. Used by Adobe Experience Platform for segment targeting.']
  is_default_profile boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default profile that is automatically selected when the subscriber logs in. True for default profile; false for secondary profiles. Only one profile per subscriber should be marked as default.']
  is_kids_profile boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a dedicated kids profile with enhanced parental controls, age-appropriate content filtering, and COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) compliance enforcement. True for kids profiles; false for adult/general profiles.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code with optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., \'en\', \'en-US\', \'es-MX\'). Determines the language for user interface, content metadata, and audio/subtitle defaults.']
  last_access_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was last accessed or used for content viewing. Used for engagement analytics, churn prediction, and inactive profile identification. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the viewer profile was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  last_platform varchar [note: 'Platform type of the last device used to access this viewer profile. Used for platform-specific analytics and Over-The-Top (OTT) distribution tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_ios|mobile_android|smart_tv|roku|fire_tv|apple_tv|chromecast|gaming_console — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the viewer profile has consented to receive marketing communications and promotional content. True allows marketing; false opts out. Required for GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.']
  maturity_rating_level varchar [note: 'Maximum content maturity rating level allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces parental controls and content filtering based on Motion Picture Association (MPA) film ratings and TV Parental Guidelines. Content exceeding this rating is blocked from playback. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  max_concurrent_streams varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent video streams allowed for this viewer profile. Enforces Digital Rights Management (DRM) and subscription tier entitlements. Typical values range from 1 to 4 streams.']
  max_downloads varchar [note: 'Maximum number of content titles that can be downloaded for offline viewing on this profile. Enforces subscription tier limits and storage management policies.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether personalized content recommendations and user experience customization are enabled for this profile. True enables Adobe Experience Platform personalization; false provides generic content discovery. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) consent requirements.']
  pin_code_hash varchar [note: 'Hashed PIN code for profile access control and parental lock enforcement. Used to restrict access to mature content or prevent unauthorized profile switching. Stored as cryptographic hash for security.']
  pin_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether PIN protection is enabled for this profile. True requires PIN entry for profile access; false allows unrestricted access.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the viewer profile (e.g., \'Dad\', \'Kids\', \'Guest\'). Used for profile selection and personalization.']
  profile_sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer value determining the display order of this profile in the profile selection interface. Lower values appear first. Used for user experience optimization.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the viewer profile. Active profiles can access content; inactive profiles are temporarily disabled; suspended profiles are blocked due to policy violations; deleted profiles are soft-deleted and retained for compliance.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the viewer profile within the household. Primary profiles have full account privileges; secondary profiles may have restricted access; kids profiles enforce parental controls; guest profiles are temporary.']
  subtitle_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for preferred subtitle language. Used when subtitle_preference is enabled.']
  subtitle_preference varchar [note: 'Default subtitle display preference for this viewer profile. Off disables subtitles; on always displays subtitles; auto displays subtitles based on content language and user language mismatch.']
  total_viewing_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative total viewing hours for this viewer profile across all content and sessions. Used for engagement metrics, Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) analysis, and Lifetime Value (LTV) calculations.']
  viewing_history_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether viewing history is tracked and stored for this profile. True enables history tracking for recommendations and resume playback; false disables tracking for privacy. Impacts GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) and CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual viewing profile within a subscriber account or household — supporting multi-profile accounts (e.g., up to 5 profiles per account). Captures profile name, avatar, language preference, content genre preferences, maturity rating level, is_kids_profile flag, and Adobe Experience Platform segment IDs for personalization. Distinct from the subscriber master record — one subscriber may have multiple viewer profiles.'
}

Table subscriber.churn_event {
  churn_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscriber churn event record.']
  cancellation_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Cancellation proration credit issuance: when subscribers cancel mid-billing-period, a credit memo is issued for unused days. Linking churn_event to the resulting credit_memo supports cancellation fina']
  cancellation_refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.refund. Business justification: Churn-triggered refund reconciliation: annual or prepaid subscription cancellations generate refunds that must be linked to the churn event for cancellation financial close-out, win-back offer cost ac']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Churn events are analyzed by cost center for retention program budgeting and departmental accountability. Marketing and customer success teams track churn metrics against their cost center budgets for']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Churn-by-demographic reporting: subscriber retention teams and audience strategy teams routinely analyze churn rates by Nielsen demographic cell (A18-49, W25-54, etc.) to identify at-risk segments and']
  distribution_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MVPD or vMVPD partner through which the subscription was provisioned. Null for direct-to-consumer subscriptions. Used for partner churn analysis.']
  final_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Cancellation final invoice reconciliation: churn events trigger final prorated invoices or zero-balance invoices at cancellation. Finance teams require churn_event → invoice linkage for churn revenue ']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household to which the churned subscriber belonged. Used for household-level churn analysis and multi-subscriber household retention strategies.']
  last_viewed_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Churn analysis requires identifying which channel a subscriber was last watching before cancellation — a standard retention analytics and content investment decision process. This FK enables channel-l']
  last_viewed_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Churn analysis tracks last content engagement to identify content gaps and inform win-back campaigns. Critical for retention analytics, content investment decisions, and personalized re-engagement off']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Churn events are platform-specific — a subscriber churning from one OTT platform is a distinct business event. Platform-level churn rate reporting, win-back campaign targeting, and competitive analysi']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Churn analysis by profit center is critical for segment profitability reporting and EBITDA calculation. Media companies track subscriber retention by business unit for strategic resource allocation an']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Churn events trigger regulatory obligations (GDPR data retention, right-to-deletion workflows, cancellation notice requirements, refund regulations). Required for compliance automation, audit trails, ']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Churn events represent lost revenue that must be attributed to a specific revenue stream for MRR/ARR churn reporting and revenue forecasting. Media broadcasting finance teams track churn impact by rev']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who churned. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific subscription that was cancelled. Links to the subscription master record.']
  auto_renew_disabled_date date [note: 'The date when the subscriber disabled auto-renewal for their subscription. Null if auto-renewal was never disabled. Used to identify planned churn versus reactive churn.']
  cancellation_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or channel through which the cancellation was initiated. Supports analysis of churn patterns by customer touchpoint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_self_service|mobile_app|customer_care|email|chat|ivr|mvpd_drop|system_automated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the stated reason for cancellation. Used for churn analytics and product improvement initiatives. Empty value_regex indicates reference to external reason code lookup table with more than 6 values.']
  cancellation_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or additional details provided by the subscriber explaining the reason for cancellation. Supports qualitative churn analysis.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber exercised CCPA opt-out rights regarding data sale or sharing. Used for privacy compliance and marketing eligibility.']
  churn_prediction_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'The machine learning model-generated churn risk score that was assigned to the subscriber prior to the churn event. Range 0.0000 to 1.0000, where higher values indicate higher churn risk. Used for model validation and predictive accuracy assessment.']
  churn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber cancellation or lapse event occurred. This is the business event timestamp representing when the subscriber relationship ended.']
  churn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the churn event based on the nature of cancellation. Voluntary indicates subscriber-initiated cancellation, involuntary indicates business-initiated termination, payment failure indicates cancellation due to billing issues.']
  competitor_service_mentioned_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber mentioned switching to a competitor service during the cancellation process. Used for competitive intelligence and market positioning analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this churn event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the last payment amount. Supports multi-currency subscription operations.']
  customer_care_contact_count varchar [note: 'The number of customer care interactions the subscriber had in the 30 days prior to churn. Used to identify service quality issues and early warning signals.']
  days_since_last_viewing varchar [note: 'The number of days between the last viewing activity and the churn event. Used for engagement-based churn prediction models.']
  device_count_at_churn varchar [note: 'The number of registered devices associated with the subscriber at the time of churn. Used for multi-device engagement analysis.']
  final_access_date date [note: 'The last date the subscriber had active access to content and services. May differ from churn_timestamp due to grace periods or paid-through dates.']
  gdpr_data_deletion_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber requested deletion of their personal data under GDPR right to erasure at the time of churn. Used for privacy compliance tracking.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the last successful payment received from the subscriber before churn. Used for ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) and revenue impact analysis.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'The date of the last successful payment received from the subscriber before churn. Used for revenue recognition and payment failure analysis.']
  last_viewing_date date [note: 'The date of the subscribers last content viewing activity before churn. Used to identify engagement drop-off patterns and at-risk subscriber signals.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the churn event captured by customer care agents or system processes.']
  parental_control_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether parental controls were enabled on the subscription at the time of churn. Used for family-oriented subscriber segmentation and churn analysis.']
  payment_failure_count varchar [note: 'The number of consecutive payment failures that occurred before the churn event. Used to identify involuntary churn patterns and dunning effectiveness.']
  promotional_discount_active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber had an active promotional discount at the time of churn. Used to analyze price sensitivity and promotional effectiveness.']
  reactivation_eligibility_date date [note: 'The date when the churned subscriber becomes eligible for reactivation campaigns or promotional offers. Used for win-back campaign targeting and timing.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'The subscription service tier that was cancelled. SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), TVOD (Transactional Video On Demand).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this churn event. Typically Zuora for subscription billing events or Adobe Experience Platform for digital engagement tracking.']
  subscription_tenure_months varchar [note: 'The total number of months the subscriber maintained an active subscription before churning. Used for cohort analysis and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculations.']
  total_lifetime_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative revenue generated from this subscriber across their entire subscription tenure. Used for LTV (Lifetime Value) analysis and churn impact assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this churn event record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  win_back_offer_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the subscriber accepted the win-back offer. True indicates acceptance and retention, False indicates rejection and churn completion. Null if no offer was presented.']
  win_back_offer_presented_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a retention or win-back offer was presented to the subscriber during the cancellation process. True indicates an offer was made, False indicates no offer was presented.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a subscriber cancellation or lapse event. Captures cancellation timestamp, cancellation channel (self-service, customer care, payment failure, MVPD drop), stated cancellation reason, win-back offer presented flag, win-back offer accepted flag, and reactivation eligibility date. Supports churn rate reporting and retention campaign targeting. Distinct from analytics — this is the operational event record.'
}

Table subscriber.subscription_change {
  subscription_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the subscription change event. Primary key.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Subscription changes (upgrades, downgrades, cross-border plan switches) may involve a change in billing legal entity. Finance needs to attribute MRR impact and proration amounts to the correct legal e']
  offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer or retention campaign that was redeemed during this subscription change. Null if no offer was applied.']
  offer_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.offer_redemption. Business justification: A subscription change is frequently triggered by a promotional offer redemption (e.g., a subscriber redeems a discount code that causes a plan upgrade or downgrade). Adding offer_redemption_id as a FK']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Subscription changes (upgrades, downgrades, cancellations) are platform-specific events. Platform-level MRR impact reporting and subscription change analytics — core finance and product KPIs — require']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Subscription changes originating from partner-driven campaigns (MVPD upgrade offers, bundling promotions, affiliate retention programs) require tracking the partner for revenue attribution, partner pe']
  primary_subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that was active before this change. Null for new subscriptions or add-on attach events.']
  proration_credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Downgrade proration credit memo tracking: subscription downgrades generate prorated credit memos for the billing period difference. subscription_change already links to the invoice via zuora_invoice_i']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Plan upgrades/downgrades affect revenue stream classification for financial reporting. Finance tracks MRR changes by revenue stream for forecasting, variance analysis, and segment performance reportin']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this subscription. Enables subscriber-level change analysis and churn tracking.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription that was modified. Links to the parent subscription record in the subscriber domain.']
  zuora_invoice_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Zuora invoice generated for this subscription change. Links the change event to billing and revenue recognition processes.']
  applied_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total discount amount applied to the new plan as part of this change. Includes promotional offers, retention discounts, and loyalty rewards.']
  applied_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the new plan subscription fee. Used for promotion effectiveness analysis and revenue impact modeling.']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic renewal is enabled for the subscription after this change. Critical for churn prediction and renewal revenue forecasting.']
  change_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the subscription change was initiated. Enables channel effectiveness analysis and self-service adoption tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile app|call center|chat|email|api|admin portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_effective_date date [note: 'The date when the subscription change became effective and billing adjustments were applied. Critical for revenue recognition and proration calculations.']
  change_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the agent or system during the change process. Captures context, customer feedback, and special handling instructions.']
  change_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the system completed processing the change and updated the subscription state. Used for operational efficiency metrics.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'The primary reason provided or inferred for the subscription change. Critical for churn analysis, retention strategy, and product improvement.']
  change_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber or agent initiated the change request. Used for time-to-fulfillment analysis and customer experience tracking.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'The type of change applied to the subscription. Categorizes the nature of the modification for lifecycle analysis and revenue impact assessment.']
  content_entitlement_change varchar [note: 'Describes changes to content library access, premium channel entitlements, or windowing rights resulting from this subscription change. Critical for content licensing and rights management.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The length of the new subscription contract term in months resulting from this change. Used for commitment analysis and churn risk modeling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription change record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  device_limit_new varchar [note: 'The maximum number of concurrent devices allowed under the new subscription plan. Enables analysis of device limit impact on upgrade and downgrade decisions.']
  device_limit_previous varchar [note: 'The maximum number of concurrent devices allowed under the previous subscription plan. Used to track device entitlement changes and household sharing patterns.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this subscription change record was last updated in the data platform. Supports change data capture and incremental processing workflows.']
  mrr_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The net change in monthly recurring revenue resulting from this subscription change. Positive for upgrades, negative for downgrades. Calculated as new_monthly_recurring_revenue minus previous_monthly_recurring_revenue.']
  new_billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing frequency of the new subscription plan. Enables analysis of billing period changes and their impact on cash flow and retention.']
  new_monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly recurring revenue amount from the new plan after this change. Used to calculate MRR expansion, contraction, and net revenue retention metrics.']
  previous_billing_period varchar [note: 'The billing frequency of the previous subscription plan. Used to analyze billing period migration patterns and payment preference trends.']
  previous_monthly_recurring_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monthly recurring revenue amount from the previous plan before this change. Critical for MRR movement analysis and revenue waterfall reporting.']
  proration_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The prorated credit or charge amount applied to the subscriber account due to mid-cycle plan changes. Positive values represent charges, negative values represent credits.']
  proration_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the proration amount. Supports multi-currency subscription operations.']
  service_tier_change varchar [note: 'Describes the service tier transition for this subscription change. Specific to media streaming business models including AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), and tiered SVOD offerings.']
  zuora_amendment_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Zuora subscription amendment record that represents this change. Enables reconciliation with the billing system of record.']

  Note: 'Audit trail of all plan changes, upgrades, downgrades, pauses, reinstatements, and promotional offer redemptions on a subscriber\'s subscription. Captures previous plan, new plan, change type (upgrade, downgrade, pause, reactivate, add-on attach, add-on detach, offer redemption), effective date, change channel, proration amount, applied discount, offer reference, redemption status, and Zuora amendment reference. Enables subscription lifecycle analysis, promotion effectiveness tracking, and revenue reconciliation.'
}

Table subscriber.payment_instrument {
  payment_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment instrument record. Primary key.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Payment instruments are processed under specific legal entities that determine which merchant account, payment gateway, and tax withholding rules apply. In multi-territory media broadcasting, the lega']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who owns this payment instrument. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  zuora_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for this payment method in the Zuora subscription billing system. Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  bank_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account for direct debit or ACH payment methods. Null for card-based payment methods.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number (ABA number in USA) for direct debit transactions. Masked or tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address associated with the payment instrument. Used for address verification and fraud prevention.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (apartment, suite, unit number). Optional field for additional address details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address associated with the payment instrument.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Used for address verification service (AVS) fraud checks.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address. Format varies by country (e.g., two-letter state code for USA).']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The brand of the credit or debit card (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, JCB, Diners Club). Null for non-card payment methods.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment instrument. Required for card-based payment methods.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment instrument (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Determines the currency for all transactions using this instrument.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument was deactivated or closed. Null for active instruments. Used for lifecycle tracking and compliance reporting.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the payment instrument was deactivated. Used for churn analysis and payment method lifecycle management.']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'The month (1-12) when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit year when the payment instrument expires. Used for card expiration validation and renewal reminders.']
  failed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of failed transaction attempts using this payment instrument. Used for fraud detection and payment method health monitoring.']
  gateway_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this payment instrument. Used for gateway-specific operations and reconciliation.']
  gift_card_balance decimal(10,2) [note: 'Remaining balance on the gift card or prepaid card. Updated after each transaction. Null for non-gift-card payment methods.']
  gift_card_code varchar [note: 'Masked gift card or prepaid card code. Full code is tokenized per PCI DSS requirements.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the default payment instrument for the subscriber. Used for automatic billing and subscription renewals.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful transaction using this payment instrument. Used for activity tracking and dormancy detection.']
  mandate_acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the subscriber accepted the direct debit mandate. Required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution.']
  mandate_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the direct debit mandate or authorization. Required for SEPA and other direct debit payment methods to comply with banking regulations.']
  masked_account_number varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the card or account number showing only the last 4 digits (e.g., ****1234). Full number is tokenized and stored securely per PCI DSS requirements.']
  payment_gateway varchar [note: 'Name of the payment gateway or processor used for this payment instrument (e.g., Stripe, Braintree, Authorize.Net, Zuora Gateway).']
  payment_instrument_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment instrument. Active instruments can be charged; expired or suspended instruments require update or replacement.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument used for subscription billing. Indicates the payment mechanism (credit card, debit card, PayPal, direct debit, bank transfer, gift card, or MVPD billing).']
  payment_token varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliant tokenization reference for the actual payment instrument details. Used to process transactions without exposing sensitive cardholder data.']
  paypal_email varchar [note: 'Email address associated with the PayPal account. Used for PayPal payment method identification and processing.']
  paypal_payer_reference varchar [note: 'Unique PayPal payer identifier for the linked PayPal account. Used for PayPal billing agreement reference.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the payment instrument was successfully verified. Used for compliance tracking and fraud prevention.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment instrument verification process. Indicates whether the payment method has been successfully validated through AVS, CVV check, or micro-deposit verification.']

  Note: 'Subscriber\'s stored payment instruments used for subscription billing. Captures payment method type (credit card, debit card, PayPal, direct debit, gift card, MVPD billing), masked card/account number, card brand, expiry date, billing address, is_default flag, and PCI DSS tokenization reference. Managed in Zuora as the payment method entity. Distinct from actual payment transactions owned by the billing domain.'
}

Table subscriber.offer_redemption {
  offer_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offer redemption transaction. Primary key for the offer redemption record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign under which this offer was promoted. Enables campaign-level performance tracking and Return on Investment (ROI) analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotional offers are tracked against marketing cost centers for campaign ROI analysis, budget accountability, and variance reporting. Finance allocates promotional discounts to responsible cost cent']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Promotional discount invoice traceability: offer redemptions (free trials, promotional rates) produce invoice adjustments that must be traceable for revenue impact reporting, marketing ROI analysis, a']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Offer redemptions (promotional discounts) generate revenue adjustments that must be attributed to a legal entity for ASC 606/IFRS 15 discount accounting and statutory reporting. In multi-territory med']
  offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional offer or discount campaign that was redeemed. Links to the offer master record.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution or retail partner through which the offer was distributed, if applicable. Null for direct-to-consumer offers.']
  qualifying_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Promotional offers often require viewing or purchasing specific titles (e.g., "watch premiere to unlock discount"). Supports campaign attribution, content-driven acquisition, and partnership promotion']
  resulting_subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan that the subscriber enrolled in or upgraded to as a result of this offer redemption. Links to the subscription plan master record.']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Promotional offers affect revenue stream classification and promotional revenue tracking for financial reporting. Finance separates promotional revenue from standard pricing for margin analysis and re']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who redeemed the promotional offer. Links to the subscriber master record.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific subscription instance that was created or modified as a result of this offer redemption. Links to the subscription record.']
  applied_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied to the subscription as a result of this redemption. Expressed in the subscription currency.']
  attribution_source varchar [note: 'The marketing source or touchpoint that led to this offer redemption. Examples include email campaign, social media ad, affiliate link, or organic search. Used for marketing attribution analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  discount_duration_months varchar [note: 'The number of months for which the discount will be applied to the subscription. Null for one-time discounts or permanent pricing changes.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied when discount_type is percentage. Null for non-percentage discount types. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 25.00 for 25% off).']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The type of discount mechanism applied through this offer redemption. Determines how the discount value is calculated and applied to the subscription.']
  eligibility_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the redemption was rejected or marked ineligible. Null if redemption was successful. Used for customer service and offer optimization.']
  eligibility_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscribers eligibility for the offer was verified at the time of redemption. True if eligibility checks passed, False otherwise.']
  first_time_subscriber_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption resulted in a new subscriber acquisition (True) or was redeemed by an existing subscriber (False). Critical for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) and Lifetime Value (LTV) analysis.']
  offer_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the promotional offer expires and can no longer be redeemed. Used to validate redemption eligibility and track expired redemption attempts.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The platform or channel through which the subscriber redeemed the offer. Enables channel effectiveness analysis for promotional campaigns.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'The unique promotional code or voucher code entered by the subscriber at the time of redemption. Used for tracking and validation.']
  redemption_device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the subscriber to redeem the offer. Supports device-specific campaign optimization and user experience analysis.']
  redemption_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the redemption request originated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered Personally Identifiable Information (PII) under General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer redemption. Tracks whether the redemption was successfully applied, expired before use, reversed due to policy violation, or rejected due to eligibility failure.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the subscriber successfully redeemed the promotional offer. Represents the business event time of redemption.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated impact on revenue resulting from this offer redemption. Negative values represent revenue reduction due to discounts; positive values may represent incremental revenue from new subscriptions or upgrades.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the offer redemption was reversed. Common reasons include fraud detection, policy violation, subscriber request, or billing dispute. Null if not reversed.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the offer redemption was reversed or cancelled. Null if the redemption remains active. Used for chargeback and refund reconciliation.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscriber explicitly accepted the terms and conditions associated with the promotional offer at the time of redemption. Required for compliance and dispute resolution.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the offer terms and conditions that the subscriber agreed to at redemption. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc. Used for legal audit trails.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this offer redemption record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  zuora_subscription_number varchar [note: 'The subscription identifier in the Zuora billing system that was created or modified as a result of this offer redemption. Used for billing reconciliation and revenue recognition.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a subscriber redeeming a promotional offer or discount code. Captures subscriber reference, offer reference, redemption timestamp, redemption channel, applied discount amount, resulting subscription plan, and redemption status (applied, expired, reversed). Enables promotion effectiveness tracking and revenue impact reconciliation.'
}

  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: Campaign-driven offer authorization: media broadcasters create promotional offers (trials, discounts) funded and authorized by specific sales campaigns. Offer management teams must trace which campaig']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotional offers have marketing and acquisition costs charged to specific cost centers. Finance tracks offer-related spend by cost center for campaign ROI analysis and budget management. Media broad']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Demographic-targeted offer design: promotional offers (student discounts, senior plans, family bundles) are designed against specific Nielsen demographic cells. Offer teams reference demographic_segme']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Partner-specific promotional offers (e.g., MVPD bundle free trials, telco co-marketing promotions) are created under and governed by distribution agreements. Finance and marketing teams attribute offe']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Promotional offers are platform-specific — a free trial or discount offer is tied to a specific OTT platform\'s service tier. Offer management, campaign targeting, redemption tracking, and platform-lev']
  package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.package. Business justification: Promotional offers are configured against specific content packages (e.g., \'Subscribe and unlock the Premium Sports Package\'). This FK supports offer configuration, marketing campaign setup, and entit']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Promotional offers must comply with advertising regulations, truth-in-advertising laws, consumer protection rules, and jurisdiction-specific promotional restrictions. Essential for legal review workfl']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Offers define pricing and discount structures whose revenue impact must be classified into a specific revenue stream for accounting. Finance teams in media broadcasting classify promotional revenue se']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription_plan. Business justification: An offer in a media streaming context is almost always tied to a specific subscription plan (e.g., \'30-day free trial on the Premium plan\', \'50% off the Standard tier for 3 months\'). Adding subscripti']
  superseded_offer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on offer (superseded_offer_id)']
  ad_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer includes advertising (AVOD model) or is ad-free.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription automatically renews at the end of the billing cycle.']

  content_quality varchar [note: 'Maximum video quality resolution available under this offer (Standard Definition, High Definition, 4K Ultra HD, 8K).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this offer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the offer price.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed discount amount applied to the standard price for promotional offers. Nullable for non-promotional offers.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the standard price for promotional offers. Nullable for non-promotional offers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this offer expires and is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for evergreen offers.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this offer becomes available for subscriber enrollment.']
  geographic_availability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this offer is available.']
  max_devices varchar [note: 'Maximum number of devices that can be registered under this offer.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the offer, used in marketing campaigns and promotional materials.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the offer including benefits, features, and terms presented to subscribers.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the offer displayed to subscribers and in marketing materials.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer indicating its availability and operational state.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the offer based on its business purpose and target audience.']
  offline_download_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscribers can download content for offline viewing under this offer.']
