  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Digital engagement events are classified by Nielsen demographic segments for audience measurement integration. The nielsen_demo_code should be replaced with FK to demographic_segment for consistency w']
  device_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.device_type. Business justification: Engagement events must reference device_type for QoS-correlated audience measurement and device-level content performance analysis. The device_type plain-text column denormalizes device_type. Cross-pl']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the distribution platform (e.g., OTT app, SVOD service, AVOD channel, FAST channel) on which the engagement event occurred. Enables multi-platform distribution and audience analytics.']
  playback_session_id varchar [note: 'Anonymized identifier for the streaming session during which this engagement event occurred. Links all events within a single continuous viewing session for session-level analytics. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform session context.']
  production_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_episode. Business justification: Episode-level engagement tracking identifies drop-off points, rewatch behavior, and scene-level audience response. Used for post-production feedback, marketing clip selection, and editorial pacing ana']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Engagement events (play, pause, seek, rewind) are tracked per production project for content optimization, A/B testing, and editorial feedback. Essential for streaming platform analytics and content d']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Engagement events drive monetization under specific revenue streams (programmatic advertising, sponsorship). Ad tech platforms link engagement metrics to revenue streams for yield optimization and rev']
  schedule_slot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.schedule_slot. Business justification: Linear engagement events (tune-in, tune-out, ad interactions) must link to schedule slots for slot-level engagement analysis, ad effectiveness measurement, and audience flow tracking. Creates schedule']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.audience_segment. Business justification: Engagement events are tagged with custom audience segments for behavioral targeting and activation. The audience_segment_code should be replaced with FK to audience_segment master for consistency and ']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the content asset (programme, episode, clip, or ad) against which this engagement event was recorded. Enables content-level audience behaviour analysis for programming decisions.']
  viewer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Anonymized identifier for the audience member or household profile that generated this engagement event. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform unified profile. Used for audience segmentation and ad targeting without exposing personal identity.']
  ad_pod_position varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the advertisement within its ad pod (group of ads in a break) at the time of the engagement event. Populated only when is_ad_event is True. Used to analyse viewer drop-off rates by pod position and optimise ad pod sequencing for revenue assurance.']
  app_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the OTT/streaming application that captured the engagement event (e.g., 4.2.1). Used to correlate engagement behaviour with app release cycles and identify version-specific anomalies in audience interaction patterns.']
  bitrate_kbps varchar [note: 'Actual bitrate in kilobits per second of the stream being delivered at the time of the engagement event. Sourced from Akamai CDN performance telemetry. Used alongside stream_quality to assess CDN delivery performance and its impact on audience engagement.']
  blackout_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a geographic broadcast restriction (blackout) was enforced for this viewer at the time of the engagement event (True) or not (False). Supports FCC blackout compliance reporting and rights management audit trails.']
  buffering_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Total duration of buffering experienced by the viewer immediately preceding or during this engagement event, in milliseconds. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform quality-of-experience telemetry. Used to correlate buffering events with pause and abandonment engagement actions.']
  content_duration_ms varchar [note: 'Total duration of the content asset in milliseconds at the time of the engagement event. Used in conjunction with content_position_ms to compute completion percentage and engagement depth score. Sourced from Dalet Galaxy asset metadata.']
  content_genre varchar [note: 'Primary genre classification of the content asset associated with this engagement event (e.g., Drama, News, Sports, Comedy, Documentary). Sourced from Dalet Galaxy asset metadata. Used for genre-level audience behaviour analysis and programming acquisition decisions.']
  content_position_ms varchar [note: 'Playback position within the content asset at the moment the engagement event was triggered, expressed in milliseconds from the start of the content. Core EVENT_PAYLOAD_OR_VALUE for this event log. Enables precise engagement depth analysis, completion rate calculation, and identification of drop-off points.']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Motion Picture Association (MPA) or TV Parental Guidelines content rating of the asset at the time of the engagement event. Used for COPPA compliance (children\'s content restrictions), audience demographic validation, and parental control enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: G|PG|PG-13|R|NC-17|TV-Y|TV-Y7|TV-G|TV-PG|TV-14|TV-MA — promote to reference product]']
  data_privacy_consent varchar [note: 'Viewer\'s data privacy consent status at the time of the engagement event, governing permissible use of this record for analytics, personalisation, and ad targeting. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform consent management. Mandatory for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis determination and CCPA opt-out compliance.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Broadcast daypart time segment in which the engagement event occurred, classified per industry-standard daypart definitions. Used for audience measurement reporting, ad sales daypart targeting, and programming scheduling decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime|late_news|late_fringe|overnight — promote to reference product]']
  device_os varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device that generated the engagement event (e.g., iOS, Android, tvOS, Roku OS, Fire OS, Windows, macOS). Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform device context. Supports technical platform compatibility analysis and audience reach reporting.']
  drm_scheme varchar [note: 'DRM (Digital Rights Management) protection scheme active on the content stream at the time of the engagement event. Used for rights compliance verification and to ensure content is consumed within licensed windows and territories. Supports Rightsline rights enforcement reporting.']
  engagement_depth_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Normalised score (0.00–100.00) representing the depth of audience engagement at the time of the event, calculated by Adobe Experience Platform based on playback position relative to content duration, interaction frequency, and event type weighting. Key metric for non-linear audience behaviour analysis and ad targeting propensity modelling.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Processing lifecycle status of the engagement event record in the Silver Layer. \'valid\' indicates the event passed all quality checks; \'invalid\' indicates a data quality failure; \'duplicate\' indicates a repeated event suppressed from analytics; \'quarantined\' indicates the event is held pending investigation.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise UTC timestamp recording when the engagement action occurred on the viewer\'s device. Principal EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this event log entity. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for time-series audience behaviour analysis, daypart analytics, and sweeps period reporting.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator identifying the specific audience interaction captured: play (content start/resume), pause (playback halted), seek (position jump forward), rewind (position jump backward), share (social distribution action), like (positive sentiment signal), comment (text interaction), complete (full content consumption). Core EVENT_TYPE_OR_CHANNEL field for non-linear audience behaviour analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: play|pause|seek|rewind|share|like|comment|complete — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the viewer\'s geographic location at the time of the engagement event, derived from IP geolocation. Used for territorial rights compliance (blackout enforcement, holdback windows), audience reach reporting by geography, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  geo_region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (state, province, or region) of the viewer\'s location at the time of the engagement event. Used for sub-national blackout enforcement, regional audience measurement, and daypart-adjusted analytics.']
  is_ad_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement event occurred during an advertisement (True) or during editorial content (False). Critical for separating content engagement metrics from ad engagement metrics in AVOD and DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) analytics.']
  is_concurrent_stream boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement event occurred on a stream that was running concurrently with another active stream on the same viewer account (True) or not (False). Used for concurrent stream limit enforcement per subscription terms and HUT (Homes Using Television) measurement.']
  is_sweeps_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engagement event occurred during a Nielsen sweeps measurement period (True) or outside of it (False). Sweeps periods are intensive ratings measurement windows critical for ad sales pricing and programming strategy. Enables sweeps vs. non-sweeps audience behaviour comparison.']
  isan_code varchar [note: 'ISAN (International Standard Audiovisual Number) of the content asset associated with this engagement event. Provides a globally unique, persistent identifier for the audiovisual work independent of platform or distribution channel. Supports rights management, royalty calculation, and regulatory reporting.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'UTC timestamp when the engagement event was received and ingested by Adobe Experience Platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (client-side occurrence time). Used to measure event latency, detect clock skew, and support data quality auditing in the Silver Layer pipeline.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Channel or mechanism through which the viewer arrived at the content that triggered this engagement event. Used for audience acquisition attribution, marketing campaign effectiveness analysis, and content discovery optimisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|search|social|email|push_notification|in_app|external_link — promote to reference product]']
  seek_from_position_ms varchar [note: 'Playback position in milliseconds from which the viewer initiated a seek or rewind action. Populated only when event_type is \'seek\' or \'rewind\'. Combined with content_position_ms (seek-to position) to measure skip behaviour and identify content segments that audiences skip or revisit.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'Original event identifier assigned by Adobe Experience Platform at the point of capture, before Silver Layer surrogate key assignment. Used for data lineage tracing, deduplication, and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the Adobe Experience Platform source system.']
  stream_quality varchar [note: 'Adaptive bitrate (ABR) quality tier of the stream at the time of the engagement event, reflecting the HLS or MPEG-DASH rendition being delivered by the CDN. Used to correlate stream quality with engagement behaviour and identify quality-driven churn signals.']
  subscription_tier varchar [note: 'Subscription or access tier of the viewer at the time of the engagement event, reflecting the monetisation model under which content was consumed. Sourced from Zuora subscription billing. Used to segment engagement behaviour by revenue tier for ARPU analysis and churn prediction.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a discrete digital audience engagement action on OTT/streaming platforms — including play, pause, seek, rewind, share, like, comment, and content completion events. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform. Captures event type, content identifier, viewer session identifier (anonymized), platform, device, timestamp, and engagement depth score. Enables non-linear audience behavior analysis for programming and ad targeting.'
}

Table audience.guarantee {
  guarantee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the audience delivery guarantee record. Primary key for the audience_guarantee data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser (party) to whom the audience delivery guarantee is committed. Sourced from Salesforce Media Cloud account records.']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Audience guarantees for broadcast advertising must reference the station license for regulatory compliance (political advertising lowest unit charge, equal opportunities, public file disclosure). Guar']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign for which this audience delivery guarantee is established. Links to the campaign record in Salesforce Media Cloud or Wide Orbit.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Audience guarantees create financial liabilities tracked in specific GL accounts for accrual accounting. Finance requires direct GL account reference for makegood reserve calculations and financial st']
  compliance_content_rating_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.content_rating. Business justification: Guarantee fulfillment must account for content rating restrictions affecting deliverable inventory. Advertisers specify rating-appropriate placements; guarantees calculate delivery against compliant i']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.talent_contract. Business justification: Upfront sales negotiations frequently tie audience guarantees to specific talent participation (e.g., guaranteed GRPs for episodes featuring marquee talent). Sales teams negotiate rate premiums and de']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Makegoods incur costs (production, airtime, inventory) that must be allocated to sales operations cost centers. Finance tracks guarantee fulfillment costs separately from original campaign costs for v']
  delivery_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: Audience guarantees specify the delivery channel for audience commitment tracking and makegood calculation. Distribution and ad sales operations require channel-level guarantee attribution to reconcil']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Guarantees target specific standardized demographic segments (e.g., A18-49, W25-54). The target_demographic_code and target_demographic_description are redundant with demographic_segment reference dat']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Each guarantee creates GL entries for revenue recognition and makegood liability accrual. Finance links guarantees to GL for audit trail, SOX compliance, and reconciliation of sold vs delivered audien']
  guarantee_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Audience guarantees are often negotiated at the network/channel level for upfront deals. Direct channel link enables automated guarantee reconciliation, makegood issuance by channel, and channel-speci']
  guarantee_network_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the television network or channel on which the guaranteed audience delivery is to be achieved. Links to the network/channel master record used in scheduling and playout systems.']
  ott_platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.ott_platform. Business justification: Audience guarantees are negotiated per OTT platform as streaming becomes a primary ad delivery vehicle. The platform_type plain-text column denormalizes ott_platform. Ad sales teams sell guaranteed GR']
  panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.panel. Business justification: An audience delivery guarantee is measured and reconciled against a specific Nielsen panel. The guaranteed_grp, guaranteed_trp, and actual_grp_delivered values are all panel-specific metrics — their v']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Audience guarantees are contractual commitments made to specific partners (advertisers, agencies, networks). Tracking the partner receiving the guarantee is essential for makegood fulfillment and cont']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Upfront audience guarantees specify which production projects/series will deliver promised ratings. Required for makegood reconciliation, advertiser delivery reports, and sales contract fulfillment. C']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Guarantees are sold by profit centers and impact their P&L through revenue recognition and makegood costs. Finance tracks guarantee performance by profit center for business unit profitability reporti']
  program_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.program_schedule. Business justification: Audience guarantees are tied to specific program schedules — the schedule defines the inventory against which GRP delivery is measured and reconciled. Guarantee reconciliation and makegood workflows r']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_proposal. Business justification: Audience guarantees are negotiated and documented in sales proposals before being formalized in orders. Proposals specify guarantee terms (GRP/TRP thresholds, makegood provisions) that become contract']
  reach_frequency_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.reach_frequency_report. Business justification: A guarantee is reconciled against a reach_frequency_report that validates whether the committed audience delivery was achieved. The reach_frequency_report contains the actual reach_pct, average_freque']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Guarantees must track compliance with advertising standards, political advertising disclosure rules, and FCC makegood requirements. Guarantee has political_ad_record_id and makegood_trigger_condition ']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Audience guarantees define revenue recognition terms and makegood liability accounting. ASC 606 compliance requires linking performance obligations (guarantees) to revenue streams for proper recogniti']
  salesforce_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'The opportunity identifier in Salesforce Media Cloud CRM linked to this audience guarantee. Enables traceability from the sales pipeline through to audience delivery commitment and reconciliation.']
  scheduling_daypart_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.daypart. Business justification: Upfront guarantees specify daypart-level GRP/TRP commitments (e.g., Prime 18-49 rating guarantee). Direct daypart link enables automated delivery tracking, makegood triggering, and daypart-specific re']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Audience guarantees (GRP/TRP commitments to advertisers) are made against specific programs/titles. Advertisers buy guarantees tied to named shows; post-campaign reconciliation and makegood issuance r']
  actual_grp_delivered decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual total GRP delivered against this guarantee as measured by the designated audience measurement source (e.g., Nielsen). Populated during and after the measurement period through reconciliation with actual delivery records. Used to determine whether the guarantee was met and whether makegoods are required.']
  actual_trp_delivered decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual total TRP delivered against this guarantee within the target demographic, as measured by the designated audience measurement source. Populated during reconciliation. Used alongside actual_grp_delivered to assess guarantee fulfillment for demographic-specific commitments.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audience guarantee was formally approved and activated, transitioning from draft to active status. Represents the binding commitment date for revenue recognition and contractual obligation purposes.']
  cprp decimal(12,2) [note: 'The negotiated cost per rating point (CPRP) for this audience guarantee, expressed in the contract currency. CPRP is the standard pricing metric in broadcast advertising used to calculate the total value of the guarantee and to assess makegood compensation amounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audience guarantee record was first created in the system. Audit trail field used for data lineage, compliance reporting, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial values associated with this audience guarantee (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used in CPRP and total guarantee value calculations.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the advertising deal under which this guarantee was established. Upfront refers to advance advertising sales events; scatter refers to last-minute ad inventory sales; direct, programmatic, and sponsorship represent other deal structures common in media broadcasting.']
  delivery_shortfall_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'The calculated difference between the guaranteed GRP level and the actual GRP delivered (guaranteed_grp minus actual_grp_delivered). A positive value indicates under-delivery and potential makegood obligation. Populated during reconciliation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the audience delivery guarantee measurement period ends. Nullable for open-ended or rolling guarantees. Defines the close of the window during which GRP/TRP delivery is tracked.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the audience delivery guarantee measurement period begins. Defines the start of the window during which GRP/TRP delivery is tracked against the committed level.']
  guarantee_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the audience guarantee, used in advertiser-facing documents, contracts, and reconciliation reports. Typically referenced in Wide Orbit sales orders and Salesforce Media Cloud opportunities.']
  guarantee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audience guarantee. Draft indicates the guarantee is being negotiated; active indicates it is in-flight; at_risk indicates delivery is trending below threshold; breached indicates the guarantee was not met and makegoods are triggered; fulfilled indicates full delivery was achieved; cancelled indicates the guarantee was voided.']
  guaranteed_frequency decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum average number of times a member of the target audience is exposed to the campaign (frequency) committed to the advertiser. Frequency is the average exposures per viewer. Used alongside reach guarantees for campaigns with specific exposure objectives.']
  guaranteed_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total Gross Rating Point (GRP) level committed to the advertiser for delivery over the measurement period. GRP is the sum of ratings for all ad spots in the campaign, representing total audience exposure as a percentage of the target universe. This is the contractually obligated delivery level.']
  guaranteed_reach decimal(15,0) [note: 'The minimum number of unique audience members (reach) committed to the advertiser over the measurement period. Reach is the count of distinct individuals exposed to the campaign at least once. Complements GRP/TRP guarantees for campaigns focused on unique audience coverage.']
  guaranteed_trp decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total Target Rating Point (TRP) level committed to the advertiser, measured specifically against the defined target demographic segment. TRP is the GRP filtered to the target audience. Used when the guarantee is demographic-specific rather than total audience.']
  is_sweeps_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guarantee measurement period falls within a Nielsen sweeps period (intensive ratings measurement period). Sweeps periods (February, May, July, November) carry higher audience measurement scrutiny and may affect guarantee valuation and pricing.']
  makegood_deadline_date date [note: 'The contractual deadline by which makegood spots must be aired to compensate for any audience delivery shortfall. Critical for revenue assurance and advertiser relationship management.']
  makegood_grp_issued decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total GRP value of makegood (compensatory ad spots) issued to the advertiser to compensate for audience delivery shortfall. Tracked for revenue assurance and advertiser relationship management. Zero if no makegoods were required.']
  makegood_trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific conditions under which makegood (compensatory ad spots) obligations are activated. Defines the contractual rules for when and how makegoods must be provided to the advertiser when guaranteed audience delivery is not met. Sourced from the advertising contract terms.']
  nielsen_order_code varchar [note: 'The external order or tracking identifier assigned by the Nielsen Media Research Platform for the audience measurement order associated with this guarantee. Used to cross-reference Nielsen ratings data and reconciliation reports.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this audience guarantee. May include negotiation history, special advertiser requirements, or reconciliation commentary.']
  rating_metric_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the primary audience measurement metric on which this guarantee is based. GRP (Gross Rating Point) measures total audience exposure; TRP (Target Rating Point) measures exposure within the target demographic; HUT (Homes Using Television) and PUT (Persons Using Television) measure set usage; reach measures unique audience count; frequency measures average exposures per viewer.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation process comparing guaranteed audience delivery against actual GRP/TRP delivery records. Pending indicates reconciliation has not started; in_progress indicates active comparison; reconciled indicates agreement reached; disputed indicates advertiser has contested the delivery figures; closed indicates the reconciliation cycle is complete.']
  tolerance_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The acceptable percentage shortfall below the guaranteed GRP/TRP level before a makegood obligation is triggered. For example, a value of 5.00 means delivery must be within 5% of the guaranteed level. Defined in the advertising contract and used in reconciliation against actual delivery records.']
  total_guarantee_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total contracted monetary value of this audience guarantee, calculated as guaranteed GRP/TRP multiplied by CPRP. Represents the revenue at risk if the guarantee is not met and is central to revenue assurance and financial reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audience guarantee record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data processing in the lakehouse Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  wide_orbit_order_code varchar [note: 'The sales order identifier in the Wide Orbit Traffic and Billing system associated with this audience guarantee. Used for cross-system reconciliation between audience delivery records and ad scheduling/invoicing.']

  Note: 'Master record of the audience delivery guarantee committed to an advertiser for a specific campaign or upfront deal — specifying the guaranteed GRP/TRP level, target demographic, measurement period, tolerance threshold, and makegood trigger conditions. Links to ad sales contracts and is reconciled against actual GRP delivery records. Central to revenue assurance and advertiser relationship management.'
}

Table audience.audience_profile {
  audience_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the audience profile record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: An audience_profile represents an anonymized audience member with inferred demographic attributes (inferred_age_band, inferred_gender, income_band). Adding demographic_segment_id links the profile to ']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Audience profiles are associated with specific DMAs for geographic targeting and measurement. The dma_code should be replaced with FK to market_coverage for consistency with other products. Country_co']
  nielsen_panel_id varchar [note: 'Pseudonymized identifier assigned by Nielsen Media Research to link this audience profile to a Nielsen panel household or individual. Enables direct integration of Nielsen ratings, GRP/TRP, and demographic data. Null for non-panel profiles.']
  ad_receptivity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Propensity score (0.00–100.00) indicating this audience member\'s likelihood to engage with advertising content, derived from behavioral signals in Adobe AEP and viewing pattern analysis. Used by ad sales teams for premium audience packaging and CPM pricing optimization.']
  aep_profile_code varchar [note: 'Unique profile identifier from Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) used to link behavioral engagement data, segment memberships, and journey analytics to this audience profile. Enables personalization and digital engagement enrichment.']
  avg_session_duration_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average duration in minutes of a single viewing session for this audience member, derived from set-top box or streaming platform telemetry. Represents Time Spent Viewing (TSV) at the individual level. Used for engagement scoring and content performance analytics.']
  ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this California-resident audience member has exercised their CCPA right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. When TRUE, this profile must be excluded from data sharing with third-party ad networks and data clean rooms.']
  consent_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to any consent or privacy flag (GDPR consent status, CCPA opt-out, COPPA flag) for this audience profile. Critical for regulatory audit trails demonstrating timely consent management and data processing compliance.']
  content_affinity_genre varchar [note: 'Top inferred content genre affinity for this audience member (e.g., \'news\', \'sports\', \'drama\', \'reality\', \'documentary\'), derived from viewing history and behavioral signals in Adobe AEP. Used for content recommendation, programming decisions, and audience-targeted ad sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: news|sports|drama|comedy|reality|documentary|entertainment|kids|lifestyle|sci_fi — promote to reference product]']
  coppa_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audience profile is subject to COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) protections, meaning the inferred or declared age is under 13. When TRUE, ad targeting, data sharing, and behavioral tracking are strictly restricted per COPPA compliance requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the audience member\'s country of residence. Used for rights windowing, content holdback enforcement, GDPR/CCPA jurisdictional compliance, and international distribution analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience profile record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse, either through initial identity resolution, panel enrollment, or first-party data ingestion. Audit trail for data lineage and GDPR data minimization compliance.']
  data_clean_room_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audience profile is eligible for inclusion in data clean room matching operations with advertising partners. Eligibility is determined by consent status, CCPA opt-out flag, COPPA protection status, and contractual data sharing agreements.']
  daypart_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred daypart (time segment of the broadcast day) during which this audience member most frequently views content. Dayparts are standard broadcast industry time segments used for ad scheduling, upfront sales, and audience guarantee reporting.']
  device_graph_code varchar [note: 'Cross-device graph linkage identifier that associates this audience profile with a cluster of known devices (smart TV, mobile, desktop, STB). Sourced from identity resolution providers or Adobe AEP device graph. Enables multi-screen reach and frequency measurement.']
  enrichment_source_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the third-party enrichment data package (e.g., Nielsen demographic file version, Adobe AEP segment refresh batch ID) used to populate inferred attributes on this profile. Enables data lineage tracing and reproducibility of audience segment definitions.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current GDPR consent status for processing this audience member\'s personal data for audience measurement and advertising purposes. \'granted\' permits processing; \'denied\' or \'withdrawn\' restricts processing; \'not_applicable\' for non-EU audience members. Drives data processing eligibility.']
  household_size varchar [note: 'Estimated number of persons in the audience household, derived from Nielsen panel data or set-top box signals. Used in HUT (Homes Using Television) and PUT (Persons Using Television) calculations for audience measurement reporting.']
  hut_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this household-level audience profile is eligible to be counted in HUT (Homes Using Television) calculations. HUT measures the percentage of TV-equipped homes actively watching television during a given time period, a foundational metric for broadcast ratings.']
  identity_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0.00–100.00) representing the reliability of the identity resolution that constructed this audience profile. Higher scores indicate stronger deterministic matching (e.g., authenticated login); lower scores indicate probabilistic device-graph inference. Used to qualify profiles for premium ad targeting.']
  identity_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 pseudonymized hash of the audience member\'s primary identity (e.g., email or subscriber ID) used for cross-platform identity resolution without exposing raw PII. Enables privacy-safe linkage across Nielsen, Adobe AEP, and first-party data sources.']
  identity_source varchar [note: 'Originating source of the audience identity construction: \'first_party\' (subscriber or app registration data), \'nielsen\' (Nielsen Media Research panel linkage), \'adobe_aep\' (Adobe Experience Platform profile), \'set_top_box\' (STB device-level data), \'clean_room\' (data clean room match), or \'modeled\' (probabilistic inference).']
  income_band varchar [note: 'Inferred household income band derived from third-party demographic enrichment (Nielsen, data clean rooms). Used for premium audience segmentation in upfront and scatter market ad sales, enabling CPM pricing differentiation by audience affluence.']
  inferred_age_band varchar [note: 'Inferred age demographic band for the audience member, derived from Nielsen panel data, set-top box signals, or modeled enrichment. Used for GRP/TRP demographic targeting and ad sales audience guarantees. Not directly collected from the individual.']
  inferred_gender varchar [note: 'Inferred gender of the audience member derived from panel data or modeled signals. Used for demographic audience targeting in ad sales (GRP/TRP calculations) and programming decisions. Not directly collected.']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent observed audience activity event (viewing session, app interaction, or platform login) for this profile. Used to determine audience recency, drive re-engagement campaigns, and calculate viewing recency metrics.']
  nielsen_grp_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Indexed GRP (Gross Rating Point) score for this audience profile relative to the total population, sourced from Nielsen Media Research. A GRP index above 100 indicates above-average exposure to rated content. Used in ad sales audience guarantees and upfront negotiations.']
  panel_weight_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Statistical weighting factor assigned by Nielsen to this panel member profile to project panel observations to the total population. Applied in GRP, TRP, HUT, and PUT calculations. Null for non-panel (modeled or first-party only) profiles.']
  platform_preference varchar [note: 'Primary viewing platform preference inferred from behavioral signals: \'linear_tv\' (traditional scheduled broadcast), \'svod\' (Subscription Video On Demand), \'avod\' (Advertising-Supported VOD), \'tvod\' (Transactional VOD), \'fast\' (Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV), \'ott\' (Over-The-Top), \'mvpd\' (Multichannel Video Programming Distributor), or \'vmvpd\' (Virtual MVPD). Drives distribution strategy and ad product targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|svod|avod|tvod|fast|ott|mvpd|vmvpd — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_device_type varchar [note: 'The primary device type through which this audience member most frequently consumes content, derived from device graph and behavioral data. Used for CDN delivery optimization, ad format targeting, and platform analytics.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audience profile. \'suppressed\' indicates a privacy opt-out or regulatory hold; \'pending_consent\' indicates consent collection is in progress; \'purged\' indicates the record has been anonymized per GDPR/CCPA erasure request.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audience profile record: \'individual\' (single person identity), \'household\' (aggregated household unit), \'panel_member\' (Nielsen or equivalent panel participant), or \'modeled\' (synthetically constructed from behavioral signals without direct identity linkage).']
  sweeps_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audience profile\'s measurement data was captured during a Nielsen Sweeps period (intensive ratings measurement period: February, May, July, November). Sweeps data carries higher statistical weight in ratings calculations and programming decisions.']
  trp_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'Indexed TRP (Target Rating Point) score representing this audience member\'s contribution to a specific demographic target group\'s rating. Distinct from GRP in that it measures reach within a defined target demographic. Used for demographic-specific ad campaign planning and post-campaign analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this audience profile record, triggered by enrichment refresh, consent status change, identity graph update, or behavioral signal ingestion. Used for data freshness monitoring and incremental processing.']
  viewing_recency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since this audience member last engaged with any content on any tracked platform. Used for audience recency segmentation, churn prediction, and re-engagement campaign targeting. Lower values indicate more recently active audiences.']
  weekly_viewing_frequency varchar [note: 'Average number of distinct viewing sessions per week for this audience member, calculated over a rolling 4-week window. Represents the Frequency dimension of audience measurement (average exposures per viewer). Used in reach and frequency reporting for ad campaign delivery.']

  Note: 'Anonymized/pseudonymized master record representing a unique audience member or household identity constructed from first-party data (subscriber records, app registrations, set-top box data) and third-party enrichment (Nielsen, Adobe AEP, data clean rooms). Captures hashed identity, device graph linkage, inferred demographic attributes, content affinity scores, platform preferences, viewing recency/frequency, and consent/privacy flags per GDPR, CCPA, and COPPA. Distinct from the subscriber SSOT in the subscriber domain — this is the audience intelligence and behavioral record, not the billing or subscription identity.'
}

Table audience.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the audience segment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master resource entity.']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: A segment (activation/targeting segment) is built from demographic, behavioral, and psychographic criteria. Adding demographic_segment_id links the activation segment to its Nielsen demographic_segmen']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Audience segments for targeted advertising must track GDPR consent basis, CCPA opt-out applicability, and children\'s privacy rules. Segment activation_status and dai_eligible flags require compliance ']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the segment governing whether it is available for use in ad targeting, campaign activation, or content personalization. \'draft\' indicates under construction; \'pending_review\' awaits compliance or data governance approval; \'active\' is live and deployable; \'inactive\' is suspended; \'archived\' is retired.']
  aep_segment_code varchar [note: 'The segment identifier as defined and managed within Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). Used for digital audience activation, personalization journeys, and cross-channel targeting. Enables synchronization between the internal audience_segment master record and the AEP segment definition for OTT and digital platforms.']
  age_range_max varchar [note: 'Maximum age (inclusive) of the primary demographic target for this segment, expressed in years. Used with age_range_min to define the Nielsen demographic cell. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., \'55+\' segments).']
  age_range_min varchar [note: 'Minimum age (inclusive) of the primary demographic target for this segment, expressed in years. Used in conjunction with age_range_max to define the Nielsen demographic cell (e.g., 25 for the \'Adults 25-54\' standard Nielsen demo). Null if the segment is not age-bounded.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the data governance or compliance approver who authorized this segment for activation, particularly for segments involving sensitive demographic data, COPPA-restricted audiences, or GDPR-regulated EU audiences. Supports audit trail requirements.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this segment received formal data governance or compliance approval for activation. Distinct from created_timestamp — captures the approval lifecycle event. Required for GDPR accountability documentation and internal audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  content_genre_affinity varchar [note: 'Primary content genre(s) this segment shows strong viewership affinity toward, used for programming strategy alignment and contextual ad targeting (e.g., \'drama,sports,news\'). Sourced from Nielsen viewing behavior data or Adobe AEP content interaction signals. Informs content acquisition decisions and ad pod placement strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audience segment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, governance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary data origin used to construct the segment. \'nielsen\' indicates Nielsen Media Research Platform ratings and demographic data; \'adobe_aep\' indicates Adobe Experience Platform behavioral profiles; \'first_party\' indicates proprietary subscriber or registration data; \'third_party\' indicates licensed external data; \'hybrid\' indicates a combination of multiple sources.']
  daypart_applicability varchar [note: 'Broadcast daypart(s) for which this segment is most relevant or has been validated, expressed as a comma-delimited list of standard daypart labels (e.g., \'primetime,late_night\'). Daypart is a standard media-buying time segment of the broadcast day used in ad scheduling and upfront sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|primetime|late_news|late_night|overnight — promote to reference product]']
  definition_logic varchar [note: 'The rule-based or SQL/JSON expression that defines segment membership criteria as implemented in Adobe AEP or Nielsen. Captures the inclusion/exclusion logic (e.g., age range, viewing frequency thresholds, content genre affinity, platform usage) used to qualify audience members into this segment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this segment definition is considered valid and available for business use. Segments may be pre-built ahead of upfront sales seasons or campaign launches. Enables temporal validity management for segment versioning and historical reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date after which this segment definition is no longer valid for new activations. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Used to manage seasonal segments (e.g., sweeps-period segments), campaign-specific segments, and segments tied to expiring data licensing agreements.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of unique individuals (persons or households) who qualify for this segment at the time of last refresh. Reach is a core Nielsen and IAB metric used in ad sales proposals, GRP/TRP calculations, and CPM pricing. Distinct from impressions — counts unique audience members, not exposures.']
  estimated_reach_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the estimated_reach figure. \'persons\' aligns with Nielsen PUT (Persons Using Television) methodology; \'households\' aligns with HUT (Homes Using Television); \'devices\' applies to digital/OTT platform segments measured by device-level identity.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Gender composition target for the segment as defined in Nielsen demographic cells and ad sales targeting parameters. \'all\' indicates gender-neutral targeting. Used in GRP (Gross Rating Point) and TRP (Target Rating Point) calculations for ad campaign planning.']
  grp_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'The GRP (Gross Rating Point) index value representing this segment\'s relative audience weight as a percentage of the total universe. GRP is a fundamental media planning metric equal to Reach × Frequency. Used in upfront and scatter market ad sales to price inventory and measure campaign delivery against this segment.']
  hut_put_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment\'s reach and rating metrics are calculated on a HUT (Homes Using Television) or PUT (Persons Using Television) basis. HUT is household-level measurement; PUT is person-level. This distinction governs how GRP/TRP values are interpreted and how the segment is applied in Nielsen-based ad sales planning.']
  income_bracket varchar [note: 'Household income tier associated with the segment\'s demographic composition, used in premium ad targeting and CPM (Cost Per Mille) pricing. Expressed as a standardized range label (e.g., \'$75K-$100K HHI\', \'$100K+ HHI\'). Sourced from Nielsen MRI-Simmons or first-party subscriber data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: under_25k|25k_50k|50k_75k|75k_100k|100k_150k|150k_plus — promote to reference product]']
  is_dai_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is approved and technically configured for use in DAI (Dynamic Ad Insertion) workflows on OTT and streaming platforms. DAI-eligible segments can be activated in real-time ad decisioning systems integrated with Akamai CDN and programmatic ad servers for addressable advertising.']
  is_sweeps_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment\'s reach and demographic composition estimates have been validated using Nielsen Sweeps period data (the intensive ratings measurement periods in February, May, July, and November). Sweeps-validated segments carry higher confidence for upfront ad sales commitments and programming decisions.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the segment membership, reach estimates, and demographic composition were last recalculated from source data. Used to assess data freshness for ad sales proposals and campaign activation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this audience segment record was last updated. Tracks changes to segment definition, status, reach estimates, or compliance flags. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail maintenance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  nielsen_segment_code varchar [note: 'The corresponding segment or audience cell identifier within the Nielsen Media Research Platform. Enables direct linkage to Nielsen ratings data, GRP/TRP delivery reports, and demographic reach/frequency outputs. Used for reconciliation between internal segment definitions and Nielsen-measured audience delivery.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, caveats, or operational notes about the segment that do not fit structured fields. Examples include data quality warnings, known limitations of the definition logic, special handling instructions for ad sales, or notes on upcoming segment retirement.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'The business team or department that owns and is accountable for this segment\'s definition, quality, and lifecycle management (e.g., \'Ad Sales Research\', \'Audience Analytics\', \'Programming Strategy\', \'Digital Marketing\'). Used for data governance, access control, and segment portfolio management.']
  platform_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of distribution platforms for which this segment is valid and activated (e.g., \'linear,ott,avod,svod,fast\'). Governs where the segment can be applied for ad targeting or content personalization across Linear, OTT (Over-The-Top), AVOD (Advertising-Supported Video On Demand), SVOD (Subscription Video On Demand), and FAST (Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television) channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|ott|avod|svod|tvod|fast|social|digital_display — promote to reference product]']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently the segment membership and reach estimates are recalculated and updated from source systems (Nielsen, Adobe AEP, or first-party data). Governs data freshness SLAs for ad targeting activation. \'real_time\' applies to DAI-eligible programmatic segments; \'daily\' is standard for digital; \'weekly/monthly\' for Nielsen-based linear segments.']
  segment_category varchar [note: 'Business-facing category grouping segments for portfolio management and reporting (e.g., \'Ad Targeting\', \'Programming Strategy\', \'Content Personalization\', \'Subscriber Retention\', \'Regulatory Reporting\'). Enables segment library organization in Salesforce Media Cloud and Adobe AEP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ad_targeting|programming_strategy|content_personalization|subscriber_retention|regulatory_reporting|audience_research — promote to reference product]']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the segment used across systems such as Adobe Experience Platform (AEP), Nielsen, and Salesforce Media Cloud. Serves as the canonical cross-system reference code (e.g., \'SEG-DEMO-F25-54-OTT\').']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment\'s business purpose, defining criteria, and intended use cases. Provides context for ad sales teams, programmers, and data analysts on what the segment represents and how it should be applied.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name for the audience segment as it appears in ad sales proposals, programming strategy decks, and campaign targeting UIs (e.g., \'Women 25-54 OTT Heavy Viewers\'). Corresponds to the segment label in Adobe AEP and Salesforce Media Cloud.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment by the primary methodology used to define it. Demographic segments are built on age/gender/HUT criteria; behavioral on viewing patterns; psychographic on interests/attitudes; contextual on content affinity; lookalike on modeled similarity to a seed audience.']
  trp_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'The TRP (Target Rating Point) index value representing this segment\'s rating within its specific target demographic universe. TRP is the targeted equivalent of GRP, measuring delivery efficiency against the defined demographic cell. Critical for ad sales pricing, makegoods calculations, and CPRP (Cost Per Rating Point) negotiations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version counter for the segment definition. Incremented each time the segment\'s definition logic, demographic criteria, or platform applicability is materially changed. Enables segment versioning for historical campaign attribution — ad campaigns reference the segment version active at time of booking.']

  Note: 'Master record for a defined, reusable audience segment built from demographic, behavioral, and psychographic criteria for use in ad targeting, programming strategy, and content personalization. Captures segment name, definition logic, estimated size, demographic composition, platform applicability, activation status, and data source (Nielsen, Adobe AEP, first-party). Distinct from demographic_segment (which is a reference classification) — this is an operationally managed, business-owned targeting segment.'
}

Table audience.segment_membership {
  segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this segment membership record']
  segment_audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audience profile that is a member of this segment']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the segment that this audience profile is a member of']
  segment_profile_audience_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the audience profile that is a member of this segment']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current activation state of this membership governing whether this profile-segment pairing is available for ad targeting and content personalization']
  aep_segment_membership varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) audience segment IDs to which this profile currently belongs. Segments represent behavioral, demographic, or propensity-based audience groups used for personalization, ad targeting, and journey activation. [Moved from audience_profile: This comma-delimited list of segment IDs on audience_profile is a denormalized representation of the many-to-many relationship. It should be replaced by properly normalized segment_membership records with full lifecycle attributes.]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was first created in the system']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience profile first qualified for and was assigned to this segment']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audience profile no longer qualified for or was removed from this segment. NULL indicates active membership.']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this membership was last evaluated against the segment definition logic to confirm continued qualification']
  membership_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence or affinity score (0.00-100.00) indicating how strongly this audience profile matches the segment definition criteria']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment membership record was last updated']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system or platform that computed and assigned this membership (e.g., Adobe AEP, Nielsen, internal segmentation engine)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the membership relationship between audience_profile and segment. It captures the operational assignment of individual audience profiles to targeting segments for ad delivery, content personalization, and campaign activation. Each record links one audience profile to one segment with membership lifecycle attributes, activation status, and scoring metadata that exist only in the context of this specific profile-segment assignment.. Existence Justification: In media broadcasting operations, audience segment membership is a core operational concept actively managed by audience intelligence and ad operations teams. A single audience profile simultaneously belongs to multiple segments (e.g., \'Sports Enthusiasts\', \'High Income HH\', \'Prime Time Viewers\'), and each segment contains thousands to millions of audience profiles. The business explicitly tracks membership lifecycle (when profiles enter/exit segments), membership scores (how strongly they match criteria), and activation status (whether the membership is available for ad targeting). This is evidenced by the aep_segment_membership attribute on audience_profile, which is a denormalized representation of this M:N relationship.'
}

Table talent.talent_profile {
  talent_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the talent profile record. Primary key for the talent master entity.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Talent profiles must map to employing legal entity for tax withholding jurisdiction, I-9 compliance, payroll processing, and work authorization tracking in multi-entity broadcasters.']
  talent_agency_id varchar [note: 'FK to talent.talent_agency']
  biometric_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent has provided consent for collection and use of biometric data (facial recognition, voice prints) for digital effects, deepfake prevention, and AI training. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent has opted out of the sale or sharing of their personal information under CCPA. Required for California-based talent or productions.']
  clearance_expiration_date date [note: 'The date when the talent\'s current clearance status expires and requires renewal. Used for compliance tracking and production eligibility verification.']
  clearance_status varchar [note: 'The current clearance status of the talent for production work. Indicates whether background checks, work authorization, insurance verification, and legal clearances are complete and current.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the talent profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The talent\'s date of birth. Required for age verification, child labor law compliance (COPPA), insurance underwriting, and residual payment calculations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'The primary email address for talent communication, contract delivery, and digital correspondence.']
  exclusivity_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is currently bound by an exclusivity clause that restricts their ability to work with competing networks, studios, or brands. Used for conflict checking during casting and booking.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the talent\'s GDPR consent for data processing. Tracks whether the talent has provided, withdrawn, or is pending consent for personal data usage under EU regulations.']
  gender_identity varchar [note: 'The talent\'s self-identified gender. Used for diversity reporting, casting analytics, and compliance with equal opportunity regulations.']
  imdb_identifier varchar [note: 'The talent\'s unique identifier in the Internet Movie Database (IMDb). Used for cross-referencing filmography, public profile linking, and industry data integration.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is currently covered by production insurance (liability, workers compensation, errors and omissions). Required for on-set work authorization.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number for the talent\'s production insurance coverage. Used for claims processing and certificate of insurance verification.']
  isni_code varchar [note: 'The International Standard Name Identifier (ISNI) for the talent. A globally unique identifier for creators used in rights management and royalty distribution systems.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the talent as it appears on official documents and contracts. Used for contract execution, payroll, and legal compliance.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'The talent\'s nationality represented as a 3-letter ISO country code. Used for work authorization, tax treaty eligibility, and international co-production compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special requirements, or contextual information about the talent. Used for casting notes, production preferences, and internal communication.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'The primary contact phone number for the talent. Used for production scheduling, emergency contact, and direct communication.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'The talent\'s primary working language represented as a 2-letter ISO language code. Used for casting, dubbing, and localization workflows.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the talent profile. Determines availability for casting, contract execution, and system access.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of their work in syndication, streaming, or international distribution. Determined by union status and contract terms.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'The professional or stage name used by the talent for public appearances, credits, and marketing materials. May differ from legal name.']
  talent_tier varchar [note: 'The classification tier of the talent based on industry recognition, box office draw, and compensation level. Used for budgeting, marketing strategy, and billing rate determination.']
  talent_type varchar [note: 'The primary professional category or role type of the talent. Determines applicable guild rules, contract templates, and credit attribution standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: actor|director|writer|producer|host|correspondent|crew|voice_artist|composer|cinematographer — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'The talent\'s tax identification number (SSN, EIN, or international equivalent). Required for payroll processing, tax withholding, and IRS 1099 reporting.']
  union_affiliation varchar [note: 'The primary labor union or guild affiliation of the talent. Determines contract terms, minimum compensation, residual eligibility, and working conditions.']
  union_member_number varchar [note: 'The talent\'s membership identifier within their primary union or guild. Required for residual payment processing and contract compliance verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the talent profile record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness verification.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'The talent\'s current work authorization status in the primary production jurisdiction. Determines eligibility for employment and production participation.']
  work_visa_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the talent\'s work visa. Critical for production scheduling and compliance with immigration regulations.']
  work_visa_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of work visa held by the talent (e.g., O-1, P-1, H-1B). Used for compliance tracking and production planning for international talent.']

  Note: 'Master identity record for all on-screen and off-screen talent — actors, directors, writers, producers, hosts, correspondents, crew, and voice artists. Stores legal name, stage name, date of birth, nationality, gender identity, primary language, union membership references, talent tier classification, representation agency references, IMDb/ISNI identifiers, biometric consent flags, data privacy status (GDPR/CCPA), clearance status, and active/inactive lifecycle state. Serves as the authoritative SSOT for talent identity referenced by production, rights, royalty, and scheduling workflows across the enterprise.'
}

Table talent.guild_affiliation {
  guild_affiliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guild affiliation record. Primary key.']
  cba_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to talent.cba_rate_card.   chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.chart_of_accounts. Business justification: Guild pension and health contributions require specific GL account mapping for regulatory remittance, union compliance reporting, and audit trail documentation mandated by CBAs.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Guild affiliations must comply with specific labor regulations governing union membership, dues collection, benefits eligibility, and collective bargaining agreement terms. Provides direct link to reg']
  talent_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the talent (actor, director, writer, producer, crew member) who holds this guild membership.']
  cba_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement became effective for this membership.']
  cba_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current collective bargaining agreement expires. Used to track contract renewal cycles and potential rate changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild affiliation record was first created in the system.']
  dues_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of membership dues payment obligations. Current (all dues paid up to date), overdue (payment past due but within grace period), delinquent (significantly past due, may affect standing), exempt (not required to pay dues), waived (dues forgiven by guild).']
  guild_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the entertainment industry guild or union. SAG-AFTRA (Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists), WGA (Writers Guild of America), DGA (Directors Guild of America), IATSE (International Alliance of Theatrical Stage Employees), AFM (American Federation of Musicians), BECTU (Broadcasting Entertainment Communications and Theatre Union - UK), ACTRA (Alliance of Canadian Cinema Television and Radio Artists). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAG-AFTRA|WGA|DGA|IATSE|AFM|BECTU|ACTRA|OTHER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  guild_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the guild or union organization.']
  health_benefits_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible for guild-provided health insurance benefits based on this membership and earnings thresholds. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  join_date date [note: 'Date when the talent officially became a member of this guild. Used to determine seniority and eligibility for certain benefits.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction or country where this guild membership is registered and governed. USA (United States), CAN (Canada), GBR (United Kingdom), AUS (Australia), NZL (New Zealand), IRL (Ireland). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|AUS|NZL|IRL|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_dues_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent membership dues payment received by the guild.']
  local_chapter varchar [note: 'Local chapter, branch, or regional division of the guild to which this membership is assigned. Used for regional governance and event coordination.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Unique membership identifier assigned by the guild to the talent member. Required for residual payment processing and production compliance verification.']
  membership_status varchar [note: 'Current standing of the talent within the guild. Good standing (active member in compliance), suspended (temporarily barred due to dues or violations), resigned (voluntarily terminated membership), expelled (involuntarily removed), inactive (not currently active but not terminated), pending (application under review).']
  membership_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of membership level within the guild. Full member (standard active membership with full benefits), fi-core (financial core member with limited participation), apprentice (trainee or provisional member), honorary (recognition membership), lifetime (permanent membership status), emeritus (retired member with recognition).']
  next_dues_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the next membership dues payment is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or remarks about this guild membership. May include information about membership restrictions, special accommodations, or historical context.']
  pension_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the talent is eligible for guild pension plan contributions and benefits. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  residual_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this guild membership makes the talent eligible to receive residual payments for reuse of their work. True if eligible, false otherwise. Critical for royalty calculation workflows.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the guild membership ended, whether through resignation, expulsion, or other termination. Null for active memberships.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this guild affiliation record was last modified in the system.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the guild membership status was last verified with the guild organization. Used to ensure data accuracy for production compliance.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the guild membership status. API (automated guild system integration), manual (direct guild contact), document (membership card or certificate scan), self-reported (talent declaration), third-party (verification service).']

  Note: 'Tracks each talent\'s formal membership and standing within entertainment industry guilds and unions — SAG-AFTRA, WGA (Writers Guild of America), DGA (Directors Guild of America), IATSE, AFM (American Federation of Musicians), and international equivalents (BECTU, ACTRA). Records membership number, guild code, membership tier (full member, fi-core, apprentice), join date, current standing (good standing, suspended, resigned), dues payment status, and applicable collective bargaining agreement (CBA) version. Critical for residual eligibility determination and production compliance.'
}

Essential for contract administration, payment routing, rights clearance, and partner r']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production (series, film, episode, special) for which this talent is contracted. Nullable for overall deals and first-look deals not tied to a specific production.']

Business justification: Talent contracts must comply with specific regulatory obligations including union rules, labor laws, minimum compensation requirements, and content regulations. Essential for contract compliance audit']
