Sales pipeline reporting, revenue forecasting, and opportunity-to-invoice tracking require linking opportunities to the billing account t']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Sales opportunities for broadcast advertising must reference the licensed station where ads will air for FCC compliance tracking, public inspection file requirements, and regulatory reporting. Station']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Opportunities must be assigned to cost centers for budget tracking, P&L attribution, and sales forecast roll-up by business unit. Essential for financial planning and variance analysis in media sales ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Opportunities often involve partner-based revenue streams (distribution deals, co-marketing, affiliate revenue). Standard CRM practice to track partner involvement in sales pipeline for forecasting an']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Opportunities require profit center attribution for segment reporting, EBITDA tracking, and management reporting by business line (Linear TV, Streaming, Syndication). Critical for quarterly business r']
  sales_agency_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising agency representing the advertiser in this deal. Null for direct sales. Links to agency master record.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Opportunities belong to sales territories for territory management and quota allocation. This FK enables territory-based opportunity tracking. N:1 relationship. New attribute needed for territory mana']
  upfront_deal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent upfront commitment deal if this opportunity is part of an upfront negotiation. Null for scatter market and direct deals. Used to track upfront commitment fulfillment.']
  campaign_objective varchar [note: 'Primary marketing objective the advertiser aims to achieve with this campaign. Influences creative strategy, placement selection, and success metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|product_launch|direct_response|lead_generation|traffic|engagement|video_views|app_installs — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  close_date date [note: 'Target date by which the sales team expects to close this opportunity (win or lose). Used for pipeline forecasting and sales velocity tracking. Updated as negotiations progress.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity was marked as closed-won or closed-lost. Null for open opportunities. Used for sales cycle analysis and win rate calculation.']
  competitive_displacement varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing competitive intelligence about which competitor this deal may displace or compete against. Used for win/loss analysis and competitive strategy.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions rate negotiated for this opportunity. Standard pricing metric for digital and streaming advertising. Expressed in currency per 1,000 impressions.']
  cprp_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost per rating point negotiated for linear TV or radio buys. Standard pricing metric for broadcast advertising. Calculated as total cost divided by total GRPs delivered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this opportunity record was first created in Salesforce Media Cloud. Used for lead-to-close cycle time analysis and pipeline velocity tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency deal tracking for international sales.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time segment of the broadcast day targeted by this opportunity. Daypart classification affects pricing and audience demographics. Applicable primarily to linear TV and radio deals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime_time|late_fringe|late_night|overnight|weekend|sports|news — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales opportunity by transaction model. Upfront = advance commitment during upfront season; Scatter = last-minute inventory sales; Direct = direct-sold advertising; Programmatic Guaranteed = automated guaranteed buys; Content License = licensing content rights; Syndication = resale of content to multiple outlets.']
  demographic_target varchar [note: 'Primary demographic audience segment targeted by this campaign (e.g., \'Adults 18-49\', \'Women 25-54\', \'Men 18-34\'). Used for audience guarantee and measurement alignment.']
  estimated_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue value of the opportunity in USD. For advertising deals, represents total campaign spend; for content licensing, represents license fee. Used for revenue forecasting and quota tracking.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for the advertising campaign or content distribution window. Defines the duration of the media buy or license period.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the advertising campaign or content distribution window. For advertising, marks the beginning of the ad flight; for content licensing, marks the start of the license window.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Sales forecast category indicating confidence level for revenue recognition. Pipeline = early stage; Best Case = likely but not committed; Commit = high confidence; Closed = won. Used for revenue forecasting rollups.']
  is_upfront boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this opportunity is part of the annual upfront sales season. True = upfront commitment; False = scatter market or other deal type. Used for upfront tracking and reporting.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales activity (call, email, meeting) logged against this opportunity. Used for pipeline hygiene monitoring and stale opportunity identification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this opportunity record. Used for data freshness validation and change tracking. Updated automatically on any field modification.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel of this sales opportunity. Tracks how the lead was generated for marketing attribution and lead source ROI analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inbound|outbound|referral|event|partner|renewal|upsell — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loss_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed explanation of why the opportunity was lost. Captures competitive intelligence, pricing feedback, and strategic insights for future deals.']
  loss_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason for losing the opportunity if stage is closed-lost. Used for loss analysis, competitive intelligence, and sales process improvement. Null for won or open opportunities.']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Description of the next action or milestone required to advance this opportunity. Used for sales activity tracking and pipeline hygiene. Updated regularly by sales executive.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sales opportunity, typically including advertiser name, campaign type, and time period (e.g., \'Acme Corp Q4 Upfront Buy\').']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the opportunity, typically auto-generated in format OPP-YYYYNNNN. Used in sales communications and reporting.']
  probability decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated likelihood of closing this opportunity as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Typically aligned with stage (e.g., Proposal = 40%, Negotiation = 60%). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the media product being sold. Linear TV = traditional broadcast; Digital Video = online video ads; Streaming = OTT/SVOD/AVOD inventory; Content Licensing = rights to broadcast content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|digital_video|streaming|radio|podcast|display|social|sponsorship|content_licensing — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requires_makegood boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this opportunity includes makegood provisions for audience delivery shortfalls. True = makegoods may be required if audience guarantees are not met. Used for inventory planning.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the sales pipeline lifecycle. Tracks progression from initial prospecting through closure. Used for pipeline forecasting and sales funnel analysis.']
  target_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target Gross Rating Points representing the total audience reach multiplied by frequency. Standard metric for linear TV and radio campaign planning. One GRP = 1% of the target audience reached.']
  target_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of ad impressions committed in this opportunity. Primary delivery metric for digital video, streaming, and programmatic deals. Measured in thousands or millions.']

  Note: 'A qualified sales pursuit representing a potential advertising campaign, content licensing deal, syndication agreement, or distribution carriage negotiation. Tracks deal stage (prospecting, proposal, negotiation, closed-won, closed-lost), estimated deal value, deal type (upfront, scatter, direct, programmatic guaranteed, content license, syndication), target flight dates, assigned sales executive, probability of close, and competitive displacement notes. Sourced from Salesforce Media Cloud Opportunity object. The primary pipeline entity feeding revenue forecasting and upfront commitment tracking.'
}

Table sales.rep {
  rep_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rep']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sales reps are assigned to cost centers for expense allocation (salaries, commissions, travel), budget tracking, and P&L reporting. Finance requires cost center assignment for headcount planning, vari']
  manager_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the sales manager or team lead who supervises this sales representative. Self-referential relationship within the sales rep hierarchy.']
  account_portfolio_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of the account portfolio managed by the sales representative. Indicates whether they manage agency relationships, direct advertiser accounts, distribution partners (MVPD/vMVPD), OTT platforms, syndication buyers, or other client segments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agency|direct_advertiser|mvpd|vmvpd|ott_platform|syndicator|international|government — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  annual_quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual revenue quota assigned to the sales representative for the current quota period. Measured in the company\'s reporting currency.']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date the sales representative\'s current territory or account portfolio assignment ended. Null if currently active in the assignment.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date the sales representative began their current territory or account portfolio assignment. May differ from hire date if reassigned.']
  commission_plan_type varchar [note: 'Type of commission structure applied to the sales representative. Defines how commissions are calculated based on sales performance (e.g., base salary plus commission, commission-only, tiered rates, accelerators for exceeding quota).']
  commission_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate percentage applied to qualified sales revenue. May vary by deal type or quota attainment tier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales representative record was first created in the system.']
  deal_type_specialization varchar [note: 'Primary deal type or sales channel specialization for the representative. Indicates whether they focus on national TV advertising, digital campaigns, content licensing, syndication deals, or other revenue streams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: national_tv|local_tv|digital|syndication|licensing|upfront|scatter|programmatic|sponsorship|multi_platform — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  digital_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the sales representative is certified and authorized to sell digital advertising inventory, including OTT, streaming, and programmatic channels.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the sales representative used for client communication and internal correspondence.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the sales representative was hired or onboarded into the sales organization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales representative record was last updated or modified.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Primary office location or city where the sales representative is based. May be a physical office or designated home office location.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Performance classification or tier assigned to the sales representative based on quota attainment and sales metrics. Used for recognition, compensation adjustments, and coaching.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the sales representative used for client outreach and deal negotiations.']
  quota_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quota amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  quota_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current quota period for the sales representative.']
  quota_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current quota period for the sales representative. Typically aligns with fiscal year or calendar year.']
  rep_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the sales representative as it appears in business records and communications.']
  rep_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales representative within the sales organization. Indicates whether they are actively selling, on leave, or no longer with the company.']
  salesforce_user_reference varchar [note: 'Unique user identifier in Salesforce Media Cloud CRM system. Links the sales rep record to their Salesforce user profile for opportunity tracking and pipeline management.']
  territory_assignment varchar [note: 'Geographic or market territory assigned to the sales representative. May be defined by region, DMA (Designated Market Area), or account segment.']
  upfront_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the sales representative is certified and authorized to negotiate and close upfront advertising deals. Upfront deals are advance commitments made during the annual upfront sales event.']

  Note: 'The sales representative or account executive profile within the sales domain, capturing the commercial assignment and quota context for each seller. Tracks rep name, employee ID (FK to HR/workforce), territory assignment, account portfolio, deal type specialization (national TV, digital, syndication, licensing), annual quota, quota period, commission plan type, manager, and active status. This is the sales-domain view of the seller — not a duplicate of HR employee master — focused on commercial assignment, quota, and territory rather than HR attributes.'
}

Table sales.sales_territory {
  sales_territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales territory. Primary key.']
  assigned_sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary sales representative or account executive assigned to this territory.']
  parent_territory_sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent territory in a hierarchical territory structure. Null for top-level territories.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sales territories map to profit centers for revenue attribution, quota allocation, and segment P&L reporting. Finance uses this for EBITDA reporting by geographic/product segment, transfer pricing, an']
  account_count varchar [note: 'Number of active advertiser or agency accounts assigned to this territory.']
  account_segment varchar [note: 'Classification of accounts within this territory by size or relationship type: enterprise (largest accounts), major (key accounts), mid_market, small_business, agency (advertising agencies), or direct (direct advertisers).']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission percentage rate applied to sales within this territory for compensation calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this territory record was first created in the system.']
  dma_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Nielsen DMA codes or names included in this territory for broadcast market segmentation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended territories.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this territory definition becomes active and assignments take effect.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic area covered by this territory (e.g., states, DMAs, postal codes, countries). Free-form field for complex geographic definitions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this territory record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about this territory, including special instructions, historical context, or operational considerations.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Strategic priority assigned to this territory for resource allocation and management focus: high, medium, low, or strategic (top-tier focus).']
  product_line_focus varchar [note: 'Primary advertising product lines or inventory types this territory is authorized to sell (e.g., Linear TV, Digital Video, OTT/Streaming, Radio, Display, Sponsorships). Comma-separated for multiple product lines.']
  revenue_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual or period revenue quota assigned to this territory, used for quota allocation and performance tracking.']
  revenue_target_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue target amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel or go-to-market approach for this territory: direct (direct sales team), agency (sold through agencies), programmatic (automated buying), self_service (online self-serve), or partner (channel partners).']
  scatter_market_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory handles scatter market (last-minute, short-term) ad inventory sales. True if scatter sales are part of territory scope.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the territory for reporting and system integration (e.g., NE-01, AUTO-WEST, MAJ-CA).']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the territory scope, coverage rules, special conditions, and strategic objectives.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the sales territory (e.g., Northeast Region, Automotive Vertical, Major Accounts West).']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory: active (in use), inactive (not currently assigned), pending (awaiting activation), suspended (temporarily disabled), or archived (historical record).']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory structure: geographic (region-based), account_segment (by account size or tier), vertical (industry-based such as automotive, pharma, retail, entertainment), hybrid (combination), national (entire country), or regional (multi-state or multi-market).']
  upfront_participation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this territory participates in annual upfront advertising sales events. True if territory is included in upfront planning and commitments.']
  vertical_industry_focus varchar [note: 'Primary industry vertical or sector this territory specializes in (e.g., Automotive, Pharmaceutical, Retail, Entertainment, Financial Services, Technology). Used for vertical-based territory models.']

  Note: 'Defines the geographic, account-based, or vertical market territory assigned to sales teams and individual reps. Captures territory name, territory type (geographic region, account segment, vertical — automotive, pharma, retail, entertainment), assigned sales rep or team, revenue target, account count, and effective date range. Supports territory management, quota allocation, and pipeline reporting by market segment. Managed within Salesforce Media Cloud territory management module.'
}

Table sales.avail {
  avail_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advertising inventory availability record.']
  ad_break_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.ad_break. Business justification: Avails in broadcast traffic systems are break-specific inventory units. Linking avail to ad_break enables break-level inventory availability reporting, hold management, and sold-vs-available reconcili']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.ad_order. Business justification: An avail (inventory availability record) gets booked against an ad_order when inventory is reserved. Linking avail to ad_order enables tracking of which inventory slots are committed to which orders, ']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.advertiser. Business justification: Avails can be held or reserved for specific advertisers (competitive separation, category exclusivity). Linking avail to advertiser enables advertiser-specific inventory holds and competitive separati']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Available inventory slots are station-specific and must reference the broadcast license for regulatory tracking, compliance verification, and ensuring sold inventory matches licensed station capabilit']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.campaign. Business justification: Avails are evaluated and reserved in the context of specific campaigns. Linking avail to campaign enables campaign-level inventory planning and avail analysis, supporting the pre-sale workflow where a']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broadcast channel or platform where this inventory is available.']
  content_episode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.content_episode. Business justification: Avail inventory in episodic TV is tracked at the episode level — traffic systems need to know which specific episode an avail slot belongs to for episode-level inventory management, content adjacency ']
  delivery_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: Avails represent available ad inventory on specific delivery channels (FAST, OTT, linear). Direct FK enables avail management systems to reference the exact delivery channel for digital/FAST inventory']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Avails are projected/reserved for specific demographic targets. Inventory management systems need to know which demo segment each avail is optimized for (based on program audience composition). Avail.']
  grant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.grant. Business justification: Avail generation in broadcast sales is driven by active rights grants — only content with a valid grant for the territory/platform/window can produce sellable inventory. Rights-to-revenue workflow req']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Avails (available ad inventory) are market-specific. Inventory management, market-based pricing, geographic targeting, and avail allocation all require knowing which market_coverage area each avail se']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales proposal that includes this availability, if applicable.']
  rights_availability_window_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_availability_window. Business justification: Broadcast sales avail validation requires confirming content rights are open for the specific platform, territory, and time window. Rights-cleared avail reports join these entities daily. A media broa']
  rights_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.rights_territory. Business justification: Avails are market/territory-specific inventory units that must be validated against rights territories for legal clearance. geographic_market is a denormalized string; the FK to rights_territory norma']
  sales_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertiser or agency account that currently holds this inventory.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative responsible for selling this inventory.']
  schedule_slot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.schedule_slot. Business justification: When an avail is sold and confirmed, it maps to a specific schedule slot for playout. Business process: order fulfillment, traffic-to-playout handoff, as-run reconciliation, affidavit generation.']
  title_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the program or content title during which this advertising slot is available.']
  ad_pod_position varchar [note: 'Position of this spot within the ad pod or commercial break (1 for first position, 2 for second, etc.).']
  air_date date [note: 'Scheduled broadcast date when this advertising slot will air.']
  air_time timestamp [note: 'Precise scheduled broadcast timestamp when this advertising slot will air.']
  avail_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for this availability record, typically generated by the traffic system.']
  break_type varchar [note: 'Type of commercial break where this inventory is positioned (opening, mid-break, closing, standalone).']
  content_rating varchar [note: 'Parental guidance rating of the program content surrounding this advertising slot (TV-Y, TV-Y7, TV-G, TV-PG, TV-14, TV-MA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this availability record was first created in the system.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time segment of the broadcast day when this inventory is available (early morning, morning, daytime, early fringe, prime access, prime time, late fringe, late night, overnight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: early_morning|morning|daytime|early_fringe|prime_access|prime_time|late_fringe|late_night|overnight — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  floor_cpm_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum Cost Per Thousand Impressions (CPM) rate in USD that the seller will accept for this inventory.']
  floor_rate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum unit rate in USD that the seller will accept for this advertising slot.']
  genre varchar [note: 'Content genre or category of the program where this advertising slot is available (e.g., News, Sports, Drama, Comedy, Reality).']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the current hold on this inventory will expire if not confirmed.']
  hold_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the current hold was placed on this inventory.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current reservation status of this inventory (available, first hold, second hold, third hold, released, sold, expired). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|first_hold|second_hold|third_hold|released|sold|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inventory_type varchar [note: 'Type of advertising unit available for sale (spot, sponsorship, integration, digital pre-roll, mid-roll, post-roll, overlay, branded content). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spot|sponsorship|integration|pre_roll|mid_roll|post_roll|overlay|branded_content — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this availability record was last updated.']
  makegood_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory is eligible for makegood compensation if performance guarantees are not met.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or special instructions related to this availability (e.g., creative restrictions, special positioning requirements).']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Distribution platform type where this inventory is available (linear, VOD, SVOD, AVOD, TVOD, OTT, FAST, digital). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear|vod|svod|avod|tvod|ott|fast|digital — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  preemptible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory can be preempted by higher-priority advertising (true) or is guaranteed (false).']
  projected_grp decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated Gross Rating Points this slot is expected to deliver, representing the percentage of the target audience reached multiplied by frequency.']
  projected_impressions varchar [note: 'Estimated number of viewer impressions this advertising slot is expected to deliver based on historical audience data.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that generated this availability record (Wide Orbit, Salesforce, programmatic SSP, manual entry).']
  unit_length_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the advertising unit in seconds (e.g., 15, 30, 60 for traditional spots).']
  window_end_date date [note: 'End date of the availability window for content licensing or sponsorship opportunities.']
  window_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the availability window for content licensing or sponsorship opportunities.']

  Note: 'An inventory availability record representing a specific advertising slot, sponsorship position, or content window that is available for sale at a given point in time. Captures channel or platform, program or content title, daypart, air date or window, unit type (spot, sponsorship, integration, digital pre-roll), spot length, available impressions or GRPs, audience demographic projection, floor CPM, hold status (available, first hold, second hold, released, sold), holding account reference, hold expiry date, and source system (Wide Orbit, programmatic SSP). Avails are the sellable inventory units that sales reps present to buyers and that proposals are built from. Sourced from Wide Orbit inventory management and EPG scheduling. Updated in near-real-time as holds are placed and orders confirmed.'
}

Table sales.forecast {
  forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue forecast record.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.channel. Business justification: Sales forecasts in broadcast are channel-specific. Channel-level revenue forecasting drives inventory planning, rate card management, and sales quota allocation. forecast currently only links to daypa']
  daypart_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to scheduling.daypart. Business justification: Sales forecasts are segmented by daypart for revenue planning. Business process: daypart-level revenue forecasting, quota allocation, inventory yield optimization by daypart.']
  delivery_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.delivery_channel. Business justification: Sales revenue forecasts in media broadcasting are broken down by delivery channel (linear, OTT, FAST, streaming). Direct FK enables channel-level revenue forecasting, budget planning, and variance ana']
  demographic_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.demographic_segment. Business justification: Sales forecasts are segmented by demographic target (upfront deals are demo-specific, e.g., A18-49 vs A25-54). Revenue forecasting by key demos is standard practice for broadcast sales planning and qu']
  market_coverage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to audience.market_coverage. Business justification: Sales forecasts are built by market (scatter vs upfront revenue by DMA). Revenue forecasting, quota setting, pipeline management, and market-based attainment tracking all require market-level aggregat']
  prior_forecast_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on forecast (prior_forecast_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Sales forecasts roll up to profit center financial plans for budget vs. actual variance analysis, EBITDA forecasting, and management reporting. Finance consolidates sales forecasts by profit center fo']
  sales_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account this forecast is associated with, if forecast is account-specific.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative who submitted this forecast.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Forecasts are often segmented by sales territory for territory-level revenue projections and quota tracking. This FK enables territory-based forecasting. N:1 relationship. New attribute needed to esta']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Sales forecasts in media are built around specific series — forecasting ad revenue for a returning series is a core broadcast sales planning activity. Series-level revenue forecasting drives upfront p']
  upfront_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.upfront_deal. Business justification: Revenue forecasts in media broadcasting are heavily driven by upfront deal commitments. Linking forecast to upfront_deal enables upfront-specific revenue forecasting and tracking of how upfront commit']
  account_segment varchar [note: 'Segmentation of the account type for this forecast: enterprise (large advertisers), mid-market, small business, agency (media buying agencies), direct (direct-to-advertiser).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast was approved by the sales manager.']
  attainment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of quota represented by the total forecasted revenue (forecasted revenue / quota * 100).']
  best_case_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount in the best case forecast category (optimistic scenario including stretch opportunities).']
  broadcast_year varchar [note: 'Broadcast year (September to August cycle) this forecast applies to, represented as the starting calendar year.']
  carriage_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from carriage fee agreements (distribution payments from cable/satellite providers).']
  closed_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount from deals already closed and won during this forecast period.']
  commit_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount in the commit forecast category (high confidence deals expected to close).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this forecast.']
  deal_type varchar [note: 'Type of sales deal being forecasted: upfront (advance commitment), scatter (last-minute inventory), licensing (content rights), syndication (content resale), carriage (distribution fee), sponsorship (branded integration).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter this forecast applies to (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4).']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the forecast confidence level: commit (high confidence), best case (optimistic), pipeline (possible), omitted (excluded from forecast), closed (already won).']
  forecast_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the forecast record, typically including period and rep name.']
  forecast_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the forecast submission.']
  forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this forecast record was last modified.']
  licensing_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from content licensing deals (selling rights to broadcast or distribute content).']
  manager_override_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount adjusted by sales manager override, used when manager believes rep forecast needs correction.']
  manager_override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by sales manager for any override adjustment to the forecast.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and commentary from the sales representative about assumptions, risks, or opportunities in this forecast.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the time period this forecast covers.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the time period this forecast covers.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Time period granularity for this forecast: quarter, broadcast year (Sept-Aug), calendar year, or month.']
  pipeline_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount in the pipeline forecast category (possible deals still in early stages).']
  quota_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Assigned sales quota target for this sales rep and period.']
  scatter_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from scatter market deals (last-minute ad inventory sales outside upfront commitments).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when this forecast version was submitted by the sales representative.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when this forecast version was submitted.']
  syndication_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from syndication deals (reselling content to multiple outlets).']
  total_forecasted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total projected revenue amount for this forecast period across all deal types and categories.']
  update_frequency varchar [note: 'Expected frequency of forecast updates for this period: weekly (standard selling periods), daily (upfront season), monthly (long-range planning), ad hoc (as needed).']
  upfront_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue from upfront deals (advance advertising commitments made during upfront season).']
  variance_to_quota decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between total forecasted revenue and quota amount (positive indicates over-quota forecast, negative indicates gap).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this forecast submission, incremented with each revision for the same period.']
  weighted_pipeline_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total pipeline value adjusted by probability weights for each opportunity, providing a risk-adjusted forecast.']

  Note: 'Revenue forecast record capturing projected bookings by sales rep, territory, account segment, deal type (upfront, scatter, licensing, syndication, carriage), and time period (quarter, broadcast year). Tracks forecast version, submission date, forecast category (commit, best case, pipeline, omitted), total forecasted revenue, weighted pipeline value, variance to quota, and manager override amount. Enables sales leadership to project revenue attainment against quota, identify pipeline gaps, and allocate resources. Updated weekly during active selling periods and daily during upfront season.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice. Primary key for the invoice entity.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.advertiser. Business justification: Direct-billed advertiser invoices (no agency intermediary) require advertiser reference for AR aging, credit limit enforcement, payment terms application, and advertiser-level revenue reporting in med']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account or subscription account associated with this invoice.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.cycle. Business justification: Invoice is generated by ONE billing cycle run. Need to track which cycle configuration was used to generate this invoice for audit and reconciliation purposes. No redundant columns to remove.']
  broadcast_license_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.broadcast_license. Business justification: Station-specific invoices (local ad sales, tower leases) must be attributable to the licensed facility for FCC financial reporting, ownership attribution calculations, and license renewal revenue disc']
  company_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Invoices must be issued by a specific legal entity for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory compliance (FCC/CRTC reporting), and consolidated financial statements in multi-entity broadcast group']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoices need cost center attribution for internal cost allocation, chargeback processes, and departmental P&L. Common in media orgs where production units, channels, or business units tracked as cost']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Invoices issued to distribution partners, syndication partners, or affiliate stations for carriage fees, license payments, or revenue shares. Core AR process in media broadcasting. Partner is the bill']
  production_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.project. Business justification: Production finance teams run project-level P&L reports aggregating all invoices (vendor payments, co-production billings) against a project. Direct project_id on invoice enables this without traversin']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Invoices must be attributed to profit centers for segment reporting, business unit P&L, and EBITDA calculation. Critical for media broadcasting where channels, platforms, or content divisions are prof']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Political advertising invoices must reference FCC public file filings per 47 CFR 73.3526. Enables compliance officers to trace revenue to regulatory disclosures and auditors to verify filing completen']
  revenue_stream_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_stream. Business justification: Invoices must classify revenue by stream for financial reporting, segment analysis, and ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition compliance. Critical for media broadcasting where revenue streams (advertisi']
  rights_content_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the content license window for syndication invoices. Defines the rights period being billed.']
  royalty_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.royalty_statement. Business justification: Royalty statements are invoiced to licensees or paid to rights holders in media broadcasting. The accounts receivable/payable reconciliation process requires linking the invoice generated for a royalt']
  sales_agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.agency. Business justification: Agency-billed invoices require agency reference for consolidated billing, commission calculation, agency credit limit management, and agency-level AR reporting in advertising operations.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer, advertiser, MVPD partner, or syndication licensee being billed.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription. Business justification: Invoice-to-subscription lifecycle management: invoices must reference the subscription they bill for dunning, renewal triggering, and subscription status updates on payment. invoice has subscriber_id ']
  subscription_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscription plan for SVOD billing invoices. Null for non-subscription revenue categories.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total adjustment amount for credits, makegoods, or billing corrections. Can be positive or negative.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid against this invoice to date. Used to calculate outstanding balance.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Relevant for subscription and carriage fee billing.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Relevant for subscription and carriage fee billing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the billing system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to this invoice, including promotional discounts, volume discounts, or contractual adjustments.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was disputed by the customer. Null if never disputed.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by customer for disputing this invoice. Populated when invoice_status is disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected based on payment terms. Used to calculate overdue status.']
  installment_number varchar [note: 'Installment sequence number for syndication license fees paid over multiple periods. Null for single-payment invoices.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued to the customer, advertiser, or partner. Used as the basis for payment terms calculation.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique invoice number used for customer communication and payment reference. Generated by billing system (Zuora, Wide Orbit, or SAP S/4HANA).']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the billing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|sent|paid|partially_paid|overdue|disputed|void|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  isci_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISCI codes for ad spots included in this advertising invoice. Used for affidavit matching and proof of broadcast.']
  mvpd_subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Number of subscribers reported by the MVPD partner as the basis for carriage fee calculation. Relevant for carriage fee invoices.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this invoice for internal reference or customer communication.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on this invoice. Calculated as total_amount_due minus amount_paid.']
  paid_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was fully paid. Null if not yet paid or only partially paid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment method used or expected for this invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|bank_transfer|ach|wire|check|direct_debit|paypal|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms defining when payment is due relative to invoice date. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|prepaid — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proration_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subscription invoice includes prorated charges due to mid-cycle changes. Relevant for SVOD billing.']
  revenue_recognition_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period over which revenue from this invoice should be recognized per ASC 606 guidelines.']
  revenue_recognition_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period over which revenue from this invoice should be recognized per ASC 606 guidelines.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Billing system that originated this invoice. Zuora for subscription billing, Wide Orbit for advertising billing, SAP S/4HANA for carriage and syndication.']
  source_system_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Original invoice identifier from the source billing system. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, fees, and adjustments. Sum of all line item amounts.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to this invoice, including sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes.']
  total_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total amount due after all taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount the customer must pay.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified in the billing system.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Reason this invoice was voided or cancelled. Populated when invoice_status is void or cancelled.']
  wide_orbit_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Wide Orbit sales order for advertising billing invoices. Null for non-advertising revenue categories.']

  Note: 'Master billing document representing a formal demand for payment issued to a subscriber, advertiser, MVPD partner, or syndication licensee. Captures invoice number, billing period, total amount due, currency, tax amounts, invoice status (draft, issued, paid, disputed, void), due date, payment terms, revenue category (subscription, advertising, carriage, syndication), ASC 606 revenue recognition period, and source system reference (Zuora, Wide Orbit, SAP S/4HANA). Supports stream-specific extended attributes: subscription plan/proration for SVOD billing, Wide Orbit order ID/ISCI codes for ad billing, MVPD subscriber count basis for carriage fees, and license window/installment schedule for syndication. Serves as the SSOT for all billing documents across all revenue streams.'
}
