Invoice lines for window-based licensing fees must reference the windowing plan to suppo']
  charge_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the charge appearing on the invoice line, explaining the service, ad spot, carriage fee period, or license fee installment being billed.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center or business unit responsible for this revenue line, used for internal financial reporting and profitability analysis.']
  cpm_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per thousand impressions rate for advertising line items, the standard pricing metric for ad inventory sales in broadcasting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the billing system, used for audit trail and billing cycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to this line item, including promotional discounts, volume discounts, makegoods credits, or contractual rate reductions.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier for the discount program or promotion applied to this line, used for tracking discount effectiveness and revenue impact.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any billing dispute associated with this line item, tracking customer challenges and resolution workflow.']
  extended_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for this line item including all charges, discounts, and taxes. This is the final billable amount for the line.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for posting this revenue line item to the financial system, ensuring proper revenue classification in financial statements.']
  grp_delivered decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross Rating Points delivered for this advertising line item, measuring the total audience reach and frequency achieved for the campaign.']
  isci_code varchar [note: 'Industry Standard Commercial Identification code for the specific ad creative that aired, used for affidavit verification and makegood tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this invoice line record, tracking billing adjustments and corrections for audit purposes.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing line item identifier displayed on customer invoices and billing statements for reference and dispute resolution.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice, used for ordering and referencing specific charges on the invoice document.']
  makegood_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a makegood (compensatory ad spot) provided to an advertiser due to underdelivery or technical issues with a previous campaign.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about this invoice line, used for documenting special billing arrangements, dispute details, or billing adjustments.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of units being charged on this line. For ad spots this is the number of impressions or spots; for subscriptions this is typically 1; for usage-based billing this is the measured consumption.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Rule or method for recognizing revenue from this line item, such as point-in-time (ad spot delivery), over-time (subscription period), or milestone-based (license installment).']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period covered by this line item charge. For subscriptions this is the billing cycle end; for ad campaigns this is the flight end date.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period covered by this line item charge. For subscriptions this is the billing cycle start; for ad campaigns this is the flight start date.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of service or product being billed on this line, such as premium channel subscription, ad pod placement, carriage agreement tier, or syndication window.']
  spot_count varchar [note: 'Number of ad spots delivered for this line item, used in advertising billing to verify delivery against the sales order commitment.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Line item subtotal before taxes and discounts, calculated as quantity multiplied by unit rate.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on this line item, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction and service type.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code that determines the tax treatment for this line item, used for tax calculation and compliance reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field, such as spot (ad placement), impression (ad view), subscriber (subscription count), stream (viewing session), or GB (data transfer).']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit for this line item. For advertising this may be CPM (Cost Per Mille) rate; for subscriptions this is the plan price; for carriage this is the per-subscriber fee.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within a billing invoice, representing a discrete charge for a specific service, ad spot, carriage fee period, or license fee installment. Captures line number, charge description, quantity, unit rate, extended amount, revenue category (subscription, advertising, carriage, syndication), performance obligation identifier per ASC 606, tax code, and discount applied. Enables granular revenue recognition and dispute resolution at the charge level.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment transaction. Primary key for the payment record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or advertiser account making the payment. Links payment to account holder.']
  company_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Payments are received by a specific legal entity (bank account holder), critical for cash management, intercompany cash pooling, and regulatory reporting. Media broadcasters with multiple entities nee']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to billing document.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Payments received from partners (carriage fees, license payments) or payments made to partners (content acquisition, minimum guarantees). Bidirectional cash flow tracking essential for partner reconci']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: Payment is made using ONE payment method (stored payment instrument). Currently duplicates card and bank account details; need FK to the stored payment_method record for single source of truth. Remove']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Payments attributed to profit centers for segment-level cash collection and DSO analysis. Critical for media broadcasters tracking cash collection performance by channel or platform for working capita']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Direct payments for regulatory fees (broadcast license renewals, FCC filing fees, regulatory authority annual assessments) require linkage to the specific filing for payment confirmation tracking, com']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of the payment transaction in the specified currency. Gross payment value before any adjustments.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the payment amount that has been applied against outstanding invoices or account balances. May differ from payment_amount if partial application or overpayment.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the payment processor or card issuer confirming approval of the transaction.']
  bank_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the bank for wire transfers, ACH transactions, or check deposits. Used for bank reconciliation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the payment was submitted (web portal, mobile app, IVR, mail, in-person, API, batch import). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_portal|mobile_app|ivr|mail|in_person|api|batch_import — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number for payments made by paper check. Used for reconciliation and tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD).']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the payment dispute or chargeback was initiated. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed reason for payment failure if status is failed. Includes processor error codes and messages.']
  gateway varchar [note: 'Name of the payment gateway or processor used to handle the transaction (e.g., Stripe, Authorize.Net, PayPal, Adyen).']
  gateway_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway or processor. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was posted to the general ledger in the financial system. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  is_autopay boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment was processed automatically through a recurring payment or autopay setup. True if autopay, false if manual.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is currently under dispute or chargeback. True if disputed, false otherwise.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument used for the transaction. Indicates the financial mechanism (credit card, ACH, wire, check, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ach|wire_transfer|check|cash|paypal|direct_debit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the payment transaction. Used for special instructions, dispute details, or reconciliation notes.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date when the payment transaction was initiated or received by the business. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique payment transaction number used for customer communication and reconciliation. Format: PAY-XXXXXXXX.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Type of business relationship generating the payment (subscriber, advertiser, distributor, syndicator, licensee, other).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks progression from pending through cleared or failed states. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|processing|cleared|failed|reversed|cancelled|refunded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the payment transaction was processed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the customer if payment status is refunded. May be partial or full refund.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was processed and issued to the customer. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for payment reversal if status is reversed. Includes chargeback reason codes and dispute details.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date when the payment funds were settled and cleared in the business bank account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the payment amount that remains unapplied and is held as credit on the account. Calculated as payment_amount minus applied_amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Record of a payment transaction received or applied against an outstanding invoice or account balance. Captures payment date, amount, currency, payment method type (credit card, ACH, wire, check), payment status (pending, cleared, failed, reversed), PCI DSS tokenized card reference, bank transaction reference, settlement date, and applied invoice linkage. Integrates with Zuora for subscriber payments and SAP S/4HANA for financial posting. SSOT for all inbound monetary receipts.'
}

Table billing.payment_method {
  payment_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment method record. Primary key.']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber or advertiser who owns this payment method. Links to the subscriber master record in Zuora.']
  bank_account_number_last_four varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the bank account number. Used for customer identification without exposing full account details. Applicable for ACH, SEPA, and wire transfer methods.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'The routing number for ACH or wire transfer payment methods. Stored in masked or tokenized format per PCI DSS requirements. Used for direct debit and electronic funds transfer.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address line associated with the payment method. Used for Address Verification Service (AVS) checks and fraud prevention.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Optional field for complete billing address capture.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality of the billing address. Used for AVS verification and tax jurisdiction determination.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address. Used for international payment processing and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR, PCI DSS regional requirements).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Critical for AVS verification and accurate sales tax calculation.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or region of the billing address. Used for AVS verification and regional tax compliance.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The brand or network of the payment card (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, American Express). Applicable only when payment method type is credit or debit card. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|diners_club|unionpay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual or entity as it appears on the payment card. Used for billing verification and fraud prevention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment method record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and payment method age analysis.']
  deactivated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment method was deactivated or closed. Null if the payment method is still active. Used for lifecycle tracking and retention analysis.']
  deactivation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the payment method was deactivated. Used for churn analysis and process improvement.']
  expiry_alert_date date [note: 'The date when the system should trigger an alert to the subscriber about upcoming payment method expiration. Typically set 30-60 days before card expiry to enable proactive customer outreach and reduce involuntary churn.']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'The month (1-12) when the payment card expires. Used to validate card validity and trigger expiry alerts for subscription renewals.']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit year when the payment card expires. Used in conjunction with expiry_month to determine card validity and schedule proactive customer outreach.']
  failed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative count of failed transaction attempts using this payment method. Used to identify problematic payment methods and trigger dunning workflows.']
  gateway_customer_profile_reference varchar [note: 'The unique customer profile identifier in the payment gateway system. Used to link this payment method to the gateway\'s customer vault for secure transaction processing.']
  gateway_payment_method_reference varchar [note: 'The unique payment method identifier assigned by the payment gateway. Used for transaction processing and reconciliation with gateway records.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default payment method for the subscriber. Used to determine which payment method to charge for recurring subscriptions and invoices.']
  last_failure_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent failed transaction attempt. Used in conjunction with failed_transaction_count to manage retry logic and customer communication.']
  last_failure_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code or message returned by the payment gateway for the most recent failed transaction. Used for troubleshooting and customer support.']
  last_four_digits varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the payment card number. Used for customer identification and verification without exposing full card details. PCI DSS permits storage of last four digits.']
  last_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of the most recent successful transaction in the subscriber\'s billing currency. Used for fraud detection and transaction pattern analysis.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent successful transaction using this payment method. Used for payment method activity tracking and dormancy analysis.']
  mandate_reference varchar [note: 'The unique reference identifier for the direct debit mandate authorizing recurring payments. Required for SEPA Direct Debit and other regulated direct debit schemes.']
  mandate_signed_date date [note: 'The date when the customer signed the direct debit mandate. Required for regulatory compliance and dispute resolution in direct debit transactions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user or system process that last modified this payment method record. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment method record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  payment_gateway varchar [note: 'The payment gateway or processor used to tokenize and process transactions for this payment method (e.g., Stripe, Adyen, Braintree, Authorize.Net). Used for routing transactions and reconciliation.']
  payment_method_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the payment method. Determines whether the payment method can be used for new transactions. Inactive or expired methods require customer action to reactivate.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument registered. Indicates the payment mechanism used for billing transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ach|sepa|wire_transfer|paypal|apple_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  token varchar [note: 'PCI DSS-compliant tokenized representation of the payment instrument. Never stores raw Primary Account Number (PAN). Used for secure payment processing without exposing sensitive cardholder data.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the payment method was last verified. Used to track verification currency and trigger re-verification workflows.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'The current verification status of the payment method. Indicates whether the payment instrument has been successfully validated through AVS, CVV check, or micro-deposit verification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user or system process that created this payment method record. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Stored and tokenized payment instrument registered by a subscriber or advertiser for recurring or one-time billing. Captures payment method type (credit card, debit card, ACH, SEPA, wire), PCI DSS-compliant token (never raw PAN), card brand, last four digits, expiry month/year, billing address, bank routing number (masked), mandate reference for direct debit, is_default flag, verification status, and expiry alert date. Managed in Zuora for subscriber billing.'
}

Table billing.billing_account {
  billing_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing account. Primary key for the billing account entity.']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.advertiser. Business justification: Billing accounts are established per advertiser for consolidated invoicing, credit limit management, and payment terms negotiation. Foundational relationship for account setup, credit approval workflo']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.cycle. Business justification: Billing account belongs to ONE billing cycle schedule (monthly, quarterly, annual). Currently stores billing_cycle_code as STRING; need FK to cycle table for full cycle configuration. Remove billing_c']
  company_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Billing accounts must be owned by a legal entity for AR ownership, credit risk management, and consolidated reporting. Broadcast groups assign different customer segments (DTC streaming, MVPD, adverti']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Partner organizations maintain billing accounts for recurring carriage fees, license payments, or revenue shares. Standard AR/AP setup for partner financial relationships, enabling credit management a']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Legal or business name associated with the billing account. For subscriber accounts, this is the account holder name; for corporate accounts, this is the registered business name.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique account number assigned to the billing account. Used on invoices, statements, and customer correspondence.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing account. Active accounts are in good standing and can transact; suspended accounts have temporary holds due to payment issues or policy violations; closed accounts are permanently terminated; pending activation accounts are awaiting initial setup completion; delinquent accounts have overdue balances and may face service interruption; write-off accounts have uncollectible balances removed from active receivables.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing account based on the nature of the billable relationship. Subscriber accounts are for SVOD/AVOD/TVOD end-users; advertiser accounts are for agencies and direct advertisers; MVPD accounts are for multichannel video programming distributors paying carriage fees; syndication licensee accounts are for content resale partners; production vendor accounts are for production service providers; talent accounts are for residuals and royalty payments.']
  auto_pay_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this account. When true, invoices are automatically charged to the default payment method on the due date.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for billing correspondence and invoice delivery.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality for the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code in which this account is billed (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). All invoices and payments for this account are denominated in this currency.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) on which recurring invoices are generated for monthly billing cycles. Null for non-monthly cycles.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region for the billing address.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date on which this billing account was permanently closed. Null for active and suspended accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding balance allowed for this account before credit hold is applied. Null indicates no credit limit (cash-in-advance or unlimited credit).']
  current_balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current outstanding balance on the account, calculated as sum of unpaid invoices minus unapplied credits and payments. Positive values indicate amounts owed to the business; negative values indicate credit balances owed to the customer.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current stage in the collections escalation process for overdue accounts. 0 indicates no dunning (account current); higher values indicate progressive escalation steps (e.g., 1=reminder notice, 2=warning, 3=service suspension notice, 4=final notice before write-off).']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering invoices to this account. Email sends PDF invoices; postal mail sends printed invoices; electronic portal makes invoices available for download in customer self-service portal; EDI transmits invoices via Electronic Data Interchange (X12 810 or EDIFACT INVOIC).']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent dunning notice or collections contact sent to this account.']
  last_invoice_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent invoice generated for this account. Used for billing cycle tracking and account activity monitoring.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment received. Used for payment pattern analysis.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received and applied to this account. Used for payment behavior analysis and credit risk assessment.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date on which this billing account was first established and activated.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due. Common values are 0 (immediate), 15, 30, 60, or 90 days. Used to calculate invoice due dates and aging buckets.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary billing contact. Used for invoice delivery, payment reminders, and billing notifications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary billing contact or accounts payable representative for this account. Used for billing inquiries and dispute resolution.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary billing contact. Used for urgent billing inquiries and collections calls.']
  sap_customer_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer master record number in SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) module. Used for GL posting, accounts receivable management, and financial reconciliation. This is the authoritative financial system identifier.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is exempt from sales tax, VAT, or other consumption taxes. True for tax-exempt organizations (e.g., non-profits, government entities, resellers with valid exemption certificates); false for standard taxable accounts.']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued certificate or registration number authorizing tax exemption. Required for audit trail when tax_exempt_flag is true.']
  tax_exemption_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the tax exemption certificate expires and must be renewed. Null for permanent exemptions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing account record was last modified.']
  wideorbit_account_reference varchar [note: 'Account identifier in WideOrbit traffic and billing system. Used for advertising sales order management, spot scheduling, and ad billing for advertiser and agency accounts.']
  zuora_account_reference varchar [note: 'Account identifier in Zuora subscription billing platform. Used for subscription lifecycle management, recurring billing, and revenue recognition for SVOD/AVOD subscriber accounts.']

  Note: 'Billing-domain representation of a billable entity — a subscriber household, an advertising agency account, an MVPD operator, or a syndication licensee. Distinct from the subscriber or advertiser master record in their respective domains; this entity owns the financial relationship: billing address, billing currency, payment terms, credit limit, account balance, billing cycle assignment, tax exemption status, dunning level, and SAP customer account number. SSOT for the financial account identity within billing.'
}

Table billing.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cycle']
  auto_renew_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subscriptions on this billing cycle automatically renew at the end of the term. True enables automatic renewal in Zuora subscription management.']
  billing_period_end_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) when the service period covered by the invoice ends. Used to calculate service period duration for revenue recognition. Null for non-monthly frequencies.']
  billing_period_start_day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) when the service period covered by the invoice begins. May differ from cycle_day for advance billing scenarios. Null for non-monthly frequencies.']
  billing_run_day_offset varchar [note: 'Number of days before or after the cycle day when the billing run executes. Positive values indicate days after cycle day; negative values indicate days before. Used to schedule invoice generation in advance of service period.']
  billing_timing varchar [note: 'Indicates whether billing occurs in advance (invoice before service period) or in arrears (invoice after service period). Critical for revenue recognition under ASC 606.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this billing cycle configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability under SOX compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing cycle configuration record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for invoices generated under this billing cycle (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). All charges in the cycle are denominated in this currency.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the billing cycle (e.g., MONTHLY_01, QUARTERLY_Q1, ANNUAL_FY). Used in Zuora subscription billing engine for cycle identification.']
  cycle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the billing cycle configuration, including business rules, special handling instructions, and usage notes for billing operations team.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the billing cycle (e.g., Monthly Standard, Quarterly Advance, Annual Arrears).']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle configuration. Active cycles are available for new subscriptions; inactive cycles are closed to new assignments but continue for existing subscribers; deprecated cycles are phased out; pending cycles are configured but not yet live.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing cycle by revenue stream: subscription (SVOD/AVOD subscriber billing), advertising (ad campaign billing via Wide Orbit), carriage (MVPD/vMVPD distribution fees), syndication (content licensing fees), usage (pay-per-view/TVOD), or custom (non-standard arrangements).']
  day varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1-31) when the billing cycle starts or billing run executes. For monthly cycles, this is the anchor date. Null for non-monthly frequencies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this billing cycle configuration is retired and no longer available for new assignments. Null for currently active cycles with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this billing cycle configuration becomes active and available for assignment to billing accounts or product plans.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Recurrence interval for invoice generation: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, semiannual, annual, or custom. Determines how often billing runs execute in Zuora. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|semiannual|annual|custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA where revenue from this billing cycle is posted. Used for financial reconciliation and reporting.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after payment due date before late fees or service suspension actions are triggered. Provides customer buffer for payment processing delays.']
  invoice_generation_day varchar [note: 'Specific day of the month (1-31) when invoices are generated for this cycle. Calculated from cycle_day and billing_run_day_offset. Null for non-monthly frequencies.']
  last_billing_date date [note: 'Most recent date when a billing run was successfully completed for this cycle. Null if no billing run has occurred yet.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this billing cycle configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability under SOX compliance requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing cycle configuration record was last updated. Audit trail field for change tracking and data governance.']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'Next date when a billing run will execute for accounts assigned to this cycle. Updated after each billing run completion. Used by Zuora billing engine for scheduling.']
  payment_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice generation when payment is due. Used to calculate invoice due date. Standard terms are Net 30, Net 15, or immediate (0 days).']
  proration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partial-period charges are prorated when subscription starts/ends mid-cycle. True enables proration calculation in Zuora.']
  proration_method varchar [note: 'Method for calculating prorated charges: daily (per-day calculation), monthly (per-month calculation), or none (no proration). Used when proration_enabled is true.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before cycle renewal when customer notification is sent. Used for auto-renew reminders and cancellation windows. Null if auto_renew_enabled is false.']
  revenue_recognition_rule varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ASC 606 revenue recognition rule applied to invoices generated under this cycle. Determines how revenue is recognized over time (e.g., straight-line, milestone-based). Maps to SAP S/4HANA revenue recognition configuration.']
  tax_calculation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic tax calculation is enabled for invoices generated under this cycle. True triggers tax engine integration (e.g., Avalara, Vertex) during invoice generation.']

  Note: 'Defines the recurring billing schedule configuration for a billing account or product plan — monthly, quarterly, annual, or custom. Captures cycle name, frequency, cycle start day, billing run date, proration rules, advance vs. arrears billing flag, next bill date, and last bill date. Used by Zuora subscription billing engine to trigger invoice generation. Reference entity that governs when invoices are produced for each account.'
}

Table billing.credit_memo {
  credit_memo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the credit memo. Primary key for the credit memo entity.']
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.acquisition_deal. Business justification: Credits issued for content delivery failures, quality issues, rights clearance problems, or deal renegotiations tied to specific acquisition deals. Essential for dispute resolution and minimum guarant']
  acquisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.acquisition. Business justification: Credit memos issued against acquisition overpayments (e.g., content not delivered, quality failure, renegotiated minimum guarantee) reference the acquisition record. This link enables acquisition cost']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_order. Business justification: Makegoods, underdelivery credits, and preemption adjustments must trace to originating ad_order for accurate credit application, revenue reversal, and advertiser account reconciliation in advertising ']
  advertising_makegood_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.makegood. Business justification: Makegood credits must reference the makegood record for audit trail, revenue reversal tracking, and reconciliation of makegood obligations with actual delivery in advertising billing.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Billing account or subscription account to which the credit is applied.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advertising campaign for which a makegood credit is being issued due to under-delivery. Null for non-advertising credits.']
  company_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Credit memos are issued by a legal entity, required for AR adjustment tracking, tax credit documentation (sales tax reversals), and entity-level financial statement accuracy. Essential for makegood tr']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Credit memos for content licensing overpayments, cancelled title licenses, or rate adjustments reference the specific title. Media broadcasters routinely issue title-level credit memos; this link enab']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Credit memos need cost center attribution for variance analysis and departmental impact tracking. Enables media orgs to track revenue adjustments and makegoods against responsible cost centers for acc']
  distribution_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.distribution_agreement. Business justification: Credits for service level breaches, signal outages, subscriber count disputes, or carriage fee adjustments under distribution agreements. SLA enforcement and MVPD reconciliation require agreement-leve']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Credit memo can reference ONE specific invoice line item (not just the entire invoice). Credits are often issued for specific line items (e.g., one ad spot that didn\'t air, one subscription month). Ne']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Credit memos in media broadcasting are issued when license agreements are amended, terminated early, or content is pulled from a window. The billing adjustment process requires linking credit memos to']
  localization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.localization. Business justification: Credit memos for localization billing errors (e.g., QC failure, rework required, overcharged hours) reference the localization job. Localization vendors issue credits against specific jobs; this link ']
  original_ad_spot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.ad_spot. Business justification: Spot-level credits for technical failures, preemptions, or affidavit discrepancies require direct ad_spot reference for precise credit calculation and makegood tracking in broadcast advertising operat']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice being credited, adjusted, or written off. Null for credits not tied to a specific invoice.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Credits issued for partner disputes (makegood spots for under-delivery, SLA breach penalties, content delivery failures) require partner attribution for dispute resolution workflows, relationship heal']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Credit memos must be attributed to profit centers for accurate segment P&L and revenue adjustments. Critical for media broadcasters tracking makegoods and revenue corrections by channel or platform fo']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Credit memos for compliance-related adjustments (political ad makegood spots, license fee corrections, regulatory penalty credits) require audit trail to the originating regulatory filing for financia']
  sales_agency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_agency. Business justification: Credit memos for agency commission adjustments, agency-negotiated makegood credits, or agency billing errors require direct agency reference for commission reconciliation, agency financial reporting, ']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer, advertiser, subscriber, or carriage partner receiving the credit.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription. Business justification: Subscription-level credit memo reconciliation: credit memos for proration, service outages, or goodwill adjustments must reference the subscription for MRR impact reporting and deferred revenue adjust']
  syndication_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.syndication_agreement. Business justification: Credits for clearance failures, preemption issues, barter spot shortfalls, or syndication fee adjustments. Operational necessity for syndication partner reconciliation and revenue true-ups per agreeme']
  applied_date date [note: 'Date the credit was actually applied to the customer account or invoice balance. Null if not yet applied.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was approved by authorized personnel. Required for credits above threshold amounts.']
  bad_debt_write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credit memo represents a bad debt write-off for an uncollectable balance. True for write-offs, false for other credit types.']
  collection_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party collection agency to which the debt was referred, if applicable.']
  collection_agency_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the original debt was referred to a collection agency before write-off. Used for tracking recovery potential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was first created in the billing system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the credit being issued, always positive. Represents the amount to be deducted from customer balance or written off.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the credit memo, used in customer communications and financial reporting. Typically follows format CM-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  credit_memo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit memo in the approval and application workflow.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the credit or write-off is posted in SAP S/4HANA for financial reporting.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was formally issued and became effective for accounting purposes.']
  makegood_spots_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of compensatory advertising spots to be provided as makegood for under-delivered campaigns. Null for non-makegood credits.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit memo record was last modified, for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net credit amount after tax adjustments. Equals credit_amount minus tax_amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to the credit memo processing and approval.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason category for issuing the credit memo. Makegood refers to compensatory ad spots for under-delivered campaigns; billing_error for invoice corrections; service_outage for subscriber service credits; goodwill for customer retention; bad_debt_write_off for uncollectable balances.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the credit memo was issued, including specific circumstances, campaign details, or error descriptions.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount subsequently recovered from a written-off debt through collection efforts or customer payment. Null if no recovery occurred.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously written-off amount was recovered. Null if no recovery occurred.']
  revenue_recognition_impact varchar [note: 'Accounting period impact classification for revenue recognition under ASC 606. Indicates whether the credit affects current, prior, or future period revenue.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial document number for the credit memo posting, used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was posted to SAP S/4HANA general ledger for financial reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax portion of the credit amount, if applicable. Used for tax reporting and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Formal credit or write-off document issued to reduce, reverse, or write off a previously billed amount — covering makegoods for under-delivered ad campaigns, billing error corrections, subscriber service credits, carriage fee adjustments, and bad debt write-offs for uncollectable balances. Captures credit memo number, reason code (makegood, billing error, service outage, goodwill, bad_debt_write_off), original invoice reference, credit amount, currency, approval status, applied date, collection agency referral flag, recovery amount if subsequently collected, and SAP credit/bad-debt posting reference. SSOT for all billing credits, adjustments, and write-offs.'
}

Table billing.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the billing dispute record. Primary key.']
  acquisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.acquisition. Business justification: Disputes arising from acquisition payment terms, delivery failures, or minimum guarantee calculations reference the acquisition record. Acquisition disputes are common in content licensing; this link ']
  ad_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.ad_order. Business justification: Disputes frequently reference specific ad orders for underdelivery claims, preemption disputes, rate discrepancies, and affidavit verification. Direct ad_order link enables dispute resolution workflow']
  advertiser_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.advertiser. Business justification: Advertiser disputes require direct advertiser context for resolution workflow, escalation routing, credit hold decisions, and advertiser relationship management in media sales operations.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: A billing dispute is raised by a billing account (subscriber household, advertiser, MVPD, or licensee). While billing_dispute links to invoice_id (which in turn links to billing_account), a direct bil']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advertising.campaign. Business justification: Advertising disputes often center on campaign-level delivery metrics (GRP, reach, frequency). Direct campaign link enables dispute resolution teams to access performance data and audience guarantees f']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Billing disputes in media broadcasting are frequently about specific titles (wrong license rate, wrong territory billed, wrong window applied). Linking disputes to titles enables title-level dispute r']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo document issued to resolve the dispute, if applicable.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice being disputed. Links to the invoice that triggered this dispute.']
  invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Disputes in media billing are frequently raised at the line-item level — e.g., a subscriber disputing a specific subscription charge, or an advertiser contesting a specific spot delivery charge on an ']
  license_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.license_agreement. Business justification: Billing disputes in media broadcasting frequently arise from disagreements over license agreement terms, territory scope, or fee calculations. The dispute resolution workflow requires linking disputes']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the entity (customer, advertiser, MVPD, licensee) who raised the dispute. Polymorphic reference resolved via disputing_party_type.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: A billing dispute is frequently raised against a specific payment transaction — e.g., a chargeback on a credit card payment or a disputed ACH debit. billing_dispute currently links to invoice_id but h']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Disputes over regulatory fees (license renewals, filing fees), political advertising lowest unit charge rates, or equal opportunities claims must reference the underlying regulatory filing for resolut']
  royalty_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to rights.royalty_statement. Business justification: Rights holders dispute royalty statements, and those disputes flow into the billing dispute management process. The royalty dispute resolution workflow in media broadcasting requires linking billing d']
  series_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.series. Business justification: Series-level licensing disputes (e.g., dispute over series license fee, episode count discrepancy) reference the series. Broadcasters and distributors dispute series-level deals; this link enables ser']
  windowing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.windowing_plan. Business justification: Disputes about window-based billing (wrong window applied, territory overlap, holdback violation billing) reference the windowing plan. Distribution window disputes are frequent in media; this link en']
  assigned_resolver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or team assigned to resolve the dispute, for operational tracking.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for the disputing party, used for dispute correspondence.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for the disputing party, used for dispute resolution communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the data system, for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual monetary credit amount issued to the disputing party as resolution, in the invoice currency.']
  credit_memo_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit memo was issued as part of the dispute resolution (True) or not (False).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the disputed amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the dispute as provided by the disputing party, capturing the specific issue and context.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute: open (newly raised), under_review (being investigated), resolved (closed with outcome), escalated (sent to higher authority), withdrawn (retracted by disputing party), rejected (dispute denied without resolution).']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dispute reason: amount_error (billing calculation error), service_not_received (content or service not delivered), makegood_claim (compensatory ad spot request), duplicate_charge (same charge billed twice), rate_discrepancy (incorrect pricing applied), contract_dispute (terms interpretation disagreement).']
  disputed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary amount being contested by the disputing party, in the invoice currency.']
  disputing_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or individual who raised the dispute, for reporting and communication purposes.']
  disputing_party_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity raising the dispute: subscriber (end consumer), advertiser (ad campaign buyer), MVPD (Multichannel Video Programming Distributor), licensee (content license holder), distributor (content distribution partner).']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times the dispute has been escalated (0 = no escalation, 1 = first escalation to supervisor, 2 = second escalation to manager, etc.).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the dispute was escalated beyond the initial resolver, capturing complexity or policy exception requirements.']
  external_case_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from an external system (e.g., CRM case ID, legal case number) if the dispute is tracked in multiple systems.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who last updated the dispute record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dispute record, for audit trail and change tracking.']
  open_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was formally raised and entered into the dispute management system.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute was created in the system, used for SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the dispute based on amount, customer tier, or contractual importance: low (routine), medium (standard), high (significant customer or amount), critical (executive escalation or legal risk).']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique reference number for the dispute, used in customer communications and tracking. Format: DSP-YYYYMMDD sequence.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date the dispute was formally closed with a final outcome.']
  resolution_deadline_date date [note: 'Target date by which the dispute must be resolved per Service Level Agreement (SLA) or contractual terms.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes documenting the investigation findings, resolution rationale, and actions taken by the resolver.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the dispute resolution: full_credit (entire disputed amount credited), partial_credit (portion credited), no_adjustment (dispute rejected, no change), makegood_issued (compensatory ad spot provided), contract_amendment (terms renegotiated), escalated_unresolved (sent to legal or executive review).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the dispute was resolved, used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying cause of the dispute for process improvement and trend analysis: billing_system_error (system calculation bug), data_entry_error (manual input mistake), rate_card_error (incorrect pricing configuration), service_delivery_failure (content or ad not delivered), contract_misinterpretation (terms unclear or misread), customer_misunderstanding (customer expectation mismatch).']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute resolution exceeded the contractual SLA deadline (True) or was resolved within SLA (False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which the dispute originated: zuora (subscription billing disputes), wide_orbit (advertising billing disputes), sap_s4hana (financial disputes), salesforce (CRM-initiated disputes), manual_entry (disputes entered directly by billing operations).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the system user who created the dispute record in the billing system.']

  Note: 'Formal billing dispute record raised by a subscriber, advertiser, MVPD, or licensee contesting an invoice or charge. Captures dispute reference number, dispute type (amount error, service not received, makegood claim, duplicate charge, rate discrepancy), disputed amount, dispute open date, resolution deadline, assigned resolver, resolution status (open, under review, resolved, escalated), resolution outcome, credit memo issued flag, and root cause category. Drives the dispute management workflow.'
}

Table billing.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the refund record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing account receiving the refund.']
  company_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Refunds are disbursed by a specific legal entity (bank account holder), essential for cash flow tracking, intercompany settlement, and audit trail. Streaming/subscription businesses need entity-level ']
  content_title_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to content.title. Business justification: Refunds for content licensing overpayments, cancelled title licenses, or subscriber refunds for unavailable content reference the specific title. Title-level refund tracking is required for revenue ad']
  credit_memo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.credit_memo. Business justification: Refund can be triggered by ONE credit memo. Credit memos often result in refunds when the credit amount exceeds the outstanding balance. Need direct FK from refund to credit_memo (not just via billing']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing dispute record that triggered this refund, if the refund is the result of a resolved dispute.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this refund is associated with, if applicable.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_partner. Business justification: Refunds issued to partners for overpayments, duplicate payments, or refunds received from partners for content returns or deal cancellations. Cash reconciliation and partner financial statement accura']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payment transaction that is being refunded.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_method. Business justification: Refund is issued to ONE payment method (refund destination). Currently stores payment_method_last_four as STRING; need FK to the stored payment_method record. Remove payment_method_last_four as it can']
  subscriber_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subscriber who is receiving the refund.']
  subscription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subscriber.subscription. Business justification: Subscription cancellation refund processing: refunds issued on subscription cancellations must reference the specific subscription for chargeback management, refund eligibility validation, and subscri']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount being refunded to the subscriber or account, before any adjustments or fees.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved for processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the refund was initiated (web portal, mobile app, call center, retail location, automated system, batch process).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  expected_settlement_date date [note: 'Projected date when the refund is expected to be settled and funds returned to the subscriber. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any processing fee deducted from the refund amount, if applicable (e.g., payment gateway fees, administrative charges).']
  is_chargeback_related boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this refund is related to a payment chargeback or dispute initiated by the subscriber through their financial institution.']
  is_partial_refund boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a partial refund (true) or a full refund (false) of the original payment amount.']
  net_refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount actually returned to the subscriber or account after deducting any processing fees (refund_amount - refund_fee).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the refund transaction, used by customer service and finance teams.']
  processing_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was submitted for payment processing. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the refund (e.g., CANCEL, DISPUTE, OVERPAY, SVCFAIL, GOODWILL).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the refund was issued, providing business context beyond the reason code.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the refund is being returned to the subscriber: back to original payment instrument, check, account credit, wire transfer, or other mechanism. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original_payment_method|credit_card|bank_transfer|check|account_credit|wire_transfer|paypal|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the refund, used in customer communications and financial reconciliation. Format: RFD-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction in the processing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|processing|completed|failed|cancelled|reversed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the refund based on the revenue stream being reversed: subscription billing, advertising billing, carriage fee, syndication license fee, overpayment, or other.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was originally requested by the subscriber or initiated by the system. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a completed refund was reversed or cancelled, if applicable.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA financial document number for the refund posting in the general ledger, used for financial reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sap_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was posted to the SAP S/4HANA general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date when the refund funds were actually settled and returned to the subscriber or account. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  wideorbit_refund_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Wide Orbit traffic and billing system for refunds originating from advertising revenue, used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  zuora_refund_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier from Zuora subscription billing system for refunds originating from subscription revenue, used for system integration and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Record of a monetary refund issued back to a subscriber or billing account following an overpayment, cancellation, or resolved dispute. Captures refund amount, currency, refund method (original payment method, check, account credit), refund reason, originating payment reference, approval status, processing date, settlement date, and SAP financial posting reference. Distinct from credit memos (which reduce invoice balances) — refunds represent actual cash outflows.'
}

  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for any modification to the original recognition schedule (e.g., \'Contract amendment - extended term by 6 months\', \'Makegood issued for underdelivered GRPs\', \'Early termination - subscriber cancellation\', \'Price adjustment - volume discount applied\'). Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state for the revenue recognition schedule. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties. Pending Approval (awaiting finance review), Approved (authorized for recognition posting), Rejected (requires correction or additional documentation).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule was approved for posting. Part of the SOX audit trail for revenue recognition controls.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'High-level business division or operating segment responsible for the revenue (e.g., Streaming Services, Linear Broadcasting, Content Licensing, Advertising Sales). Used for executive reporting and segment disclosure.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue-generating contract. Determines recognition method and accounting treatment. Subscription (SVOD recurring), Syndication License (content resale), Upfront Ad Deal (advance advertising commitment), Carriage Agreement (MVPD distribution fee), Transactional VOD (TVOD pay-per-view), Sponsorship (branded content deal).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue recognized to date from contract inception through the current reporting period. Increases each period as revenue is earned. Used for deferred revenue balance calculation and revenue assurance reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value and recognized amounts (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CAD). Used for multi-currency revenue reporting and consolidation.']
  current_period_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount recognized in the current accounting period (month or quarter). Posted to the SAP revenue account. May differ from monthly recognized amount for usage-based or milestone-based contracts.']
  deferred_revenue_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unearned revenue balance on the contract. Calculated as total contract value minus cumulative recognized amount. Represents the liability on the balance sheet under ASC 606. Decreases as revenue is recognized over time.']
  last_recognition_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revenue recognition posting. Used to track recognition progress and identify schedules requiring current-period processing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition schedule record was last updated. Tracks changes to recognition amounts, dates, or status for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  monthly_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard monthly revenue amount to be recognized under straight-line method. Calculated as total contract value divided by recognition period months. May vary for usage-based or milestone-based contracts.']
  next_recognition_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next revenue recognition posting. Used by automated revenue recognition jobs to process monthly or quarterly recognition batches.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or audit notes related to the revenue recognition schedule (e.g., \'Upfront deal with makegood provision\', \'Syndication license with holdback period\', \'Early termination - prorated final month\').']
  performance_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the performance obligation under ASC 606 (e.g., \'Provide streaming access to content library for 12 months\', \'Deliver 52 episodes of syndicated series\', \'Deliver 1000 GRPs in Q4 upfront commitment\'). Documents the basis for revenue recognition timing.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition is scheduled to complete. Corresponds to the contract expiration date or end of the performance obligation period. Null for evergreen contracts until termination notice.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition method applied to this contract. Straight-Line (equal monthly recognition for subscriptions), Milestone-Based (triggered by delivery events for syndication), Usage-Based (actual consumption for AVOD/TVOD), Proportional Performance (percentage-of-completion for production contracts), Point-in-Time (immediate recognition for transactional sales).']
  recognition_period_months varchar [note: 'Total number of months over which revenue will be recognized. Calculated from recognition start date to end date. Used for straight-line recognition calculations and forecasting.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition begins. Corresponds to the contract effective date or service commencement date under ASC 606. Marks the beginning of the performance obligation period.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revenue recognition schedule. Scheduled (future start date), In Progress (actively recognizing), Completed (fully recognized), Suspended (temporarily paused), Terminated (contract cancelled early), Adjusted (modified due to contract amendment or makegood).']
  sap_deferred_revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) liability account code in SAP S/4HANA where deferred revenue balance is maintained. Decreases as revenue is recognized and transferred to the revenue account.']
  sap_revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA where recognized revenue is posted. Maps to the appropriate revenue account based on contract type and business unit (e.g., subscription revenue, advertising revenue, licensing revenue).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record that originated the contract and revenue schedule. Zuora (subscription billing), Wide Orbit (ad sales and trafficking), Rightsline (rights and royalties), Salesforce (ad sales CRM), SAP (manual GL entries), Manual (finance team adjustments).']
  source_system_contract_reference varchar [note: 'Native contract identifier in the source system (e.g., Zuora subscription ID, Wide Orbit order number, Rightsline license agreement ID). Used for data lineage and reconciliation back to operational systems.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract to be recognized over the performance period. Represents the transaction price under ASC 606. Denominated in the contract currency.']

  Note: 'ASC 606 revenue recognition schedule defining how and when deferred revenue from multi-period contracts (subscriptions, syndication licenses, upfront ad deals) is recognized into earned revenue. Captures contract reference, total contract value, recognition start date, recognition end date, recognition method (straight-line, milestone, usage-based), monthly recognized amount, cumulative recognized amount, deferred balance, and SAP revenue account posting reference. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA revenue accounting module.'
}

Business justification: Partners (distributors, affiliates, content licensors, co-producers) must meet specific regulatory obligations (content standards, closed captioning, political advertising rules, children\'s programmin']

Examples include scripted drama, unscripted reality, sports, news, children\'s programming, documentary, or multi-genre. Supports content acquisition strategy and partner matching.']

  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the partner headquarters is located. Format varies by country.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partnership includes exclusivity provisions that restrict either party from engaging with competing partners in specified territories, content categories, or distribution channels. Critical for rights management and competitive strategy.']

  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special considerations, relationship history, or operational notes about the partner that do not fit structured fields. Used by relationship managers for institutional knowledge capture.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the partner onboarding workflow. Tracks progression from initial prospect through due diligence, contract negotiation, legal review, approval, and final onboarding completion.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the partner used in operational systems, contracts, and inter-company communications. Typically assigned during onboarding.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the partner organization as registered with governing authorities. Used for contracts, invoicing, and legal documentation.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the partner based on their role in the media ecosystem. Studios produce original content, syndicators distribute content to multiple outlets, content providers supply licensed material, MVPDs are traditional cable/satellite distributors, vMVPDs are virtual distributors, and OTT aggregators operate streaming platforms.']
  preferred_payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the partner for content licensing, royalties, or service fees. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, advance against royalties, or milestone-based payments. Feeds into accounts payable and cash flow planning.']

  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the partner organization responsible for day-to-day relationship management and operational coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the main business contact at the partner organization. Includes country code and extension where applicable.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this partner was formally terminated or is scheduled to end. Null for active ongoing relationships. Used for historical analysis and transition planning.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this partner was formally established, typically corresponding to the first executed agreement or onboarding completion. Used for relationship tenure analysis and anniversary tracking.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the business relationship. Active indicates ongoing business, pending indicates onboarding in progress, suspended indicates temporary hold, inactive indicates no current agreements, and terminated indicates relationship ended.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Internal risk classification based on financial stability, contract compliance history, operational reliability, and strategic importance. Used for relationship management prioritization and escalation protocols.']
  strategic_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner\'s strategic importance to the business. Tier 1 represents critical strategic partners with significant content volume or distribution reach, Tier 2 represents important but not critical partners, Tier 3 represents transactional relationships, and emerging represents new partners under evaluation.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional granularity within the partner type. Examples include major studio vs independent studio, first-run syndicator vs off-network syndicator, FAST channel operator, co-production house, or affiliate network.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the partner entity. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in other regions). Used for tax reporting and compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for all external business partners including studios, syndicators, content providers, MVPDs, vMVPDs, FAST channel operators, and third-party distribution partners. Serves as the SSOT for partner identity, classification, legal entity details, and relationship status. Tracks partner type (studio, syndicator, MVPD, OTT aggregator, co-production house), corporate hierarchy, domicile jurisdiction, and onboarding lifecycle stage.'
}

  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who last modified this partner contact record.']

  created_by varchar [note: 'The user identifier or name of the person who created this partner contact record in the system.']

  Note: 'Individual contacts associated with a partner organization — executives, legal representatives, business development leads, technical liaisons, and finance contacts. Tracks name, title, role type, communication preferences, and relationship owner within Media Broadcasting. Supports negotiation workflows and partner relationship management.'

Table partner.acquisition_deal {
  acquisition_deal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the content acquisition deal. Primary key for the acquisition deal entity.']
